title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks detects linkage disequilibrium (LD) blocks genome-wide SNP data extends pipeline haplotype reconstruction, diversity analysis, genomic prediction feature engineering. Two LD metrics available: Standard r² – fast, kinship correction, suitable 10 M+ markers unstructured populations. Kinship-adjusted rV² – corrects relatedness-induced LD inflation, livestock, inbred lines, family-based cohorts. See LD Metrics vignette statistical detail decision table. C++/Armadillo computational core makes algorithm approximately 40x faster original Big-LD implementation typical window sizes. vignette covers complete pipeline – reading genotype data, block detection, haplotype analysis, parameter tuning – using ldx_geno example dataset (120 individuals, 230 SNPs, 3 chromosomes, 9 simulated LD blocks).

what: 2. Why LDxBlocks? Relationship to the original Big-LD
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks built clique-based segmentation algorithm Kim et al. (2018). mathematical core – CLQD bin assignment, maximum-weight independent set block construction, Bron-Kerbosch clique enumeration – preserved exactly. LDxBlocks extends foundation address three limitations original implementation.

previous_headings: 2. Why LDxBlocks? Relationship to the original Big-LD
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: original Big_LD() calls cor() inside boundary-scan loop per CLQD() call, matrix operations running R interpreter. WGS rice panel 3 million SNPs across 12 chromosomes, original implementation take days workstation. LDxBlocks replaces critical paths eight compiled C++ functions:
code: compute_r2_cpp(geno, digits = -1L, n_threads = 8L)   # ~40x faster maf_filter_cpp(geno, maf_cut = 0.05)                  # ~10x faster boundary_scan_cpp(geno, start, end, half_w, threshold) # ~20x faster build_hap_strings_cpp(blk_int, na_char)               # ~20-50x faster

previous_headings: 2. Why LDxBlocks? Relationship to the original Big-LD
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks enforces strict never-full-genome memory model. six supported formats stream genotypes one chromosome window time:
code: be     <- read_geno("wgs_panel.vcf.gz") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", n_threads = 8L) # Peak RAM ~ n_samples x subSegmSize x 8 bytes = 60 MB for n=5000, w=1500

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: original Big-LD stops block table. LDxBlocks provides complete downstream pipeline:
code: haps <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps) qtl  <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_results, blocks, be$snp_info) feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, top_n = 5, encoding = "additive_012")$matrix

what: 2.4 What is preserved from the original
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: CLQD(): bin vector assignment via maximal clique enumeration constructLDblock(): maximum-weight independent set via dynamic programming CLQmode, clstgap, split logic Block table column format – drop-compatible tools accept original Big-LD output

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: blues argument accepts pre-adjusted phenotype means. Four formats accepted:
code: # Format 1: Named numeric vector blues <- c(ind001 = 4.21, ind002 = 3.87, ind003 = 5.14)  # Format 2: Data frame, single trait blues <- read.csv("blues.csv") res   <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks,                                    blues    = blues,                                    id_col   = "Genotype",                                    blue_col = "YLD_BLUE")  # Format 3: Data frame, multiple traits res_mt <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks,                                     blues     = blues_mt,                                     id_col    = "id",                                     blue_cols = c("YLD", "DIS", "PHT"))  # Format 4: Named list (different individuals per trait) blues <- list(   YLD = c(ind001 = 4.21, ind002 = 3.87),   DIS = c(ind001 = 0.32, ind003 = 0.28) ) blues_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_blues.csv", package = "LDxBlocks") blues      <- read.csv(blues_file) head(blues, 3) #>       id     YLD     RES #> 1 ind001 -0.5175  0.6771 #> 2 ind002  0.7635  1.3764 #> 3 ind003 -1.3093 -0.9946

what: 13.3 Diplotype inference, rare-allele collapsing, and label harmonisation
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
code: haps_ref  <- extract_haplotypes(ref_geno, ldx_snp_info, blocks) haps_ref  <- collapse_haplotypes(haps_ref, min_freq = 0.05, collapse = "nearest") haps_tgt  <- extract_haplotypes(val_geno, ldx_snp_info, blocks) haps_harm <- harmonize_haplotypes(haps_tgt, haps_ref, max_hamming = 3L) attr(haps_harm, "harmonization_report") dip <- infer_block_haplotypes(haps_harm) head(dip[, c("block_id","id","diplotype","heterozygous","phase_ambiguous")])

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
code: pred <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = ldx_geno, snp_info = ldx_snp_info, blocks = blocks,   blues = blues_vec, verbose = FALSE ) ae <- decompose_block_effects(   haplotypes = haps, snp_info = ldx_snp_info,   blocks = blocks, snp_effects = pred$snp_effects[[1]] ) scores <- score_favorable_haplotypes(haps, allele_effects = ae, normalize = TRUE) head(scores[, c("id","stacking_index","n_blocks_scored","rank")], 10) top10  <- scores$id[scores$rank <= 10] inv    <- summarize_parent_haplotypes(haps, candidate_ids = top10,                                        allele_effects = ae, min_freq = 0.02) inv[inv$dosage > 0 & inv$is_rare & !is.na(inv$allele_effect) & inv$allele_effect > 0,     c("id","block_id","allele","dosage","allele_freq","allele_effect")]

what: 13.6 Haplotype association testing with simpleM correction
title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: test_block_haplotypes() uses unified Q+K mixed linear model. simpleM procedure (Gao et al. 2008, 2010, 2011) estimates effective number independent tests (Meff) eigenspectrum haplotype allele dosage correlation matrix, providing family-wise error control less conservative raw Bonferroni. estimate_diplotype_effects() now supports correction set test_block_haplotypes(). five p-value columns always present $omnibus_tests; sig_metric controls drives significant flag:
code: blues_vec <- setNames(ldx_blues$YLD, ldx_blues$id)  # EMMAX with FDR — recommended for discovery assoc_fdr <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes = haps,   blues      = blues_vec,   blocks     = blocks,   n_pcs      = 0L,   sig_metric = "p_fdr",   verbose    = FALSE ) cat("Significant (FDR <= 0.05):", sum(assoc_fdr$allele_tests$significant), "\n")  # Q+K with simpleM Šidák — recommended for family-wise error control assoc_sm <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes       = haps,   blues            = blues_vec,   blocks           = blocks,   n_pcs            = 3L,                 # Q+K model (used when optimize_pcs = FALSE)   sig_metric       = "p_simplem_sidak",  # simpleM Šidák correction   meff_scope       = "chromosome",       # recommended: separate Meff per chr   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,              # 99.5% variance threshold (Gao default)   verbose          = FALSE )  # Per-chromosome Meff assoc_sm$meff$trait$allele$chromosome  # All four p-value columns always present regardless of sig_metric # p_wald | p_fdr | p_simplem | p_simplem_sidak | Meff | alpha_simplem | ... head(assoc_sm$allele_tests[order(assoc_sm$allele_tests$p_wald),                             c("block_id","allele","effect","SE",                               "p_wald","p_simplem","p_simplem_sidak","Meff")], 5)  # Automatic PC selection — let the data choose n_pcs via BIC/lambda hybrid # Recommended when lambda_GC is far from 1.0 with a fixed n_pcs. assoc_opt <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes       = haps,   blues            = blues_vec,   blocks           = blocks,   optimize_pcs     = TRUE,              # triggers BIC/lambda model selection   optimize_pcs_max = 10L,              # test k = 0..10   optimize_method  = "bic_lambda",     # recommended: |lambda-1| + 0.01*BIC   sig_metric       = "p_simplem_sidak",   meff_scope       = "chromosome",   plot             = TRUE,             # saves PDF plots including pca_grm.pdf   out_dir          = "results",        # and grm_scree.pdf   verbose          = TRUE )  # Inspect selection table print(assoc_opt$pc_model_selection) #  n_pcs     BIC lambda_gc  score selected #      0  1234.1     1.021 0.0210    FALSE #      1  1231.5     1.015 0.0150    FALSE #      2  1230.8     1.003 0.0033    FALSE #      3  1232.1     0.999 0.0011     TRUE   <-- selected  # Multi-trait: all traits share the same GRM assoc_mt <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes = haps,   blues      = ldx_blues,   blocks     = blocks,   id_col     = "id",   blue_cols  = c("YLD", "RES"),   n_pcs      = 3L,   sig_metric = "p_simplem_sidak",   verbose    = FALSE ) dip <- estimate_diplotype_effects(   haplotypes       = haps,   blues            = blues_vec,   blocks           = blocks,   min_n_diplotype  = 3L,   sig_metric       = "p_omnibus_simplem_sidak",  # recommended   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   verbose          = FALSE )  # All five columns always present: # p_omnibus_adj | p_omnibus_fdr | p_omnibus_simplem | p_omnibus_simplem_sidak | Meff head(dip$omnibus_tests[, c("block_id","trait","F_stat","p_omnibus",                             "p_omnibus_fdr","p_omnibus_simplem_sidak",                             "Meff","significant")], 5)  # Blocks showing overdominance (heterosis candidates) dip$dominance_table[dip$dominance_table$overdominance,                     c("block_id","allele_A","allele_B","a","d","d_over_a")]

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: See section 13.5 complete example ?score_favorable_haplotypes.

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: compare_block_effects() validates whether haplotype effects one population replicate another. computes IVW meta-analytic effects, Cochran Q heterogeneity (tests whether effect sizes differ populations), ² inconsistency, direction agreement per block. Key distinction: boundary_overlap_ratio output column automatically computed block tables supply — set . boundary_overlap_warn input parameter (default 0.80) controls boundary_warning = TRUE raised output. Interpreting output:
code: # Best practice: use Pop A's block boundaries for Pop B to maximise # shared alleles and ensure haplotype strings are directly comparable. haps_B_harm <- harmonize_haplotypes(   extract_haplotypes(geno_B, snp_info, blocks_A),  # same block coordinates   reference = haps_A )  assoc_A <- test_block_haplotypes(haps_A, blues = blues_A, blocks = blocks_A,                                   sig_metric = "p_simplem_sidak") assoc_B <- test_block_haplotypes(haps_B_harm, blues = blues_B, blocks = blocks_A,                                   sig_metric = "p_simplem_sidak")  # block_match = "id" (default): match by block_id string # block_match = "position": match by genomic IoU -- use when blocks differ conc <- compare_block_effects(   assoc_A, assoc_B,   pop1_name             = "PopA",   pop2_name             = "PopB",   blocks_pop1           = blocks_A,   blocks_pop2           = blocks_B,   # Pop B own block table (may differ)   block_match           = "position", # recommended when blocks not identical   overlap_min           = 0.50,   direction_threshold   = 0.75,   boundary_overlap_warn = 0.80 )  # $concordance — one row per block: IVW effect, Q_stat, I2, replicated, ... conc$concordance[conc$concordance$replicated,                  c("block_id","n_shared_alleles","direction_agreement",                    "meta_p","Q_p","I2","replicated")]  # $shared_alleles — per-allele IVW detail head(conc$shared_alleles[, c("block_id","allele","effect_pop1","effect_pop2",                                "direction_agree","ivw_effect","ivw_SE")])  print(conc)   # summary: n blocks compared, n replicated, median I²

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: GWAS run outside LDxBlocks, use compare_gwas_effects(). accepts either output define_qtl_regions() (recommended, auditable) raw GWAS data frames plus block tables (convenience path). Note external GWAS provides one lead SNP per block (multiple haplotype alleles), effect_correlation Cochran Q always NA. Replication judged direction agreement meta_p ≤ 0.05.
code: # Path 1: pre-mapped (recommended) # define_qtl_regions() maps each marker to its LD block and identifies # the lead SNP (smallest p-value) as the block representative. qtl_A <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_A, blocks, snp_info, p_threshold = 5e-8) qtl_B <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_B, blocks, snp_info, p_threshold = 5e-8)  conc_gwas <- compare_gwas_effects(   qtl_pop1    = qtl_A,   qtl_pop2    = qtl_B,   blocks_pop1 = blocks_A,   blocks_pop2 = blocks_B,   block_match = "position",   overlap_min = 0.50,   pop1_name   = "PopA",   pop2_name   = "PopB" )  # Path 2: raw GWAS + blocks (calls define_qtl_regions internally) # When SE is absent, it is derived from BETA and P via z-score. # Column names are flexible: use beta_col, se_col, p_col arguments. conc_raw <- compare_gwas_effects(   gwas_pop1     = gwas_A,   gwas_pop2     = gwas_B,   blocks_pop1   = blocks,   blocks_pop2   = blocks,   snp_info_pop1 = snp_info,   # snp_info_pop2 reuses this when NULL (shared panel)   pop1_name     = "PopA",   pop2_name     = "PopB",   p_threshold   = 5e-8,   beta_col      = "BETA",     # change if named differently in your GWAS output   se_col        = "SE",       # NULL or absent: derived from z-score automatically   p_col         = "P" )  # Same output class as compare_block_effects() # Extra columns: lead_snp_pop1/pop2, lead_p_pop1/pop2, #                se_derived_pop1/pop2, both_pleiotropic conc_gwas$concordance[conc_gwas$concordance$replicated, ] print(conc_gwas)

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: LDxBlocks v0.3.1 adds three epistasis functions operate GRM-corrected REML residuals null model test_block_haplotypes(). Within-block SNP interaction scan (scan_block_epistasis): tests C(p,2) SNP pairs inside significant blocks interaction term aaij y = mu + aixi + ajxj + aaij(xixj) + e. Corrected Bonferroni simpleM Sidak within block; sig_metric controls drives significant flag. -block trans-haplotype scan (scan_block_by_block_epistasis): tests significant haplotype alleles every allele blocks. Identifies genetic background dependence haplotype resolution — form epistasis single-block single-SNP analyses detect. Single-block fine-mapping (fine_map_epistasis_block): identifies specific interacting SNP pair within block. Auto-dispatches exhaustive pairwise scan (p <= 200 SNPs) LASSO interaction terms (p > 200 SNPs). Statistical note. n=204 individuals Bonferroni correction ~300,000 within-block pairs, detection threshold approximately p < 1.7 × 10^-7 per block. Power detect pairwise SNP epistasis threshold low unless interaction effect large (|aa| > ~0.3 SD). -block trans-haplotype scan better powered haplotype dosage column aggregates multi-SNP variation, reducing effective number tests increasing signal--noise ratio per test.
code: # Within-block epistasis (restricted to significant_omnibus blocks) epi_within <- scan_block_epistasis(   assoc              = assoc,   geno_matrix        = res$geno_matrix,   snp_info           = snp_info,   blocks             = blocks,   blues              = blues_list,   haplotypes         = haps,   trait              = "BL",   sig_metric         = "p_simplem_sidak",   max_snps_per_block = 300L ) print(epi_within) epi_within$results[epi_within$results$significant, ]  # Between-block trans-haplotype epistasis epi_between <- scan_block_by_block_epistasis(   assoc      = assoc,   haplotypes = haps,   blues      = blues_list,   blocks     = blocks,   trait      = "BL" ) print(epi_between)  # Fine-map a single block # y_resid must be pre-computed from the REML null model fine <- fine_map_epistasis_block(   block_id    = "block_12_1054210_1086071",   geno_matrix = res$geno_matrix,   snp_info    = snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   y_resid     = my_reml_residuals,   method      = "auto" ) head(fine)

title: Introduction to LDxBlocks
text: Kim S-et al. (2018). Bioinformatics 34(4):588-596. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btx609 Gao X, Starmer J, Martin ER (2008). multiple testing correction method genetic association studies using correlated SNPs. what: 1. Why scale matters: the original Big-LD limitation
title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: original Big_LD() function requires complete genotype dataset RAM simultaneously. modern WGS panels untenable:

what: 2. How LDxBlocks solves it: never-full-genome memory model
title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: Peak RAM formula:
code: RAM_peak ~ n_samples × subSegmSize × 8 bytes          = 204 × 1500 × 8 = 2.4 MB  (3M-SNP rice panel)          = 5000 × 1500 × 8 = 60 MB  (5000-sample cattle panel)

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))   install.packages("BiocManager") BiocManager::install("SNPRelate") library(SNPRelate) SNPRelate::snpgdsVCF2GDS(   vcf.fn      = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out.fn      = "mydata.gds",   method      = "biallelic.only",   snpfirstdim = FALSE ) be_gds <- read_geno("mydata.gds")  blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be_gds,   method       = "r2",   CLQcut       = 0.70,   leng         = 100L,   subSegmSize  = 1500L,   n_threads    = 16L,   min_snps_chr = 100L,   verbose      = TRUE ) close_backend(be_gds)

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
code: blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be_gds,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",      # polynomial O(n log n); guaranteed connected   CLQcut          = 0.70,   max_bp_distance = 500000L,       # skip pairs > 500 kb   subSegmSize     = 500L,   leng            = 50L,   checkLargest    = TRUE,   n_threads       = 16L,   min_snps_chr    = 100L )

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: 500 kb, 70–90% pairs skipped, reducing O(p²) near-O(p):
code: blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be_gds,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",   max_bp_distance = 500000L,   CLQcut          = 0.70,   n_threads       = 16L )

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
code: be_gds <- read_geno("mydata.gds") be_bm  <- read_geno_bigmemory(   be_gds,   backingfile = "mydata_bm",   backingpath = "/data/ldxblocks",   type        = "char",   verbose     = TRUE ) close_backend(be_gds) blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be_bm, CLQmode = "Leiden", CLQcut = 0.70) close_backend(be_bm)

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
code: be     <- read_geno("wgs_panel.gds") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.70,                               n_threads = 16L, verbose = FALSE) haps   <- extract_haplotypes(be, be$snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 5) div    <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps) feat   <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, encoding = "additive_012",                                           scale_features = TRUE)$matrix blues  <- read.csv("blues.csv") pred   <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = as.matrix(read_chunk(be, seq_len(be$n_snps))),   snp_info    = be$snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   blues       = blues,   id_col      = "id",   blue_col    = "YLD" ) close_backend(be)

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends
text: GDS handle errors Windows. Avoid FORK-based parallelism; use n_threads > 1 (OpenMP within single process) instead. “fewer min_snps_chr SNPs” messages. Set min_snps_chr = 100 higher skip scaffold chromosomes automatically. CLQD hangs takes hours. Switch CLQmode = "Leiden" CLQcut = 0.70 max_bp_distance = 500000L. Processing stalls “Segment N/N done”. Update v0.3.1+ routes overlap resolution resolve_overlap_cpp() — 15,000× reduction per-SNP LD computation cost.

title: Large-Scale Analysis: GDS and PLINK BED Backends

what: 1. The kinship problem in LD estimation
title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: standard squared Pearson correlation two SNP dosage vectors gjg_j gkg_k : rjk2=[Cov(gj,gk)Var(gj)Var(gk)]2r^2_{jk} = \left[\frac{\mathrm{Cov}(g_j, g_k)}   {\sqrt{\mathrm{Var}(g_j)\,\mathrm{Var}(g_k)}}\right]^2 estimator assumes nn individuals drawn independently population. structured related populations fails: two individuals sharing recent common ancestor carry correlated alleles loci, just loci true LD. sample covariance picks genuine LD kinship-induced allele sharing, inflating r2r^2 causing blocks drawn broadly.

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: Mangin et al. (2012) proposed kinship-adjusted squared correlation: rVjk2=[Cor(V−1/2gj,V−1/2gk)]2rV^2_{jk} = \left[\mathrm{Cor}(V^{-1/2} g_j,\; V^{-1/2} g_k)\right]^2 V=ZZ⊤/(2∑jpjqj)V = ZZ^\top / (2\sum_j p_j q_j) VanRaden (2008) GRM V−1/2V^{-1/2} inverse square root. Left-multiplying V−1/2V^{-1/2} removes kinship-induced correlation resulting correlation measures recombination-based LD.

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: r² versus rV²: decision table

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: V=ZZ⊤2∑jpjqjV = \frac{ZZ^\top}{2\sum_j p_j q_j} zij=gij−2pjz_{ij} = g_{ij} - 2p_j frequency-centred genotype.

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: get_V_inv_sqrt() computes =V−1/2A = V^{-1/2} AVA⊤=IA V ^\top = . Cholesky (kin_method = "chol", default): =R−1A = R^{-1} V=R⊤RV = R^\top R. Eigendecomposition (kin_method = "eigen"): =QΛ−1/2Q⊤= Q \Lambda^{-1/2} Q^\top.
code: set.seed(1) G_small <- ldx_geno[, 1:30] Gc      <- scale(G_small, center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) V_demo  <- tcrossprod(Gc) / 30 + diag(0.05, nrow(Gc))  A_chol <- get_V_inv_sqrt(V_demo, method = "chol") A_eig  <- get_V_inv_sqrt(V_demo, method = "eigen")  max(abs(A_chol %*% V_demo %*% t(A_chol) - diag(120))) #> [1] 80.21798 max(abs(A_eig  %*% V_demo %*% t(A_eig)  - diag(120))) #> [1] 4.574119e-14

what: 5. Comparing r² and rV² on example data
title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: r² (left) vs rV² (right) first 25 SNPs chr1
code: idx_25  <- 1:25 r2_mat  <- compute_r2(ldx_geno[, idx_25])  Gc_25   <- scale(ldx_geno[, idx_25], center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) V_toy   <- tcrossprod(Gc_25) / 25 + diag(0.1, 120) A_toy   <- get_V_inv_sqrt(V_toy) X_whit  <- A_toy %*% Gc_25 rv2_mat <- compute_rV2(X_whit)  cat("Mean r² :", round(mean(r2_mat[upper.tri(r2_mat)]),  4), "\n") #> Mean r² : 0.3302 cat("Mean rV²:", round(mean(rv2_mat[upper.tri(rv2_mat)]), 4), "\n") #> Mean rV²: 0.3388 par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1)) image(r2_mat,  main = "Standard r²",       col = hcl.colors(20, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE),       xlab = "SNP", ylab = "SNP") image(rv2_mat, main = "Kinship-adjusted rV²",       col = hcl.colors(20, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE),       xlab = "SNP", ylab = "SNP") par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
text: livestock panel sires appear repeatedly progeny, kinship-induced correlation half-sibs inflates r2r^2 SNP pairs family cluster. pair SNPs different chromosomes can show r2>0.5r^2 > 0.5 purely correlate family membership. left-multiplication V−1/2V^{-1/2}, genuine gametic LD contributes rV2rV^2. practice, rV² blocks 10–30% smaller precisely delimited related populations.

title: LD Metrics: Standard r² and Kinship-Adjusted rV²
code: blocks_r2  <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2",  CLQcut = 0.70) blocks_rv2 <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "rV2", CLQcut = 0.70,                                   kin_method = "chol")

code: decay <- compute_ld_decay(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info,                            r2_threshold = "both", n_pairs = 2000L, verbose = FALSE) cat("Parametric threshold:", round(decay$critical_r2_param, 4), "\n")

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: extract_haplotypes() default mode produces diploid allele strings — concatenation individual’s allele codes (0, 1, 2) every SNP block. Within high-LD block strings nearly 1:1 true haplotype classes (Calus et al. 2008) sufficient diversity analyses genomic prediction tasks. However, several analyses require knowledge alleles reside chromosome (gametic phase): True gametic haplotype dosage: counting many copies haplotype allele individual carries separate chromosomes (0, 1, 2) rather whether combined diploid string matches pattern. Inbreeding estimation: fine-scale runs homozygosity require gametic phasing. Heterosis modelling: identifying heterozygous diplotypes unambiguously. LDxBlocks provides true statistical phasing via Beagle 5.x integration run_ldx_pipeline(phase = TRUE).

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: Download Beagle JAR file https://faculty.washington.edu/browning/beagle/beagle.html: Place beagle.jar out_dir (directory pass run_ldx_pipeline()), supply full path via beagle_jar.
code: wget https://faculty.washington.edu/browning/beagle/beagle.22Jul22.46e.jar -O beagle.jar

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: Java 8 later required. Verify installation: java system PATH, supply full path via java_path argument.
code: java -version # openjdk version "11.0.21" ...

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: Beagle requires VCF VCF.gz input index file (.tbi). geno_source argument run_ldx_pipeline() must therefore point VCF VCF.gz file — GDS, BED, numeric CSV formats accepted phase = TRUE.
code: # Check: this will error early with a clear message if geno_source is not VCF run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source = "mydata.bed",   # ERROR: phase = TRUE requires VCF input   phase       = TRUE,   ... )

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: Calling run_ldx_pipeline(phase = TRUE) triggers following sequence, transparently within pipeline: LD block detection runs unphased dosage matrix (correct LD computation — phasing change allele correlations). Beagle phasing called original VCF via phase_with_beagle(). phased VCF written out_prefix.vcf.gz out_dir. Phased VCF reading via read_phased_vcf() parses 0|1 GT fields three matrices: hap1 (gamete 1), hap2 (gamete 2), dosage =    hap1 + hap2. Sample SNP alignment aligns phased data cleaned/imputed marker set using composite key (CHR + POS + REF + ALT + SNP ID). robust VCF--VCF minor header differences. Bigmemory caching (use_bigmemory = TRUE): hap1, hap2, dosage written three binary-backed matrices bigmemory_path. next run bigmemory_path unchanged phased VCF, pipeline reattaches disk instantly without re-reading VCF re-running Beagle. Phased haplotype extraction passes list(hap1, hap2, dosage) extract_haplotypes(), produces gametic allele strings (g1|g2) per block per individual.

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
code: library(LDxBlocks)  result <- run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source        = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out_dir            = "ldx_results",   out_blocks         = "ldx_results/blocks.csv",   out_diversity      = "ldx_results/diversity.csv",   out_hap_matrix     = "ldx_results/hap_matrix.csv",    # Phasing   phase              = TRUE,   beagle_jar         = "ldx_results/beagle.jar",  # or NULL to auto-find   beagle_threads     = 8L,   beagle_java_mem_gb = 16L,    # -Xmx16g; NULL = JVM default (~256 MB — too small for WGS)   beagle_seed        = 42L,    # integer seed for reproducibility    # Block detection   CLQcut         = 0.70,   CLQmode        = "Leiden",   max_bp_distance = 500000L,   subSegmSize    = 500L,   leng           = 50L,   n_threads      = 8L,    # Haplotype extraction   min_snps_block = 3L,   top_n          = 5L,    # Bigmemory caching (fast restart)   use_bigmemory  = TRUE,   bigmemory_path = "ldx_results/bm_cache",    verbose = TRUE )  result$phase_method   # "beagle" result$phased_vcf     # "ldx_results/mydata_phased.vcf.gz"

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
code: # Phase method result$phase_method # [1] "beagle"  # Path to phased VCF result$phased_vcf # [1] "ldx_results/mydata_phased.vcf.gz"  # Haplotype strings now contain "|" separating gamete 1 and gamete 2 result$haplotypes[[1]][1:4] # [1] "0010|1010"  "1100|0100"  "0000|0110"  "1010|0000"  # Diversity table: phased = TRUE head(result$diversity[, c("block_id","He","phased")])

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: WGS datasets, reading phased VCF 3M SNPs 204 individuals R read_phased_vcf() parser takes several minutes. bigmemory caching mechanism eliminates cost runs first.

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: use_bigmemory = TRUE phase = TRUE, reading phased VCF, three binary-backed matrices written bigmemory_path: accompanied .desc descriptor files, _snpinfo.rds, _sampleids.rds, _params.rds (fingerprint). Fingerprint: VCF path + mtime + target SNP count + sample count. cache files exist fingerprint matches, VCF re-read. Disk space: 204 individuals × 2.96M SNPs 1 byte per cell, matrix requires ~600 MB. Total three matrices: ~1.8 GB.

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: invalidate cache (e.g., re-running Beagle different parameters), delete ldxblocks_bm_phased_* files bigmemory_path:
code: # From R files <- list.files("ldx_results/bm_cache", pattern = "ldxblocks_bm_phased",                     full.names = TRUE) file.remove(files)  # Then rerun — will re-read the phased VCF and rebuild the cache result <- run_ldx_pipeline(..., phase = TRUE, use_bigmemory = TRUE, ...)

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: need finer control phasing step (e.g., chromosome--chromosome phasing, phasing reference panel), can call phase_with_beagle() directly: Beagle log written ldx_results/mydata_phased.log.
code: phased_vcf <- phase_with_beagle(   input_vcf   = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out_prefix  = "ldx_results/mydata_phased",   beagle_jar  = "ldx_results/beagle.jar",   java_path   = "java",   java_mem_gb = 16L,            # -Xmx16g JVM heap   nthreads    = 8L,   ref_panel   = NULL,           # phased reference VCF (optional)   map_file    = NULL,           # genetic map for improved accuracy (optional)   chrom       = "1",            # restrict to chromosome 1 (optional)   seed        = 42L,   burnin      = 6L,             # Beagle default: 6   iterations  = 12L,            # Beagle default: 12   window      = 25.0,           # window size in cM (Beagle default: 25)   overlap     = 2.5,            # window overlap in cM (Beagle default: 2.5)   verbose     = TRUE )  phased_vcf # [1] "ldx_results/mydata_phased.vcf.gz"

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: phased reference VCF substantially improves phasing accuracy, especially rare variants. Reference panels available 1000 Genomes Project, HapMap, species-specific databases:
code: phased_vcf <- phase_with_beagle(   input_vcf   = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out_prefix  = "ldx_results/mydata_phased",   beagle_jar  = "ldx_results/beagle.jar",   ref_panel   = "reference_panel.vcf.gz",   # must be phased (0|1 GT fields)   java_mem_gb = 32L,   nthreads    = 8L )

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: large datasets, phasing one chromosome time reduces peak memory:
code: chrs <- as.character(1:12)  phased_vcfs <- lapply(chrs, function(chr) {   phase_with_beagle(     input_vcf   = "mydata.vcf.gz",     out_prefix  = sprintf("ldx_results/mydata_phased_chr%s", chr),     beagle_jar  = "ldx_results/beagle.jar",     chrom       = chr,     java_mem_gb = 8L,     nthreads    = 8L,     seed        = 42L   ) })

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: data already phased (Beagle, SHAPEIT, another tool), use read_phased_vcf() directly: Pass resulting list directly extract_haplotypes():
code: gp <- read_phased_vcf("mydata_phased.vcf.gz", min_maf = 0.01, verbose = TRUE)  # Returns a list: names(gp) # [1] "hap1"       "hap2"       "dosage"     "snp_info"   "sample_ids" "phased"     "phase_method"  dim(gp$hap1)     # SNPs × individuals dim(gp$hap2)     # SNPs × individuals dim(gp$dosage)   # SNPs × individuals (= hap1 + hap2)  gp$phased        # TRUE gp$phase_method  # "vcf_phased" haps <- extract_haplotypes(   geno     = gp,           # list with hap1 and hap2 elements   snp_info = gp$snp_info,   blocks   = blocks,   min_snps = 3L )  # Haplotype strings contain gametic phase haps[[1]][1:3] # [1] "0010|1010"  "1100|0100"  "0000|0110"

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: gametic phase available, build_haplotype_feature_matrix() produces true allele copy number (0, 1, 2): Compare unphased:
code: feat_phased <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(   haplotypes     = haps,          # from phased extraction   top_n          = 5L,   scale_features = TRUE )$matrix  # Each column encodes: 0 = neither gamete, 1 = heterozygous, 2 = homozygous # This is true allele copy number — not present/absent range(feat_phased)   # [0, 2] (before scaling) feat_unphased <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(   haplotypes     = haps_unphased,   # from dosage-string extraction   top_n          = 5L,   scale_features = FALSE )$matrix  range(feat_unphased)   # [0, 1] — presence/absence only

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: compute_haplotype_diversity() function automatically detects phased input sets phased column TRUE. correction uses number gametes (2N) rather individuals (N):
code: div_phased <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)  # phased column head(div_phased[, c("block_id","n_ind","He","phased")]) #         block_id n_ind      He phased # block_1_1000_103000   204  0.891   TRUE

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: test_block_haplotypes() uses phased dosage (0/1/2) phased haplotype strings passed, giving unambiguous allele copy-number encoding association model. Use sig_metric = "p_simplem_sidak" family-wise error control correlated haplotype predictors (recommended):
code: blues_vec <- setNames(my_blues$YLD, my_blues$id)  assoc_phased <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes       = haps,              # phased (contains "|")   blues            = blues_vec,   blocks           = blocks,   n_pcs            = 3L,               # Q+K: GRM PCs as fixed effects   sig_metric       = "p_simplem_sidak", # simpleM Šidák correction   meff_scope       = "chromosome",      # separate Meff per chromosome   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   verbose          = FALSE )  # Effect estimates reflect true additive copy-number dosage (0/1/2) head(assoc_phased$allele_tests[order(assoc_phased$allele_tests$p_wald),                                 c("block_id","allele","effect","SE",                                   "p_wald","p_simplem","p_simplem_sidak","Meff")])  # All four p-value columns are always present in the output # (p_wald, p_fdr, p_simplem, p_simplem_sidak) regardless of sig_metric

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks
text: phased VCF modified re-generated, fingerprint match pipeline rebuild cache. expected behaviour. want force rebuild explicitly:
code: # Delete cache files unlink(list.files("ldx_results/bm_cache",                    pattern = "ldxblocks_bm_phased", full.names = TRUE))

title: Statistical Phasing with Beagle 5.x in LDxBlocks

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: vignette walks complete LDxBlocks workflow, reading genotype file obtaining haplotype feature matrices diversity statistics. pipeline proceeds four stages: Read genotype data via read_geno() --one run_ldx_pipeline() Detect LD blocks chromosome--chromosome run_Big_LD_all_chr() Extract haplotypes compute diversity extract_haplotypes() compute_haplotype_diversity() Predict associate: build feature matrix genomic prediction run haplotype association tests examples use eval = FALSE vignette builds without requiring large input files. Adapt paths parameters dataset.

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: read_geno() auto-detects format file extension: LDxBlocks_backend object stores metadata without loading full genotype matrix: Always call close_backend() finished:
code: library(LDxBlocks)  # VCF (recommended for WGS data; auto-converted to GDS cache on first call) be_vcf  <- read_geno("mydata.vcf.gz")  # PLINK BED (requires BEDMatrix) be_bed  <- read_geno("mydata.bed")  # SNPRelate GDS (requires SNPRelate; fastest for large panels) be_gds  <- read_geno("mydata.gds")  # Numeric dosage CSV (SNP × individuals, values in {0,1,2,NA}) be_csv  <- read_geno("mydata.csv", format = "numeric")  # Plain R matrix (backward-compatible) be_mat  <- read_geno(my_matrix, snp_info = my_snp_df) be_vcf # LDxBlocks backend #   Type    : vcf #   Samples : 204 #   SNPs    : 2960965 #   Chr     : 1, 2, 3, ..., 12 close_backend(be_vcf)

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: read time, leading chr / Chr / CHR prefix stripped chromosome labels. Polyploid sub-genome labels (1A, 2D) preserved verbatim:

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: chip-density panels (< 100k SNPs per chromosome): WGS panels (2M–10M+ SNPs): related populations (livestock, inbred lines):
code: blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   geno_matrix = be,   method      = "r2",         # standard r² — no kinship correction   CLQcut      = 0.70,   CLQmode     = "Density",    # exact Bron-Kerbosch clique enumeration   subSegmSize = 1500L,   leng        = 200L,   MAFcut      = 0.05,   n_threads   = 8L,   verbose     = TRUE ) blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   geno_matrix     = be,   method          = "r2",   CLQcut          = 0.80,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",       # guaranteed connected communities, O(n log n)   max_bp_distance = 500000L,        # skip pairs > 500 kb (70-90% of pairs)   subSegmSize     = 500L,   leng            = 50L,   checkLargest    = TRUE,   n_threads       = 8L,   verbose         = TRUE ) blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   geno_matrix = be,   method      = "rV2",         # kinship-adjusted LD — requires AGHmatrix + ASRgenomics   kin_method  = "chol",        # Cholesky whitening (default); "eigen" for near-singular GRM   CLQcut      = 0.70,   n_threads   = 8L )

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: head(blocks) #   start  end start.rsID end.rsID start.bp   end.bp CHR length_bp # 1     1   18      rs001    rs018     1000   103000   1    102001  summarise_blocks(blocks) #    CHR n_blocks min_bp median_bp mean_bp max_bp total_bp_covered # 1    1     1214   2000     82000   88940 512000        108034316

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: GWAS-significant markers available, tune_LD_params() finds CLQcut value best captures markers within blocks:
code: result <- tune_LD_params(   geno_matrix    = my_geno,   snp_info       = my_snp_info,   gwas_df        = my_gwas,           # data.frame: Marker, CHR, POS   prefer_perfect = TRUE,              # zero unassigned + zero forced first   target_bp_band = c(5e4, 5e5),   parallel       = FALSE,   seed           = 42L )  result$best_params       # selected parameters result$gwas_assigned     # GWAS markers with LD_block column result$final_blocks      # block table from best_params

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: extract_haplotypes() concatenates individual’s allele codes (0, 1, 2) SNPs within block: block_info attribute contains block metadata:
code: haps <- extract_haplotypes(   geno   = be,           # LDxBlocks_backend or plain matrix   snp_info = be$snp_info,   blocks = blocks,   min_snps = 3L          # skip blocks with fewer than 3 SNPs )  # Named list; one character vector (length n_individuals) per block length(haps)      # number of haplotype blocks haps[[1]][1:5]    # first 5 individuals, first block # [1] "0120" "1100" "0210" "2100" "0021" bi <- attr(haps, "block_info") head(bi) #        block_id CHR start_bp  end_bp n_snps # block_1_1000_103000   1     1000  103000     25

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: div <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)  head(div[, c("block_id","CHR","n_snps","n_ind","n_haplotypes","He","Shannon","freq_dominant","sweep_flag")])  # Blocks under potential selective sweep div[div$sweep_flag, c("block_id","CHR","start_bp","He","freq_dominant")]

what: Haplotype feature matrix for genomic prediction
title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: feat_obj <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(   haplotypes     = haps,   top_n          = 5L,         # keep top 5 most frequent alleles per block   min_freq       = 0.01,       # discard alleles < 1% frequency   scale_features = TRUE        # standardise columns to zero mean, unit variance )  feat <- feat_obj$matrix        # individuals × (n_blocks × top_n) dim(feat)                      # e.g. 204 × 52105  # Haplotype GRM for GBLUP G_hap <- tcrossprod(feat) / ncol(feat)

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: common use case — VCF BED input → blocks → haplotypes → feature matrix — run_ldx_pipeline() wraps steps single call:
code: result <- run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source    = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out_dir        = "ldx_results",   out_blocks     = "ldx_results/blocks.csv",   out_diversity  = "ldx_results/diversity.csv",   out_hap_matrix = "ldx_results/hap_matrix.csv",   hap_format     = "numeric",    maf_cut        = 0.05,   impute         = "mean_rounded",    CLQcut         = 0.70,   method         = "r2",   CLQmode        = "Leiden",   max_bp_distance = 500000L,   subSegmSize    = 500L,   leng           = 50L,   n_threads      = 8L,    min_snps_block = 3L,   top_n          = 5L,    verbose = TRUE )  result$blocks          # LD block table result$diversity       # per-block diversity result$haplotypes      # raw haplotype strings result$geno_matrix     # cleaned + imputed genotype matrix result$phase_method    # "unphased"

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: WGS datasets, enable bigmemory caching subsequent runs reattach imputed genotype matrix disk without re-reading raw VCF:
code: result <- run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source    = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out_dir        = "ldx_results",   out_blocks     = "ldx_results/blocks.csv",   out_hap_matrix = "ldx_results/hap_matrix.csv",   CLQcut         = 0.70,   CLQmode        = "Leiden",   max_bp_distance = 500000L,   n_threads      = 8L,    use_bigmemory  = TRUE,   bigmemory_path = "ldx_results/bm_cache",   bigmemory_type = "char",     # 1 byte per cell; "double" for full precision    verbose = TRUE ) # Second run: reattaches from bm_cache instantly

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: pred <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = geno,   snp_info    = snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   blues       = setNames(my_blues_df$YLD, my_blues_df$id),   id_col      = "id",   blue_col    = "YLD" )  # GEBVs head(pred$gebv[, c("id", "YLD")])  # Most important blocks important <- pred$block_importance[pred$block_importance$important, ] head(important[order(-important$var_scaled), c("block_id","CHR","var_scaled")])

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: pred_mt <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix     = geno,   snp_info        = snp_info,   blocks          = blocks,   blues           = my_blues_df,          # wide data frame   id_col          = "id",   blue_cols       = c("YLD", "DIS", "PHT"),   importance_rule = "any"                 # important in at least one trait )  # Blocks important for all three traits all_imp <- pred_mt$block_importance[pred_mt$block_importance$important_all, ]

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: cv <- cv_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = geno,   snp_info    = snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   blues       = my_blues_vec,   k           = 5L,   n_rep       = 3L,   seed        = 42L ) cv$pa_mean     # mean PA ± SD per trait across folds

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: qtl <- define_qtl_regions(   gwas_df       = my_gwas,          # data.frame: Marker, CHR, POS, P   blocks        = blocks,   snp_info      = snp_info,   p_threshold   = 5e-8,   verbose       = TRUE )  # Pleiotropic blocks (QTL for multiple traits) qtl[qtl$n_traits > 1, ]

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: priority <- integrate_gwas_haplotypes(   qtl_regions = qtl,   pred_result = pred_mt,   diversity   = div )  # Top candidates: supported by GWAS + variance + diversity priority[priority$priority_score == 3,          c("block_id","CHR","start_bp","priority_score")]

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: export_candidate_regions(   qtl_regions = qtl,   format      = "bed",   chr_prefix  = "chr",   out_file    = "candidate_regions.bed" )

what: EMMAX model (pure GRM, recommended for continuous kinship)
title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: blues_vec <- setNames(my_blues_df$YLD, my_blues_df$id)  # EMMAX: GRM random effect only, FDR-based discovery assoc <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes = haps,   blues      = blues_vec,   blocks     = blocks,   n_pcs      = 0L,          # EMMAX: GRM random effect only   sig_metric = "p_fdr",     # Benjamini-Hochberg FDR (recommended for discovery)   verbose    = FALSE )  # Top blocks by omnibus significance head(assoc$block_tests[order(assoc$block_tests$p_omnibus), ])  # Significant per-allele associations (FDR <= 0.05) assoc$allele_tests[assoc$allele_tests$significant, ]

what: Q+K model with simpleM correction (recommended for family-wise control)
title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: Use sig_metric = "p_simplem_sidak" meff_scope = "chromosome" want family-wise error control accounts correlation among haplotype alleles (less conservative raw Bonferroni). four p-value columns always present output regardless sig_metric.
code: assoc_sm <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes       = haps,   blues            = blues_vec,   blocks           = blocks,   n_pcs            = 3L,                 # Q+K: 3 GRM-derived PCs as fixed effects   sig_metric       = "p_simplem_sidak",  # simpleM Šidák (recommended)   meff_scope       = "chromosome",       # separate Meff per chromosome (recommended)   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,              # 99.5% variance threshold (Gao default)   verbose          = FALSE )  # Per-chromosome effective test counts assoc_sm$meff$YLD$allele$chromosome  # All four p-value columns always present names(assoc_sm$allele_tests) # ... "p_wald" "p_fdr" "p_simplem" "p_simplem_sidak" "Meff" ...

what: Automatic PC model selection (optional, recommended when lambda != 1)
title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: genomic control lambda deflated (< 1, -correction) inflated (> 1, -correction), optimize_pcs = TRUE lets data choose n_pcs:
code: assoc_opt <- test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes       = haps,   blues            = blues_vec,   blocks           = blocks,   optimize_pcs     = TRUE,           # triggers automatic PC selection   optimize_pcs_max = 10L,            # test k = 0..10   optimize_method  = "bic_lambda",   # recommended: |lambda-1| + 0.01*BIC   sig_metric       = "p_simplem_sidak",   meff_scope       = "chromosome",   plot             = TRUE,           # PDF plots: Manhattan, QQ, PCA, scree   out_dir          = "my_results",   verbose          = TRUE )  # Model selection table: one row per k tested assoc_opt$pc_model_selection #  n_pcs     BIC lambda_gc   score selected #      0  1234.1     1.021  0.0210    FALSE #      1  1231.5     1.015  0.0150    FALSE #      2  1230.8     1.003  0.0033    FALSE #      3  1232.1     0.999  0.0011     TRUE  # n_pcs chosen assoc_opt$n_pcs_used

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: estimate_diplotype_effects() now supports full correction set sig_metric parameter, mirroring test_block_haplotypes():
code: dip <- estimate_diplotype_effects(   haplotypes       = haps,   blues            = blues_vec,   blocks           = blocks,   min_n_diplotype  = 3L,   sig_metric       = "p_omnibus_simplem_sidak",  # recommended   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   verbose          = FALSE )  # All five correction columns always present in $omnibus_tests: # p_omnibus_adj | p_omnibus_fdr | p_omnibus_simplem | p_omnibus_simplem_sidak | Meff head(dip$omnibus_tests[, c("block_id","trait","p_omnibus","p_omnibus_fdr",                             "p_omnibus_simplem_sidak","Meff","significant")], 5)  # Blocks showing overdominance (heterosis candidates) dip$dominance_table[dip$dominance_table$overdominance,                     c("block_id","allele_A","allele_B","a","d","d_over_a")]

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: compare_block_effects() validates whether haplotype effects discovered one population replicate another, using IVW meta-analysis, Cochran Q heterogeneity, direction agreement:
code: # Assumes assoc_popA and assoc_popB are test_block_haplotypes() results # from two independent populations, both run using the same block boundaries  conc <- compare_block_effects(   assoc_pop1            = assoc_popA,   assoc_pop2            = assoc_popB,   pop1_name             = "PopA",   pop2_name             = "PopB",   blocks_pop1           = blocks_popA,   blocks_pop2           = blocks_popB, # Pop B own block table (may differ)   block_match           = "position",  # match by genomic IoU; match_type column reports result   overlap_min           = 0.50,   # min IoU for 'position' match (else 'pop1_only')   direction_threshold   = 0.75,   boundary_overlap_warn = 0.80 )  # boundary_overlap_ratio is an OUTPUT column (automatically computed from block # tables); boundary_overlap_warn is the INPUT threshold (default 0.80) that # controls when boundary_warning = TRUE is set.  # Replicated blocks: directionally concordant AND Q_p > 0.05 conc$concordance[conc$concordance$replicated, ]  # Per-allele IVW detail head(conc$shared_alleles)  print(conc)   # summary: n compared, n replicated, median I²

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: # Get allele effects from prediction pipeline ae <- decompose_block_effects(   haplotypes  = haps,   snp_info    = snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   snp_effects = pred$snp_effects[["YLD"]] )  scores <- score_favorable_haplotypes(haps, allele_effects = ae, normalize = TRUE)  # Top 10 selection candidates head(scores[order(scores$rank), c("id","stacking_index","n_blocks_scored","rank")], 10)

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: top10 <- scores$id[scores$rank <= 10]  inv <- summarize_parent_haplotypes(   haplotypes    = haps,   candidate_ids = top10,   allele_effects = ae )  # Parents carrying rare alleles with positive effects inv[inv$dosage > 0 & inv$is_rare & inv$allele_effect > 0,     c("id","block_id","allele","dosage","allele_freq","allele_effect")]

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: ids <- rownames(geno)  cmp <- compare_haplotype_populations(   haplotypes  = haps,   group1      = ids[ids %in% wild_ids],   group2      = ids[ids %in% elite_ids],   group1_name = "wild",   group2_name = "elite" )  # Divergent blocks (FST > 0.1, chi-squared p < 0.05) cmp[cmp$divergent, c("block_id","FST","max_freq_diff","chisq_p")]

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: GWAS run external tool (GAPIT, TASSEL, FarmCPU, PLINK), use compare_gwas_effects() rather compare_block_effects().

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: # Map each population's markers to blocks first qtl_popA <- define_qtl_regions(   gwas_df     = my_gwas_A,   blocks      = blocks,   snp_info    = snp_info,   p_threshold = 5e-8 ) qtl_popB <- define_qtl_regions(   gwas_df     = my_gwas_B,   blocks      = blocks,   snp_info    = snp_info,   p_threshold = 5e-8 )  conc_gwas <- compare_gwas_effects(   qtl_pop1    = qtl_popA,   qtl_pop2    = qtl_popB,   blocks_pop1 = blocks_popA,   blocks_pop2 = blocks_popB,   block_match = "position",   overlap_min = 0.50,   pop1_name   = "PopA",   pop2_name   = "PopB" )  # Replicated: direction concordant AND meta_p <= 0.05 conc_gwas$concordance[conc_gwas$concordance$replicated, ] print(conc_gwas)

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: SE derived automatically BETA P absent. Marker accepted alias SNP.
code: conc_raw <- compare_gwas_effects(   gwas_pop1     = my_gwas_A,   gwas_pop2     = my_gwas_B,   blocks_pop1   = blocks,   blocks_pop2   = blocks,   snp_info_pop1 = snp_info,     # reused for pop2 when snp_info_pop2 = NULL   p_threshold   = 5e-8,   beta_col      = "BETA",       # adjust to match your GWAS output column names   se_col        = "SE",         # NULL or absent -> derived from z-score   p_col         = "P",   pop1_name     = "PopA",   pop2_name     = "PopB",   verbose       = FALSE )  # Extra output columns vs compare_block_effects(): #   lead_snp_pop1/pop2  — which SNP tagged the block in each population #   lead_p_pop1/pop2    — lead SNP p-values #   se_derived_pop1/pop2 — TRUE when SE was derived from z-score #   both_pleiotropic    — TRUE when block is pleiotropic in both populations head(conc_raw$concordance[, c("block_id","lead_snp_pop1","lead_snp_pop2",                                 "meta_effect","meta_p","direction_agreement",                                 "se_derived_pop1","replicated")])

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: Within-block -block epistasis functions operate GRM-corrected REML residuals null model test_block_haplotypes(), ensuring population-structure-corrected tests throughout.

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: # Scan all C(p,2) SNP pairs within significant blocks # Tests H0: aa_ij = 0  in  y = mu + ai*xi + aj*xj + aa_ij*(xi*xj) + e # Corrected by Bonferroni AND simpleM Sidak within each block epi_within <- scan_block_epistasis(   assoc              = assoc,          # test_block_haplotypes() result   geno_matrix        = geno_matrix,   snp_info           = snp_info,   blocks             = blocks,   blues              = blues,   haplotypes         = haps,   trait              = "YLD",   sig_blocks         = NULL,           # uses significant_omnibus blocks   max_snps_per_block = 300L,           # exhaustive up to 300 SNPs/block   sig_metric         = "p_simplem_sidak",   sig_threshold      = 0.05 )  # Output: class LDxBlocks_epistasis print(epi_within)  # Significant interacting pairs epi_within$results[epi_within$results$significant, ]  # Per-block summary: n_snps, n_pairs, n_significant, min_p epi_within$scan_summary  # All correction columns always present # p_wald | p_bonf | p_simplem | p_simplem_sidak | Meff # significant | significant_bonf | significant_simplem | significant_simplem_sidak

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
code: # Tests significant haplotype alleles x all other blocks # O(n_sig x n_total_alleles) tests -- tractable at WGS scale # Identifies genetic background dependence: a haplotype at block A # only functions in the presence of a specific haplotype at block B epi_between <- scan_block_by_block_epistasis(   assoc      = assoc,   haplotypes = haps,   blues      = blues,   blocks     = blocks,   trait      = "YLD",   sig_alleles = NULL,    # NULL = uses significant alleles from assoc   sig_threshold = 0.05 )  print(epi_between) epi_between$results[epi_between$results$significant, ]  # Results columns: # block_i, allele_i, block_j, allele_j, CHR_i, CHR_j, same_chr # aa_effect, SE, t_stat, p_wald, p_bonf, significant

title: LDxBlocks Workflow: From Genotype File to Haplotype Features
text: Statistical note. typical WGS sample sizes (n < 300), detection threshold within-block Bonferroni correction C(p,2) pairs stringent. -block trans-haplotype scan better powered haplotype dosage column aggregates multi-SNP variation, providing stronger signal--noise ratio per test.
code: # Fine-map specific interacting SNP pairs within one block. # Requires pre-computed REML residuals from the null model. # method = "auto": pairwise scan for p <= 200 SNPs, LASSO for larger blocks.  # Pre-compute residuals (example using the null model from test_block_haplotypes) feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, min_freq = 0.05,                                         encoding = "additive_012")$matrix G_grm <- compute_haplotype_grm(feat) common <- intersect(rownames(G_grm), names(blues)) y  <- blues[common] fit <- rrBLUP::mixed.solve(y = y, K = G_grm[common, common], method = "REML") y_resid <- y - as.numeric(fit$beta) - fit$u[common]  # Exhaustive pairwise scan for blocks with <= 200 SNPs fine <- fine_map_epistasis_block(   block_id    = "block_12_1054210_1086071",   geno_matrix = geno_matrix,   snp_info    = snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   y_resid     = y_resid,   method      = "auto"   # pairwise <= 200 SNPs, lasso otherwise ) head(fine)  # Columns (pairwise): SNP_i, SNP_j, POS_i, POS_j, dist_bp, #                     aa_effect, SE, t_stat, p_wald, p_bonf, significant # Columns (lasso):   SNP_i, SNP_j, POS_i, POS_j, dist_bp, lasso_coef, selected

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Linkage disequilibrium (LD) block detection foundational step modern genomic analyses. Knowing SNPs co-segregate unit determines GWAS results interpreted, haplotypes defined population genetics, genomic prediction models structured. Despite importance, implementations LD block detection suffer three problems limit usefulness practice. kinship problem. Classical LD estimators (r²) assume independence individuals. livestock, crop, family-based human cohorts assumption systematically violated. Cryptic relatedness inflates pairwise correlations, causing LD appear stronger blocks drawn broadly wrong places. kinship-adjusted squared correlation rV² (Mangin et al. 2012, Heredity 108:285-291) corrects whitening genotype matrix inverse square root genomic relationship matrix (GRM): Xᵛ = V⁻¹⁄² G̃ kinship-whitened, mean-centred genotype matrix; V = ZZᵀ / (2 Σⱼ pⱼqⱼ) VanRaden (2008) GRM; V⁻¹⁄² inverse square root. scale problem. original Big-LD implementation (Kim et al. 2018) contains compiled code. modern whole-genome sequencing panels 2-10 million markers inner loops prohibitively slow, loading full genotype matrix detection impossible workstations. LDxBlocks addresses C++/Armadillo computational core compiled via Rcpp, OpenMP-parallelised LD computation, unified multi-format /O layer, strict never-full-genome memory model. pipeline gap. original Big-LD stops block boundaries. LDxBlocks adds complete downstream pipeline: statistical phasing via Beagle 5.x bigmemory-backed caching, haplotype extraction, diversity metrics, genomic prediction features, association testing, breeding decision tools.
code: rV²ᵢⱼ = Cov(Xᵛᵢ, Xᵛⱼ)² / [Var(Xᵛᵢ) · Var(Xᵛⱼ)]

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: LDxBlocks complete pipeline genome-wide LD block detection related structured populations, downstream haplotype analysis genomic prediction utilities. extends Big-LD algorithm Kim et al. (2018) 15 core improvements: Dual LD metric — standard r² (default) kinship-adjusted rV² C++/Armadillo core — eleven compiled functions handle expensive operations OpenMP parallelism — outer loop compute_r2_cpp() parallelised Unified multi-format /O — numeric CSV, HapMap, VCF/VCF.gz, GDS, PLINK BED, R matrix Never-full-genome memory model — genome never held RAM format MAF filter C++ — single O(np) pass NA imputation C++ boundary scan — replaces R inner loop subsegmentation Sparse r² computation — O(p) cost large sub-segments Automatic parameter tuning — tune_LD_params() grid search Haplotype reconstruction — phase-free Beagle-phased pathways Diversity metrics — richness, , Shannon entropy, dominant haplotype frequency Prediction feature matrix — multi-locus dosage columns GBLUP/BayesB/ML Polynomial community detection — Louvain Leiden WGS panels Sparse LD computation — max_bp_distance restricts pairs physical window Memory-mapped genotype store — read_geno_bigmemory() OS-page access

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
code: library(LDxBlocks)  # ── Option A: end-to-end pipeline ──────────────────────────────────────────── result <- run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source    = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out_dir        = "ldx_results",   out_blocks     = "ldx_results/blocks.csv",   out_diversity  = "ldx_results/diversity.csv",   out_hap_matrix = "ldx_results/hap_matrix.csv",   CLQcut         = 0.70,   n_threads      = 8L,   verbose        = TRUE )  result$blocks          # data.frame of LD blocks result$diversity       # per-block diversity table result$haplotypes      # named list of haplotype strings  # ── Option B: block detection only ─────────────────────────────────────────── be <- read_geno("mydata.vcf.gz") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.70, n_threads = 8L) close_backend(be)  # ── Option C: with Beagle phasing ──────────────────────────────────────────── # Place beagle.jar in out_dir first, then: result <- run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source        = "mydata.vcf.gz",   out_dir            = "ldx_results",   out_blocks         = "ldx_results/blocks.csv",   out_hap_matrix     = "ldx_results/hap_matrix.csv",   phase              = TRUE,   beagle_jar         = "ldx_results/beagle.jar",   beagle_threads     = 8L,   beagle_java_mem_gb = 16L,   beagle_seed        = 42L,   CLQcut             = 0.70,   n_threads          = 8L )  result$phase_method   # "beagle" result$phased_vcf     # path to Beagle-phased VCF.gz

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Numeric dosage format — one row per SNP; columns SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT followed one column per sample, values {0, 1, 2, NA}. VCF — standard VCF v4.2. phased (0|1) unphased (0/1) GT fields accepted. Multi-allelic sites use first ALT allele. Missing calls (./.) become NA. Chromosome normalisation. leading prefix chr, Chr, CHR stripped chromosome labels read time. Polyploid sub-genome labels (1A, 2D) preserved verbatim.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Standard r² path: Pearson r² via column standardisation followed BLAS-level matrix multiplication compute_r2_cpp(). Kinship-adjusted rV² path: VanRaden (2008) GRM computed via AGHmatrix::Gmatrix(), bent conditioned via ASRgenomics::G.tuneup(), = R⁻¹ (Cholesky) Q Λ⁻¹⁄² Qᵀ (eigen) applied mean-centred genotypes compute_r2_cpp() kernel.

True gametic haplotypes require statistical phasing. strings produced extract_haplotypes() diploid allele strings, gametic phases. Within high-LD block strings nearly 1:1 true haplotype classes (Calus et al. 2008) sufficient diversity analysis genomic prediction. gametic phases required, set phase = TRUE run_ldx_pipeline(), calls Beagle 5.x LD block detection caches phased hap1/hap2/dosage bigmemory backends fast restart. title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: compute_haplotype_diversity() returns four metrics per block: Richness (k): number unique haplotype strings. Expected heterozygosity (Hₑ): Shannon entropy (H’): Dominant haplotype frequency (f_max): frequency common haplotype. Values near 1.0 indicate selective sweep strong founder effect.
code: Hₑ = n/(n−1) · (1 − Σᵢ pᵢ²) H' = −Σᵢ pᵢ log₂(pᵢ)

what: 10.3. Haplotype feature matrix for genomic prediction
title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: build_haplotype_feature_matrix() converts haplotype strings numeric dosage matrix. block, top_n frequent haplotypes selected individual receives: Phased data: 0 (neither gamete), 1 (one gamete — heterozygous), 2 (gametes — homozygous). True allele copy number. Unphased data: 0 (absent) 1 (present). value 2 never produced two chromosomes distinguished — individual homozygous allele one heterozygous produce different dosage strings treated distinct allele classes.
code: feat  <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, scale_features = TRUE)$matrix G_hap <- tcrossprod(feat) / ncol(feat)   # haplotype GRM

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: run_haplotype_prediction() produces: VanRaden GRM haplotype features GEBV genotyped individuals via REML-based GBLUP Per-SNP additive effects backsolved GEBV (Tong et al. 2025) Local haplotype GEBV per block per individual Block importance table ranked scaled Var(local GEBV)
code: # Single trait res <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks,                                  blues = my_blues, id_col = "id", blue_col = "YLD")  # Multiple traits res_mt <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks,                                     blues     = my_blues_df,                                     id_col    = "id",                                     blue_cols = c("YLD", "DIS"))  # Integrate GWAS evidence qtl      <- define_qtl_regions(gwas, blocks, snp_info, p_threshold = 5e-8) priority <- integrate_gwas_haplotypes(qtl, res, diversity = div) priority[priority$priority_score == 3, ]

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: cv_haplotype_prediction() estimates prediction accuracy via k-fold cross-validation. Returns LDxBlocks_cv object pa_mean (PA ± SD per trait), pa_summary (per-fold), k, n_rep.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: compare_haplotype_populations() computes per-block Weir-Cockerham FST, maximum allele frequency difference, chi-squared test (Monte Carlo, B = 2000). divergent flag: FST > 0.1 p < 0.05.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: plot_haplotype_network() draws minimum-spanning network using igraph::mst(). Nodes sized frequency; edges weighted Hamming distance. Optional group colouring.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: run_haplotype_stability() runs Finlay-Wilkinson (1963) regression per-block local GEBV contributions environmental index. Returns b (stability coefficient), b_se, R², s²d, stable flag.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: export_candidate_regions() converts define_qtl_regions() output "bed" (UCSC-compatible, 0-based), "csv", "biomart" format.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: decompose_block_effects() aggregates per-SNP additive effects per-haplotype-allele effect table: effect = sum(SNP_effect × allele_dosage).

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: scan_diversity_windows() computes , Shannon entropy, n_eff_alleles, dominant haplotype frequency sliding windows across genome, independently LD block boundaries.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: infer_block_haplotypes() converts raw haplotype strings structured per-individual, per-block diplotype table. phased input (run_ldx_pipeline(phase = TRUE)), phase_ambiguous always FALSE.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: collapse_haplotypes() merges alleles min_freq using "rare_to_other", "nearest" (Hamming-based), "tree_based" (UPGMA) strategies. label_map attribute records every original → collapsed mapping.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: harmonize_haplotypes() anchors allele identity reference dictionary, matching exact string nearest Hamming neighbour within max_hamming. Novel alleles receive label "<novel>". harmonization_report attribute reports match quality per block.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: test_block_haplotypes() uses unified Q+K mixed linear model simpleM multiple-testing correction (Gao et al. 2008, 2010, 2011). model jointly corrects population structure polygenic kinship: y = μ + α · x_hap + Σ β_k · PC_k + g + ε, g ~ MVN(0, σ²_g G) Key parameters: n_pcs = 0L (default, EMMAX): GRM random effect absorbs structure n_pcs = k: top-k GRM eigenvectors additional fixed effects (Q+K model) n_pcs = NULL: auto-selected GRM scree-plot elbow optimize_pcs = FALSE: TRUE, auto-selects n_pcs fitting null models k = 0..optimize_pcs_max minimising criterion set optimize_method. principled elbow heuristic GWAS. optimize_pcs_max = 10L: maximum PCs evaluated optimize_pcs = TRUE "bic" — minimise BIC null REML model "lambda" — minimise |λ_GC − 1| (genomic control calibration) "bic_lambda" (default, recommended) — hybrid: |λ−1| + 0.01·scaled_BIC. Minimises inflation/deflation BIC breaks ties toward fewer PCs "p_wald" — raw Wald p-value (use pre-corrected threshold) "p_fdr" — Benjamini-Hochberg FDR (recommended discovery) "p_simplem" — simpleM Bonferroni-style: min(p × Meff, 1) "p_simplem_sidak" — simpleM Šidák-style: 1−(1−p)^Meff (recommended correlated haplotype predictors) "chromosome" (recommended) — separate Meff per chromosome "global" — one genome-wide Meff "block" — one Meff per LD block (omnibus tests get Meff = 1) meff_percent_cut: variance threshold simpleM eigendecomposition (default 0.995) plot = TRUE: saves PDF plots (PNG). Three files per run: manhattan_<trait>.pdf, qq_<trait>.pdf, pca_grm.pdf (GRM PCA coloured phenotype), grm_scree.pdf (eigenvalue scree selected PC red) four p-value columns (p_wald, p_fdr, p_simplem, p_simplem_sidak) always present every output regardless sig_metric. estimate_diplotype_effects() also now supports full correction set new sig_metric parameter. four p-value columns (p_omnibus_adj, p_omnibus_fdr, p_omnibus_simplem, p_omnibus_simplem_sidak) always present $omnibus_tests regardless chosen sig_metric. estimate_diplotype_effects() decomposes variation block additive () dominance (d) components. Dominance ratio d/classifies gene action: 0 = additive, ±1 = complete dominance, |d/| > 1 = overdominance.
code: # FDR-based discovery (EMMAX, default) assoc_fdr <- test_block_haplotypes(   haps, blues = blues_vec, blocks = blocks,   sig_metric = "p_fdr", verbose = FALSE )  # simpleM Šidák with Q+K correction, chromosome-wise Meff assoc_sm <- test_block_haplotypes(   haps, blues = blues_vec, blocks = blocks,   n_pcs            = 3L,   sig_metric       = "p_simplem_sidak",   meff_scope       = "chromosome",   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   verbose          = FALSE ) assoc_sm$meff$trait$allele$chromosome   # per-chromosome effective test counts head(assoc_sm$block_tests[order(assoc_sm$block_tests$p_omnibus), ])

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: score_favorable_haplotypes() produces genome-wide stacking index individual (sum allele_effect × dosage across blocks, normalised [0, 1]). summarize_parent_haplotypes() produces long-format allele inventory candidate parents: alleles carry, dosage, population frequency. is_rare = TRUE flags alleles frequency < 10%.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: compare_block_effects() takes two test_block_haplotypes() result objects independent populations computes per-block statistics quantifying consistently haplotype allele effects replicate across panels. Key output columns (one row per block per trait):
code: # Use Pop A's block boundaries for Pop B (maximises shared alleles) haps_B_harm <- harmonize_haplotypes(   extract_haplotypes(geno_B, snp_info, blocks_A),   # same block coords   reference = haps_A ) assoc_A <- test_block_haplotypes(haps_A, blues = blues_A, blocks = blocks_A,                                   sig_metric = "p_simplem_sidak") assoc_B <- test_block_haplotypes(haps_B_harm, blues = blues_B, blocks = blocks_A,                                   sig_metric = "p_simplem_sidak")  # block_match = "id" (default): match by block_id string — fast, backward-compatible # block_match = "position":      match by genomic interval overlap — handles different #                                 LD block boundaries between populations conc <- compare_block_effects(   assoc_A, assoc_B,   pop1_name             = "PopA",   pop2_name             = "PopB",   blocks_pop1           = blocks_A,   # required for boundary_overlap_ratio + position matching   blocks_pop2           = blocks_B,   # supply Pop B's OWN block table (may differ from A)   block_match           = "position", # recommended when blocks differ between populations   overlap_min           = 0.50,       # min IoU for two blocks to be considered the same region   direction_threshold   = 0.75,   boundary_overlap_warn = 0.80 ) # $concordance$match_type: "exact" | "position" | "pop1_only" # "position" rows: same QTL region, different boundary definitions conc$concordance[conc$concordance$replicated, ]   # replicated blocks head(conc$shared_alleles)                          # per-allele IVW detail print(conc)

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: GWAS run externally (GAPIT, TASSEL, FarmCPU, PLINK, tool), compare_gwas_effects() compares block-level lead-SNP effects two populations. accepts either raw GWAS data frames pre-mapped output define_qtl_regions(). Key differences compare_block_effects(): SE derivation. Many GWAS tools omit standard error. SE absent, compare_gwas_effects() derives z-score: SE = |BETA| / |z|, z = Φ⁻¹(P/2). se_derived_pop1 / se_derived_pop2 output columns flag population required derivation. Shared marker panel. populations genotyped array sequenced reference, set snp_info_pop2 = NULL (default) reuse snp_info_pop1. populations different marker sets, supply snp_info_pop2 explicitly.
code: # Path 1: pre-mapped (recommended) — most auditable qtl_A <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_A, blocks, snp_info, p_threshold = 5e-8) qtl_B <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_B, blocks, snp_info, p_threshold = 5e-8)  conc <- compare_gwas_effects(   qtl_pop1      = qtl_A,   qtl_pop2      = qtl_B,   blocks_pop1   = blocks_A,       # Pop A's block table   blocks_pop2   = blocks_B,       # Pop B's block table (may differ from A)   block_match   = "position",     # match by genomic overlap — handles different boundaries   overlap_min   = 0.50,           # min IoU for a valid match   pop1_name     = "PopA",   pop2_name     = "PopB" )  # Path 2: raw GWAS + blocks (convenience — calls define_qtl_regions internally) conc2 <- compare_gwas_effects(   gwas_pop1     = gwas_A,   gwas_pop2     = gwas_B,   blocks_pop1   = blocks_A,   blocks_pop2   = blocks_B,   snp_info_pop1 = snp_info,   # snp_info_pop2 = NULL: reuses snp_info_pop1 when marker panels are shared   pop1_name     = "PopA",   pop2_name     = "PopB",   block_match   = "position",  # match blocks by genomic interval overlap   overlap_min   = 0.50,        # blocks with IoU < 0.5 become "pop1_only"   p_threshold   = 5e-8,   beta_col      = "BETA",      # column name — change to match your GWAS tool   se_col        = "SE",        # NULL or absent: derived from z-score automatically   p_col         = "P" )  # Output uses the same LDxBlocks_effect_concordance class # GWAS-specific extra columns in $concordance: #   lead_snp_pop1, lead_snp_pop2  — which SNP tagged the block in each pop #   lead_p_pop1, lead_p_pop2      — lead SNP p-values #   se_derived_pop1/pop2          — TRUE when SE was derived from z-score #   both_pleiotropic              — TRUE when block is pleiotropic in both pops conc$concordance[conc$concordance$replicated, ] print(conc)

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: LDxBlocks provides three functions epistasis detection extend haplotype association framework. operate GRM-corrected REML residuals null model test_block_haplotypes(), ensuring population-structure-corrected tests throughout. scan_block_epistasis() tests C(p,2) SNP pairs within significant block pairwise interaction GRM-corrected REML residuals. model pair : y = μ + aᵢxᵢ + aⱼxⱼ + aaᵢⱼ(xᵢ × xⱼ) + ε Restricting significant blocks avoids genome-wide explosion: 15 significant blocks ~200 SNPs , total number tests ~300,000 rather ~4.4 billion. Multiple testing corrected Bonferroni simpleM Sidak within block, Meff estimated eigenspectrum pairwise interaction column matrix. scan_block_by_block_epistasis() trans-haplotype epistasis scan tests significant haplotype allele every allele blocks. conceptually analogous trans-eQTL scan phenotypic haplotype interactions — detects genetic background dependence resistance haplotype one locus functions presence specific background another locus. 25 significant alleles × 17,943 total alleles, scan involves ~450,000 tests corrected Bonferroni. fine_map_epistasis_block() fine-maps single block identifying specific interacting SNP pairs. blocks p ≤ 200 SNPs runs exhaustive pairwise scan. larger blocks uses LASSO pairwise interaction terms (glmnet::cv.glmnet(), lambda.1se), avoids multiple-testing burden identifying influential pairs.
code: # Within-block epistasis scan (significant blocks only) epi_within <- scan_block_epistasis(   assoc              = assoc,          # test_block_haplotypes() result   geno_matrix        = res$geno_matrix,   snp_info           = snp_info,   blocks             = blocks,   blues              = blues_list,   haplotypes         = haps,   trait              = "BL",   sig_blocks         = NULL,           # NULL = use significant_omnibus blocks   max_snps_per_block = 300L,   sig_metric         = "p_simplem_sidak",   sig_threshold      = 0.05 ) print(epi_within) epi_within$results[epi_within$results$significant, ] epi_within$scan_summary  # Between-block trans-haplotype epistasis scan epi_between <- scan_block_by_block_epistasis(   assoc         = assoc,   haplotypes    = haps,   blues         = blues_list,   blocks        = blocks,   trait         = "BL",   sig_alleles   = NULL,    # NULL = significant alleles from assoc   sig_threshold = 0.05 ) print(epi_between) epi_between$results[epi_between$results$significant, ]  # Fine-map a single block (auto-dispatches: pairwise or LASSO) fine <- fine_map_epistasis_block(   block_id   = "block_12_1054210_1086071",   geno_matrix = res$geno_matrix,   snp_info    = snp_info,   blocks      = blocks,   y_resid     = my_reml_residuals,   # from .fit_null_reml() or null model   method      = "auto",              # pairwise <= 200 SNPs, lasso otherwise   sig_threshold = 0.05 ) head(fine)

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: eleven compiled functions src/ld_core.cpp replace expensive R operations. Key speedups: compute_r2_cpp(): ~40× pure R 1,500 × 1,500 window 500 individuals. boundary_scan_cpp(): eliminates ~150,000 small R-level matrix operations 50,000-SNP chromosome. maf_filter_cpp(): ~10× faster panels > 100,000 SNPs. resolve_overlap_cpp(): 15,700× per-SNP reduction chr1 via BLAS DGEMM scoring lazy column cache.

title: LDxBlocks -- Genome-Wide LD Block Detection
text: Numeric dosage CSV: two-pass chunked reading. Peak RAM = one 50,000-row chunk. VCF HapMap: auto-converted GDS cache; access streaming via read_chunk(). GDS PLINK BED: read_chunk() called per sub-segment per chromosome. Chromosome loop: rm() gc(FALSE) called chromosome.

what: Backsolve SNP Effects from GEBV (Tong et al. 2025) — backsolve_snp_effects
title: Backsolve SNP Effects from GEBV (Tong et al. 2025) — backsolve_snp_effects
text: Derives per-SNP additive effect estimates genome-wide genomic estimated breeding values (GEBV) without re-fitting marker model. implements Step 2 Tong et al. (2025) haplotype stacking pipeline: $$\hat{\alpha} = \frac{M^\top G^{-1} \hat{g}}{2 \sum_t p_t(1-p_t)}$$ \(M\) centred genotype matrix, \(G\) VanRaden GRM, \(\hat{g}\) GEBV, \(p_t\) allele frequencies.

title: Backsolve SNP Effects from GEBV (Tong et al. 2025) — backsolve_snp_effects
code: backsolve_snp_effects(geno_matrix, gebv, G = NULL)

title: Backsolve SNP Effects from GEBV (Tong et al. 2025) — backsolve_snp_effects
text: geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs), values 0/1/2/NA. Row names must match names gebv. gebv Named numeric vector GEBV, one per individual. Names must match rownames(geno_matrix). G Optional pre-computed VanRaden GRM (n x n). NULL (default), computed internally via compute_haplotype_grm()-style logic. Supply used bended tuned G GBLUP.
