what: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
title: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
text: Core per-chromosome LD block detection. Two LD metrics supported: method = "r2" (default) Standard squared Pearson     correlation, computed C++ Armadillo kernel. kinship matrix     required. Suitable large datasets (hundreds thousands     millions markers) unstructured mildly structured populations. method = "rV2" Kinship-adjusted squared correlation     (Kim et al. 2018). Requires computing inverting GRM via     AGHmatrix + ASRgenomics. Recommended highly related populations     (livestock, inbred lines) moderate marker counts (< 200 k per chr). genome-wide analyses use run_Big_LD_all_chr. automatic parameter tuning use tune_LD_params.

title: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
code: Big_LD(   geno,   SNPinfo,   method = c("r2", "rV2"),   CLQcut = 0.5,   clstgap = 40000,   leng = 200,   subSegmSize = 1500,   MAFcut = 0.05,   appendrare = FALSE,   singleton_as_block = FALSE,   checkLargest = FALSE,   CLQmode = c("Density", "Maximal", "Louvain", "Leiden"),   kin_method = "chol",   split = FALSE,   max_bp_distance = 0L,   digits = -1L,   n_threads = 1L,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
text: geno Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs), 0/1/2. Row names used individual IDs; auto-generated absent. SNPinfo Data frame: col 1 = rsID, col 2 = bp position. method Character. "r2" (default) "rV2". CLQcut Numeric (0,1]. LD threshold clique edges. Default 0.5. clstgap Integer. Max bp gap within clique (split=TRUE). Default 40000. leng Integer. Boundary-scan half-window (SNPs). Default 200. subSegmSize Integer. Max SNPs per CLQD call. Default 1500. MAFcut Numeric. Minor allele frequency minimum. Default 0.05. appendrare Logical. Append rare SNPs block detection. Default FALSE. singleton_as_block Logical. TRUE, every SNP passes MAF filtering pairwise r^2 CLQcut neighbours (.e. assigned clique) returned single-SNP block start == end length_bp == 1. Default FALSE. blocks excluded haplotype analysis default min_snps = 3 threshold extract_haplotypes(), useful auditing coverage single-SNP feature engineering. checkLargest Logical. Dense-core pre-pass large windows. Default FALSE. CLQmode "Density" (default) "Maximal". kin_method Character. GRM whitening: "chol" (default) "eigen". Ignored method = "r2". split Logical. Split cliques gaps > clstgap. Default FALSE. digits Integer. LD rounding: -1 (default, rounding) positive integer. n_threads Integer. OpenMP threads C++ LD kernel. Default 1. seed Integer NULL. Set reproducibility. verbose Logical. Progress messages.

title: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
text: data.frame columns: start, end, start.rsID, end.rsID,   start.bp, end.bp.

title: LD Block Segmentation (r^2 or rV^2, C++ accelerated) — Big_LD
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) m <- 80; b1 <- 60; b2 <- 60 make_block <- function(m, size, p, flip = 0.03) {   seed_snp <- rbinom(m, 2, p)   M <- matrix(seed_snp, nrow = m, ncol = size)   for (j in seq_len(size)) {     idx <- sample.int(m, max(1L, floor(flip * m)))     M[idx, j] <- pmin(2L, pmax(0L, M[idx, j] + sample(c(-1L, 1L), length(idx), TRUE)))   }   M } G <- cbind(make_block(m, b1, 0.30), make_block(m, b2, 0.60)) colnames(G) <- paste0("rs", seq_len(ncol(G))) rownames(G) <- paste0("ind", seq_len(nrow(G))) pos <- c(seq(1, by = 1000, length.out = b1), seq(5e6, by = 1000, length.out = b2)) SNPinfo <- data.frame(SNP = colnames(G), POS = pos) blocks <- LDxBlocks:::Big_LD(G, SNPinfo, method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.6,                  leng = 30, subSegmSize = 120, verbose = FALSE) head(blocks) } # }

what: Build Haplotype Dosage Matrix for Genomic Prediction — build_haplotype_feature_matrix
title: Build Haplotype Dosage Matrix for Genomic Prediction — build_haplotype_feature_matrix
text: Converts haplotype strings numeric matrix genomic   prediction. Supports phased unphased input two encoding schemes. encoding="additive_012" (default, recommended GBLUP/rrBLUP/BGLR):     Phased:   0=0 copies, 1=1 copy (het), 2=2 copies (hom)     Unphased: 0=match, 2=match (1 identifiable without phase) encoding="presence_02" (kernel methods, random forest):     Phased:   2=either gamete matches, 0=neither, NA=missing     Unphased: 2=match, 0=match, NA=missing

title: Build Haplotype Dosage Matrix for Genomic Prediction — build_haplotype_feature_matrix
code: build_haplotype_feature_matrix(   haplotypes,   top_n = NULL,   encoding = c("additive_012", "presence_01"),   missing_string = ".",   scale_features = FALSE,   min_freq = 0.01 )

title: Build Haplotype Dosage Matrix for Genomic Prediction — build_haplotype_feature_matrix
text: haplotypes List extract_haplotypes(). top_n Integer NULL. Maximum number haplotype alleles retain per block, ranked frequency. NULL (default) retains alleles pass min_freq – recommended analyses. Set integer cap (e.g. top_n = 5L) need limit matrix width memory reasons panels thousands blocks highly diverse haplotypes (many rare alleles min_freq). encoding Dosage encoding feature matrix: "additive_012" (default):     Phased data: values 0 (neither gamete carries allele),     1 (one gamete carries - heterozygous), 2 (gametes - homozygous).     gives true allele dosage standard 0/1/2 scale.     Unphased data: values 0 1 (presence/absence     dosage-pattern haplotype). value 2 never produced unphased     data impossible confirm chromosomes carry     haplotype string without phase information.     Compatible rrBLUP, BGLR, sommer, ASReml-R. "presence_01": values 0 1 phased unphased     data. phased data: 1 either gamete carries allele (loses     copy-number information vs "additive_012"). unphased data:     identical "additive_012" since already gives 0/1.     May preferable Bayesian variable selection models (BayesB,     BayesC) prior expects binary indicators. missing_string Missing data marker. Default ".". scale_features Center scale columns. Default FALSE. min_freq Minimum allele frequency include. Default 0.01.

title: Build Haplotype Dosage Matrix for Genomic Prediction — build_haplotype_feature_matrix
text: Numeric matrix (individuals x haplotype allele columns).

title: Build Haplotype Dosage Matrix for Genomic Prediction — build_haplotype_feature_matrix
text: Phased data (read_phased_vcf, phase_with_beagle): individual's block string "g1|g2" g1 g2 two gametic sequences. Haplotype alleles identified gamete level - true haplotypes biological sense. Frequencies gamete frequencies (individual contributes two observations). Dosage values 0, 1, 2 measure actual allele copy number. Unphased data (unphased VCF dosage matrix): individual's block string single multi-SNP dosage string (e.g. "021002"). Haplotype alleles distinct dosage patterns, true gametic haplotypes. Frequencies measure proportion individuals carrying dosage pattern. biologically meaningful (distinct genotypic patterns block level) equated true haplotype allele frequency without phasing. recommended workflow true haplotype analysis : phase first Beagle, call extract_haplotypes() phased output.

title: Clique-Based LD Block Detection — CLQD
text: Partitions SNPs genomic window LD cliques. Three algorithms available via CLQmode: "Density" (default) Standard Big-LD clique scoring – edges     built full r? matrix, cliques found Bron-Kerbosch     (igraph::max_cliques), scored density = size / span.     Exact, exponential worst-case. Use checkLargest = TRUE     guard blowup dense WGS panels. "Maximal" algorithm Density scored clique     size (original Big-LD maximality criterion). "Louvain" Louvain community detection     (igraph::cluster_louvain). Polynomial time O(n log n) – never     exponential. Recommended WGS panels (>500 SNPs per window)     time CLQmode = "Density" slow. Communities returned     bin IDs matching Density output format downstream code     unaffected. "Leiden" Leiden community detection     (igraph::cluster_leiden). Stricter Louvain – produces     well-connected communities without disconnected nodes. Requires     igraph >= 1.3.0. Slightly slower Louvain higher quality     communities. Recommended Louvain resolution matters. max_bp_distance supplied (> 0), SNP pairs within physical distance r? computed (compute_r2_sparse_cpp). reduces O(p?) LD computation near-O(p) WGS panels long-range LD negligible (typically > 500 kb). Applied clique algorithm.

title: Clique-Based LD Block Detection — CLQD
code: CLQD(   subgeno,   subSNPinfo,   adj_subgeno,   CLQcut = 0.5,   clstgap = 40000,   CLQmode = c("Density", "Maximal", "Louvain", "Leiden"),   codechange = FALSE,   checkLargest = FALSE,   split = FALSE,   digits = -1L,   n_threads = 1L,   max_bp_distance = 0L,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Clique-Based LD Block Detection — CLQD
text: subgeno Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs), 0/1/2 coded. subSNPinfo Data frame: col 1 = rsID, col 2 = bp position. adj_subgeno n x p matrix prepare_geno(). CLQcut Numeric (0,1]. LD threshold edges. Default 0.5. clstgap Integer. Max bp gap within clique (split=TRUE). CLQmode Algorithm: "Density" (default, exact, exponential worst-case), "Maximal" (exact, maximality criterion), "Louvain" (polynomial, recommended WGS), "Leiden" (polynomial, stricter communities, igraph >= 1.3.0). codechange Logical. Sign-flip heuristic. Default FALSE. checkLargest Logical. Dense-core pre-pass >= 500 SNPs using Density Maximal mode. Default FALSE. effect CLQmode Louvain Leiden (already polynomial). split Logical. Split cliques gaps. Default FALSE. digits Integer. Rounding LD. -1 = none (default). n_threads Integer. OpenMP threads. Default 1. max_bp_distance Integer. Maximum base-pair distance SNP pair r? computed. Pairs beyond distance assumed negligible LD set 0. Default 0L = disabled (compute pairs, original behaviour). Recommended value WGS panels: 500000L (500 kb). effect window spans less max_bp_distance. verbose Logical.

title: Clique-Based LD Block Detection — CLQD
text: Integer vector length p: clique/community ID per SNP, NA = singleton.

what: Collapse Rare Haplotype Alleles Into Biologically Meaningful Groups — collapse_haplotypes
title: Collapse Rare Haplotype Alleles Into Biologically Meaningful Groups — collapse_haplotypes
text: Rather dropping rare haplotype alleles frequency threshold (build_haplotype_feature_matrix ), function merges appropriate existing allele. Three strategies supported: "rare_to_other" alleles min_freq     pooled single catch-"<>" category. Simple     lossless total frequency; best rare alleles truly     heterogeneous. "nearest" rare allele merged similar     common allele (minimum Hamming distance). Preserves biological     similarity; best rare alleles likely sequencing errors     recent recombinants existing haplotypes. "tree_based" Builds UPGMA dendrogram pairwise     Hamming distances merges rare alleles cutting tree     level produces fewest groups keeping groups     min_freq. Computationally heavier biologically     principled.

Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max.  #> -0.07549 -0.06681 -0.04398 -0.05121 -0.03950 -0.03319  # }

title: Cross-Population Haplotype Effect Concordance — compare_block_effects
text: Given two allele_tests data frames produced test_block_haplotypes two independent populations, computes per-block (optionally per-trait) statistics quantify consistently haplotype allele effects replicate across populations. Statistics computed per block: Effect correlation (\(r\)) Pearson correlation per-allele     effect sizes populations across shared alleles. Requires     least 3 shared alleles; NA otherwise. Direction agreement Proportion shared alleles     populations assign sign effect. value >= 0.75     (.e. least 3 4 alleles agree direction) considered     strong directional replication. Inverse-variance weighted (IVW) meta-analytic effect weighted     mean effect \(\hat\beta_{\mathrm{IVW}} = \sum w_i \beta_i /     \sum w_i\) \(w_i = 1/\mathrm{SE}_i^2\).  Computed separately     shared allele summarised mean per-allele IVW     estimates. IVW meta-analysis framework used     two-sample Mendelian randomisation cross-population GWAS     meta-analysis (see Borenstein et al. 2009). Cochran's Q heterogeneity statistic Tests whether effect sizes     differ significantly two populations:     \(Q = \sum w_i (\beta_i - \hat\beta_{\mathrm{IVW}})^2\).     null heterogeneity, \(Q \sim \chi^2_{n-1}\).     Significant Q (large \(Q_p\)) indicates effect sizes differ     populations - sign GxE interaction, LD structure     differences, population-specific allelic action. \(^2\) inconsistency \(^2 = 100 \times \max(0,\, (Q - df)/Q)\).     Values > 50% indicate substantial -population heterogeneity. Block boundary concordance blocks_pop1     blocks_pop2 supplied, bp overlap ratio two     populations' block definitions computed block. ratio     < 0.8 flags blocks LD structure likely differs     populations effect comparisons interpreted cautiously. Addressing two principal limitations cross-population validation: Population structure confounding: Q+K mixed model     test_block_haplotypes() already corrects within-population     structure via haplotype GRM.  Cross-population validation     inherently controls -population confounding design:     haplotype allele must associate phenotype genetically     distinct background, making false-positive carry-Pop 's     stratification implausible.  meta-analytic \(^2\) statistic     additionally flags cases effect sizes heterogeneous     shared biological mechanism unlikely. LD block boundary differences: Block boundaries can differ     populations due different historical recombination rates     ancestral haplotype structure.  boundary_overlap_ratio     column quantifies directly every block.  Low overlap (< 0.8)     means two populations carve genomic region blocks     different sizes; case haplotype allele tested 50 kb     window Pop compared allele 30 kb window Pop B     strings match well.  n_shared_alleles     column empirical consequence: low shared-allele counts     despite adequate allele frequencies populations direct     symptom block boundary mismatch.  recommended remedy - passing     Pop 's block table blocks argument     test_block_haplotypes() Pop B - eliminates issue     entirely forcing runs use identical coordinates.

title: Cross-Population Haplotype Effect Concordance — compare_block_effects
code: compare_block_effects(   assoc_pop1,   assoc_pop2,   pop1_name = "pop1",   pop2_name = "pop2",   traits = NULL,   min_shared_alleles = 2L,   blocks_pop1 = NULL,   blocks_pop2 = NULL,   block_match = c("id", "position"),   overlap_min = 0.5,   direction_threshold = 0.75,   boundary_overlap_warn = 0.8,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Cross-Population Haplotype Effect Concordance — compare_block_effects
text: assoc_pop1 Output test_block_haplotypes population 1 (discovery). Must contain allele_tests data frame columns block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp, trait, allele, effect, SE, p_wald. assoc_pop2 Output test_block_haplotypes population 2 (validation). structure assoc_pop1. pop1_name Character. Label population 1 output. Default "pop1". pop2_name Character. Label population 2 output. Default "pop2". traits Character vector NULL. Traits compare. NULL (default), traits present result objects included. min_shared_alleles Integer. Minimum number alleles shared two populations block included output. Blocks fewer shared alleles retained marked enough_shared = FALSE. Default 2L. blocks_pop1 Optional data frame LD blocks population 1 (output run_Big_LD_all_chr). blocks_pop1 blocks_pop2 supplied, boundary_overlap_ratio column computed every block. Required columns: block_id (constructible CHR/start.bp/end.bp), start.bp, end.bp. blocks_pop2 Optional data frame LD blocks population 2. structure blocks_pop1. block_match Character. match blocks populations. "id" (default) - match exact block_id string.     Fast backward-compatible. Use populations analysed     block table (recommended workflow: run     test_block_haplotypes() populations using Pop 's block     boundaries Pop B well). "position" - match genomic interval overlap     (Intersection--Union, IoU, base pairs). Pop1 block,     best-matching Pop2 block chromosome found. Blocks     IoU \(\geq\) overlap_min matched;     labelled "pop1_only". Use block boundaries genuinely     differ populations (different ancestral LD structures,     different CLQcut used, independent block detection runs). overlap_min Numeric (0, 1]. Minimum Intersection--Union (IoU) base pairs two blocks considered region block_match = "position". Default 0.50. Blocks threshold assigned match_type = "pop1_only" compared. Lower values (e.g. 0.30) tolerate boundary discordance; higher values (e.g. 0.80) require tighter boundary agreement. Ignored block_match = "id". direction_threshold Numeric (0.5, 1]. Minimum direction-agreement proportion consider block directionally concordant. Default 0.75. boundary_overlap_warn Numeric (0, 1). Threshold used raise warning flag output. automatically computed boundary_overlap_ratio (output column, user-set value) threshold, corresponding boundary_warning output column set TRUE. Default 0.80. Raise value (e.g. 0.90) flag conservatively, lower (e.g. 0.60) flag severely mismatched blocks. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default TRUE.

title: Cross-Population Haplotype Effect Concordance — compare_block_effects
text: named list class c("LDxBlocks_effect_concordance", "list"): concordance Data frame one row per block per trait.     Columns: block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp,         trait - block coordinates trait name. n_alleles_pop1, n_alleles_pop2 - alleles tested         population (intersection). n_shared_alleles - alleles present populations. enough_shared - logical; TRUE         n_shared_alleles >= min_shared_alleles. effect_correlation - Pearson r per-allele effects         across populations (NA n_shared < 3). direction_agreement - proportion shared alleles         concordant effect signs. directionally_concordant - logical; direction_agreement         >= direction_threshold. meta_effect - IVW meta-analytic effect (mean shared         alleles, weighted combined inverse-variance). meta_SE - SE IVW estimate. meta_z - meta-analytic z-score. meta_p - two-sided p-value meta-analytic effect. Q_stat - Cochran Q heterogeneity statistic. Q_df - degrees freedom Q (n_shared - 1). Q_p - p-value Q chi-squared distribution. I2 - \(^2\) inconsistency (0-100%). replicated - logical; TRUE         directionally_concordant Q_p > 0.05         enough_shared. boundary_overlap_ratio - bp intersection / bp union         two populations' block boundaries. NA block tables         supplied. boundary_warning - logical; TRUE         boundary_overlap_ratio < boundary_overlap_warn. blocks         interpreted cautiously different LD structures         likely produce non-comparable haplotype strings. match_type - character; block matched         populations: "exact" (block_id string),         "position" (matched genomic IoU \(\geq\)         overlap_min block_match = "position"),         "pop1_only" (Pop2 block overlapped threshold),         NA block tables supplied. Sorted CHR, start_bp, meta_p (ascending). shared_alleles Data frame. One row per shared allele per     block per trait. Contains effect_pop1, SE_pop1,     p_wald_pop1, effect_pop2, SE_pop2,     p_wald_pop2, direction_agree, ivw_effect,     ivw_SE, detailed per-allele inspection. pop1_name, pop2_name Character. Population labels. block_match Character. Matching strategy used. overlap_min Numeric. IoU threshold used (relevant     block_match = "position"). traits Character vector traits compared. direction_threshold Numeric. Threshold used. boundary_overlap_warn Numeric. Boundary warning threshold.

what: Cross-Population GWAS Effect Concordance from External Results — compare_gwas_effects
title: Cross-Population GWAS Effect Concordance from External Results — compare_gwas_effects
text: Compares haplotype block-level association signals two populations GWAS run externally (e.g. GAPIT, TASSEL, FarmCPU, PLINK, tool) rather test_block_haplotypes. function accepts external GWAS results two ways: Pre-mapped (recommended): supply output     define_qtl_regions population. QTL table     already maps marker LD block, providing lead SNP,     effect size (BETA), p-value per block.     reliable input block assignment explicit. Raw GWAS + blocks: supply raw GWAS data frames plus block     tables; function calls define_qtl_regions internally     map markers blocks comparing. compared : External GWAS produces marker-level effects, haplotype-allele-level effects. block level population contributes one observation: lead SNP (smallest p-value block). comparison therefore analogous two-sample Mendelian randomisation - one effect estimate per exposure (block) per population, combined via IVW meta-analysis. one "allele" per block (lead SNP), several statistics differ compare_block_effects: n_shared_alleles always 1 (lead SNP concept). effect_correlation always NA (needs >= 3 alleles). direction_agreement 0 1 (sign lead SNP effect agrees     ). Q_df always 0 (one observation per population; Cochran Q     undefined two populations one allele per block). meta_p tests whether IVW combined effect differs     zero - primary replication test. Standard error derivation: Many GWAS tools report BETA P SE. SE absent, compare_gwas_effects() derives z-score: $$SE = \frac{|\beta|}{|z|},\quad z = \Phi^{-1}(P/2)$$ \(\Phi^{-1}\) standard normal quantile function. exact large-sample Wald tests (dominant GWAS framework) provides valid SE IVW weighting. Interpreting pleiotropic blocks: trait_col supplied GWAS results contain multiple traits, function compares trait trait. Blocks significant multiple traits populations (pleiotropic = TRUE QTL tables) flagged both_pleiotropic = TRUE output, identifying robust cross-population replication targets.

title: Cross-Population GWAS Effect Concordance from External Results — compare_gwas_effects
code: compare_gwas_effects(   qtl_pop1 = NULL,   qtl_pop2 = NULL,   gwas_pop1 = NULL,   gwas_pop2 = NULL,   blocks_pop1 = NULL,   blocks_pop2 = NULL,   snp_info_pop1 = NULL,   snp_info_pop2 = NULL,   pop1_name = "pop1",   pop2_name = "pop2",   beta_col = "BETA",   se_col = "SE",   p_col = "P",   trait_col = "trait",   p_threshold = 5e-08,   min_snps = 3L,   block_match = c("id", "position"),   overlap_min = 0.5,   direction_threshold = 0.75,   boundary_overlap_warn = 0.8,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Cross-Population GWAS Effect Concordance from External Results — compare_gwas_effects
text: qtl_pop1 Data frame. Output define_qtl_regions population 1 (discovery). Required columns: block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp, lead_snp, lead_p, lead_beta. Optional: traits, pleiotropic. Ignored gwas_pop1 supplied instead. qtl_pop2 Data frame. Output define_qtl_regions population 2 (validation). structure qtl_pop1. Ignored gwas_pop2 supplied instead. gwas_pop1 Data frame. Raw GWAS results population 1. Required qtl_pop1 NULL. Must contain SNP (Marker), CHR, POS, columns named beta_col, se_col (optional), p_col. gwas_pop2 Data frame. Raw GWAS results population 2. structure gwas_pop1. blocks_pop1 Data frame. LD block table population 1 (output run_Big_LD_all_chr). Required gwas_pop1 supplied. Also used boundary_overlap_ratio computation qtl_pop1 input. blocks_pop2 Data frame. LD block table population 2. structure blocks_pop1. snp_info_pop1 Data frame. SNP metadata population 1 (columns: SNP, CHR, POS). Required gwas_pop1 supplied. snp_info_pop2 Data frame. SNP metadata population 2. structure snp_info_pop1. NULL (default), snp_info_pop1 reused (appropriate populations share marker panel). pop1_name Character. Label population 1. Default "pop1". pop2_name Character. Label population 2. Default "pop2". beta_col Character. Name effect-size column raw GWAS data frames. Default "BETA". se_col Character NULL. Name standard-error column. NULL absent, SE derived beta_col p_col via z-score formula. Default "SE". p_col Character. Name p-value column. Default "P". trait_col Character. Name trait column GWAS results contain multiple traits. Default "trait". p_threshold Numeric. Significance threshold applied mapping raw GWAS results blocks via define_qtl_regions. Ignored qtl_pop1 / qtl_pop2 supplied directly. Default 5e-8. min_snps Integer. Minimum number SNPs block included QTL mapping step. Default 3L. block_match Character. match blocks populations. "id" (default) matches exact block_id string - fast backward-compatible, appropriate populations mapped block table. "position" matches genomic interval overlap (Intersection--Union \(\geq\) overlap_min) - recommended block boundaries differ populations due different ancestral LD structures independent block-detection runs. overlap_min Numeric (0, 1]. Minimum Intersection--Union (IoU) base pairs two blocks considered region block_match = "position". Default 0.50. Ignored block_match = "id". direction_threshold Numeric (0.5, 1]. Minimum direction agreement consider block directionally concordant. Default 0.75. Note: one lead SNP per block effectively 0/1 flag threshold, parameter kept consistency compare_block_effects. boundary_overlap_warn Numeric (0, 1). boundary_overlap_ratio value triggers boundary_warning = TRUE. boundary_overlap_ratio **output column** automatically computed block tables - set user. Default 0.80. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default TRUE.

text: Data frame one row per block, sorted CHR   start_bp. block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp Block coordinates. n1, n2 Sample sizes group 1 group 2. n_alleles Number distinct alleles block. FST Weir-Cockerham FST, clamped [0,1]. max_freq_diff Maximum absolute allele frequency difference. dominant_g1, dominant_g2 frequent allele group. chisq_p Chi-squared p-value (Monte Carlo). NA < 2 alleles. divergent Logical; TRUE FST > 0.1 chisq_p < 0.05.

title: Compare Haplotype Allele Frequencies Between Two Population Groups — compare_haplotype_populations

code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, package = "LDxBlocks") haps <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) ids  <- rownames(ldx_geno) cmp  <- compare_haplotype_populations(   haplotypes   = haps,   group1       = ids[1:60],   group2       = ids[61:120],   group1_name  = "cycle1",   group2_name  = "cycle2" ) cmp[cmp$divergent, c("block_id", "FST", "max_freq_diff", "chisq_p")] #> [1] block_id      FST           max_freq_diff chisq_p       #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) # }

what: Compute Haplotype Diversity Per Block — compute_haplotype_diversity
title: Compute Haplotype Diversity Per Block — compute_haplotype_diversity
text: Calculates per-block haplotype diversity metrics: richness (n_haplotypes), expected heterozygosity (, Nei 1973 sample-size corrected), Shannon entropy, effective number alleles (1/\(\sum p_i^2\)), dominant haplotype frequency, sweep flag (TRUE freq_dominant \(\geq\) 0.90). metrics directly correspond used characterise block diversity identify selection signatures Difabachew et al. (2023) Tong et al. (2024). Phased data contributes two gamete observations per individual, doubling effective sample size.

title: Compute Haplotype Diversity Per Block — compute_haplotype_diversity
code: compute_haplotype_diversity(haplotypes, missing_string = ".")

what: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
title: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
text: Computes additive genomic relationship matrix (GRM) haplotype feature matrix using VanRaden (2008) method extended multi-allelic haplotype blocks. resulting G matrix can used directly GBLUP, rrBLUP, ASReml-R, software accepts realized relationship matrix.

title: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
code: compute_haplotype_grm(hap_matrix, bend = FALSE, phased = NULL)

title: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
text: hap_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x haplotype alleles) build_haplotype_feature_matrix. bend Logical. TRUE, add small constant diagonal ensure positive-definiteness: diag(G) + 0.001. Useful passing G mixed model solvers. Default FALSE. phased Logical NULL (default). Whether haplotype feature matrix uses additive_012 encoding phased data (dosage values 0/1/2, dose scale = 2) unphased data (presence/absence 0/1, dose scale = 1). NULL auto-detects column maxima: column value > 1 implies phased encoding. guaranteed correctness haplotype alleles lack homozygous carriers (max=1 even phased data), supply phased = TRUE FALSE explicitly.

title: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
text: Symmetric n x n numeric matrix (individuals x individuals).   Row column names match rownames(hap_matrix).

title: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
text: GRM computed : $$G = \frac{ZZ^\top}{2\sum_j p_j(1-p_j)}$$ \(Z\) centred haplotype dosage matrix (columns centred \(2p_j\)) \(p_j\) frequency haplotype allele \(j\). matches standard G matrix Weber et al. (2023) Difabachew et al. (2023), ensuring relationship scale compatible conventional SNP-based GRMs. Missing dosage values (NA) mean-imputed per column centering.

title: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
text: VanRaden PM (2008). Efficient methods compute genomic predictions. Journal Dairy Science 91(11):4414-4423. doi:10.3168/jds.2007-0980 Weber SE et al. (2023). Haplotype blocks genomic prediction: comparative evaluation multiple crop datasets. Frontiers Plant Science 14:1217589. doi:10.3389/fpls.2023.1217589

title: Compute Haplotype-Based Genomic Relationship Matrix — compute_haplotype_grm
code: data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, package = "LDxBlocks") haps <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, min_snps = 3) feat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, top_n = 5)$matrix G    <- compute_haplotype_grm(feat) dim(G) #> [1] 120 120 round(range(diag(G)), 3)  # diagonal ~= 1 for typical populations #> [1] 0.162 0.797

"r2" (default) "rV2". digits Integer. Rounding precision. -1 skips rounding. n_threads Integer. OpenMP threads C++ kernel. Default 1. Use parallel::detectCores() choose automatically.

text: Symmetric p x p numeric matrix, diagonal 0, values [0, 1].

what: Compute LD Decay and Chromosome-Specific Decay Distances — compute_ld_decay
title: Compute LD Decay and Chromosome-Specific Decay Distances — compute_ld_decay
text: Calculates decay linkage disequilibrium (r\(^2\) rV\(^2\)) physical distance chromosome, fits optional decay models (Hill-Weir nonlinear LOESS), determines distance LD drops user-specified data-driven critical threshold. output can passed directly define_qtl_regions() define chromosome-specific candidate gene windows around GWAS hits.

title: Compute LD Decay and Chromosome-Specific Decay Distances — compute_ld_decay
code: compute_ld_decay(   geno,   snp_info = NULL,   method = c("r2", "rV2"),   kin_method = c("chol", "eigen"),   sampling = c("random", "sliding_window", "both"),   window_snps = 50L,   n_pairs = 50000L,   max_dist = 5000000L,   r2_threshold = "both",   n_unlinked = 100000L,   pctile = 95,   fit_model = c("loess", "nonlinear", "both", "none"),   n_bins = 100L,   by_chr = TRUE,   chr = NULL,   seed = 42L,   n_threads = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute LD Decay and Chromosome-Specific Decay Distances — compute_ld_decay
text: geno LDxBlocks_backend (read_geno read_geno_bigmemory), numeric dosage matrix (individuals x SNPs), file path accepted read_geno. snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS. Auto-extracted backend geno backend object. method LD metric: "r2" (default) "rV2" (kinship-adjusted; requires AGHmatrix ASRgenomics). kin_method Whitening method rV2: "chol" (default) "eigen". sampling Pair-sampling strategy: "random" (default, WGS-safe), "sliding_window" (TASSEL approach), "". window_snps Integer. Window size SNPs "sliding_window". Default 50L. n_pairs Integer. Maximum SNP pairs per chromosome "random" sampling. Default 50000L. max_dist Integer. Maximum physical distance (bp) pair. Default 5000000L (5 Mb). r2_threshold Threshold decay distance: numeric value (e.g. 0.1), "parametric" (95th percentile unlinked r\(^2\)), "", NULL. Default "". n_unlinked Integer. Cross-chromosome pairs parametric threshold. Default 100000L. pctile Numeric (0-100). Percentile unlinked r\(^2\) distribution parametric threshold. Default 95. fit_model Decay model: "loess" (default), "nonlinear" (Hill-Weir), "", "none". n_bins Integer. Distance bins smoothed decay curve. Default 100L. by_chr Logical. Return per-chromosome decay distances. Default TRUE. chr Character vector chromosomes process. NULL = . seed Integer. RNG seed. Default 42L. n_threads Integer. OpenMP threads C++ r\(^2\) kernel. Default 1L. verbose Logical. Print timestamped progress. Default TRUE.

title: Compute LD Decay and Chromosome-Specific Decay Distances — compute_ld_decay
text: named list class LDxBlocks_decay: pairs Data frame: CHR, dist_bp, r2. decay_curve Binned decay curve per chromosome:     CHR, dist_bp, r2_mean, r2_median,     n_pairs, optionally r2_loess, r2_hw. decay_dist Per-chromosome decay distance:     CHR, decay_dist_bp, decay_dist_kb,     threshold_used, r2_col_used, censored     (TRUE smoothed curve never drops threshold     within max_dist – returned distance lower bound,     estimate; extend max_dist lower threshold). decay_dist_genome Genome-wide median decay distance (bp). critical_r2 Active threshold value. critical_r2_fixed Always set 0.1 reference     value (standard threshold used GWAS papers). field     populated regardless r2_threshold requested.     active threshold r2_threshold = 0.1     r2_threshold = "" parametric result available. critical_r2_param Parametric threshold (NULL     computed). unlinked_r2 Cross-chromosome r\(^2\) values used     parametric threshold (NULL computed). model_params Hill-Weir C parameter per chromosome     (NULL fitted). n_pairs_used Named integer: pairs computed per chromosome. method LD metric used. sampling Sampling strategy used. call Matched function call.

title: Compute LD Decay and Chromosome-Specific Decay Distances — compute_ld_decay
text: C++ acceleration: pairwise r\(^2\) computations use package's compiled C++ kernels (Armadillo BLAS via RcppArmadillo): Random sampling: compute_r2_sparse_cpp() computes     pairs within max_dist single C++ call compact     submatrix, replacing R pair--pair loop. n_threads     forwarded enable OpenMP parallelism. Sliding window: column preparation done per window     via apply(); within-window stats::cor() used     (windows small, typically 50 SNPs). Parametric threshold: col_r2_cpp() computes     cross-chromosome r\(^2\) Armadillo BLAS. Memory-efficient backend access: GDS bigmemory backends function never loads full chromosome. Instead samples pair indices SNP positions (genotype /O), collects unique SNP indices involved, calls read_chunk() compact set. 50,000 random pairs 500,000-SNP chromosome 500 individuals, peak RAM approximately 40 MB rather 2 GB. Important: method = "rV2" memory-safe WGS scale. kinship whitening matrix requires full-genome read SNPs per-chromosome processing begins. panels millions markers can dominant memory step. Use method = "r2" LD decay estimation; reserve method = "rV2" block detection. Sampling strategies: "sliding_window" Computes r\(^2\) within moving     window window_snps SNPs, reading columns per     step. Uses adaptive stride: window placed     approximately n_pairs / (window_snps*(window_snps-1)/2)     evenly-spaced positions across chromosome, total pairs     stays near n_pairs regardless marker density.     window_snps=50 500k-SNP chromosome     n_pairs=50000: stride=12,195, 41 read_chunk     calls instead 10,000. density guard automatically     switches chromosomes > 200,000 SNPs "random"     sampling (consecutive SNPs WGS density always near-     perfect LD, making sliding-window curve uninformative).     Matches TASSEL approach (Bradbury et al. 2007); recommended     chip-density panels (< 200k SNPs per chromosome). "random" Randomly samples n_pairs SNP pairs     per chromosome within max_dist bp, loads unique     SNPs involved. Bounded RAM regardless panel density; recommended     WGS panels (> 500k SNPs per chromosome). "" Sliding window decay curve shape;     random sampling parametric threshold estimation. Critical r\(^2\) threshold: Fixed numeric (e.g. 0.1) Standard value used GWAS     papers. distance decay curve crosses value defines     LD block extent candidate gene searches. "parametric" 95th percentile r\(^2\) markers     different chromosomes (unlinked markers). structured     population, unlinked r\(^2\) > 0 due kinship. threshold     captures background LD attributable structure, LD     genuine. high parametric threshold (> 0.05) strong indicator     method = "rV2" used block detection. "" Returns ; uses parametric active     threshold decay distance estimation. Decay model: Hill-Weir (1988) expectation relates expected r\(^2\) distance \(d\) (Mb) via \(C = 4 N_e r d\). LDxBlocks fits per chromosome C free parameter via nonlinear least squares. LOESS smooth non-parametric alternative robust non-standard decay shapes. Connection define_qtl_regions() GWAS integration: output compute_ld_decay() can passed directly define_qtl_regions(ld_decay = decay) replace fixed block boundaries chromosome-specific LD-aware windows. Without ld_decay define_qtl_regions() asks     blocks CONTAIN significant GWAS SNP? search     window equals block boundary. GWAS hits gaps blocks     near block edges missed. ld_decay Asks blocks LD     significant GWAS SNP? search window extended     chromosome-specific decay distance sides. Adds columns     candidate_region_start, candidate_region_end,     candidate_region_size_kb ready BioMart/Ensembl     Plants candidate gene queries. directly feeds integrate_gwas_haplotypes(): GWAS biological-evidence layer (layer 1 three-evidence score) awards point blocks contain GWAS hit. ld_decay, blocks near hit (within decay distance) also receive evidence point, making three-evidence scoring rank_haplotype_blocks() sensitive biologically accurate. recommended workflow therefore: decay <- compute_ld_decay(, ...) qtl <- define_qtl_regions(..., ld_decay = decay) ranked <- integrate_gwas_haplotypes(qtl, pred, ...)

title: Compute LD Decay and Chromosome-Specific Decay Distances — compute_ld_decay
code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno,     package = "LDxBlocks") data(ldx_snp_info, package = "LDxBlocks")  # Random sampling -- memory-safe for any panel density decay <- compute_ld_decay(   geno         = ldx_geno,   snp_info     = ldx_snp_info,   sampling     = "random",   r2_threshold = "both",   fit_model    = "loess",   n_pairs      = 5000L,   verbose      = FALSE ) decay$critical_r2_param    # background kinship-induced LD #>        95%  #> 0.03718883  decay$critical_r2_fixed    # standard 0.1 threshold #> [1] 0.1 decay$decay_dist           # per-chromosome decay distances #>   CHR decay_dist_bp decay_dist_kb threshold_used r2_col_used censored #> 1   1         39181         39.18     0.03718883    r2_loess    FALSE #> 2   2         50902         50.90     0.03718883    r2_loess    FALSE #> 3   3         29560         29.56     0.03718883    r2_loess    FALSE  # Sliding window (TASSEL approach) + Hill-Weir model decay_hw <- compute_ld_decay(   geno         = ldx_geno,   snp_info     = ldx_snp_info,   sampling     = "sliding_window",   window_snps  = 50L,   r2_threshold = 0.1,   fit_model    = "nonlinear",   verbose      = FALSE )  # WGS backend -- only sampled columns are loaded per chromosome if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ be <- read_geno("my_wgs.vcf.gz") decay_wgs <- compute_ld_decay(   geno         = be,   sampling     = "random",   n_pairs      = 50000L,   max_dist     = 5000000L,   r2_threshold = "both",   fit_model    = "loess",   n_threads    = 8L ) close_backend(be) } # }  # Pass to define_qtl_regions for chromosome-specific candidate windows data(ldx_blocks, package = "LDxBlocks") data(ldx_gwas,   package = "LDxBlocks") qtl <- define_qtl_regions(ldx_gwas, ldx_blocks, ldx_snp_info,                           ld_decay    = decay,                           p_threshold = NULL,                           trait_col   = "trait") qtl[, c("block_id", "lead_snp", "candidate_region_start",          "candidate_region_end", "candidate_region_size_kb")] #>                block_id lead_snp candidate_region_start candidate_region_end #> 1    block_1_1000_25027   rs1005                      0                44369 #> 2   block_1_81064_99022   rs1048                  58031               136393 #> 3 block_1_155368_179371   rs1070                 129829               208191 #> 4    block_2_1000_30023   rs2004                      0                55303 #> 5  block_2_86236_105290   rs2050                  49548               151352 #> 6    block_3_1000_19068   rs3004                      0                33383 #>   candidate_region_size_kb #> 1                     78.4 #> 2                     78.4 #> 3                     78.4 #> 4                    101.8 #> 5                    101.8 #> 6                     59.1 # }

what: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
title: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
text: LD block, computes local GEBV (haplotype effect) every individual summing per-SNP additive effects alleles carry within block: $$\text{local GEBV}_f = \sum_{t \f} \left[ h_t \alpha_{1t} +   (1-h_t)\alpha_{0t} \right]$$ \(h_t \\{0, 0.5, 1\}\) allele dosage SNP \(t\) scaled [0,1], \(\alpha_{1t}\) ALT allele effect, \(\alpha_{0t} = -\alpha_{1t}\) REF allele effect (symmetry additive model). Blocks ranked Var(local GEBV) – blocks high variance contribute strongly trait differences among individuals likely harbour causal loci (Tong et al. 2025).

title: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
code: compute_local_gebv(geno_matrix, snp_info, blocks, snp_effects, scale = TRUE)

title: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
text: geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs), values 0/1/2/NA. snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS. blocks Block table run_Big_LD_all_chr. snp_effects Named numeric vector per-SNP additive effects backsolve_snp_effects marker model directly. scale Logical. TRUE (default), scale Var(local GEBV) [0,1] blocks comparable across traits datasets.

title: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
text: list two elements: local_gebv Numeric matrix (individuals x blocks) per-block     local GEBV values. block_importance Data frame one row per block: block_id,     CHR, start_bp, end_bp, n_snps, var_local_gebv, var_scaled, important     (logical: scaled variance >= 0.9).

title: Compute Local Haplotype GEBV per Block (Tong et al. 2025) — compute_local_gebv
text: Tong J et al. (2025). Haplotype stacking improve stability stripe rust resistance wheat. Theoretical Applied Genetics 138:267. doi:10.1007/s00122-025-05045-0

title: Compute Standard r^2 LD Matrix — compute_r2
text: X NumericMatrix (individuals x SNPs). Values 0/1/2. NA allowed. digits Integer. Rounding decimal places. -1 (default) = none. n_threads Integer. OpenMP threads. Default 1.

title: Compute Standard r^2 LD Matrix — compute_r2
text: Symmetric p x p NumericMatrix, diagonal 0, values [0, 1].

what: When to use r^2 vs rV^2
title: Compute Standard r^2 LD Matrix — compute_r2
text: r^2 Use large unstructured datasets (> 500 k markers), random     mating populations, whenever speed matters. standard estimator     inflated related populations (.e. -estimate LD)     usually leads slightly conservative (larger) blocks rather     catastrophically wrong ones. rV^2 Use highly structured / related populations (livestock,     inbred lines, family-based human cohorts) kinship inflation     meaningfully distort block boundaries. Requires computing     inverting GRM - prohibitive beyond ~5 k individuals.

title: Compute Standard r^2 LD Matrix — compute_r2
code: set.seed(1) G <- matrix(sample(0:2, 80 * 30, replace = TRUE), 80, 30) r2 <- compute_r2(G, digits = 6L) range(r2)   # [0, 1] #> [1] 0.000000 0.117788

title: Decode Haplotype Strings to Nucleotide Sequences — decode_haplotype_strings
text: data frame columns: block_id Block identifier. CHR Chromosome. start_bp, end_bp Block boundaries. hap_rank Rank frequency (1 = common). hap_id Column name appears feature matrix. dosage_string Raw dosage string e.g. "02110". nucleotide_sequence Decoded nucleotide string e.g. "AGTT?". frequency Observed frequency across non-missing individuals. n_carriers Number individuals carrying haplotype (dosage > 0). snp_positions Semicolon-separated CHR:POS SNP block. snp_alleles Semicolon-separated REF/ALT SNP.

title: Decode Haplotype Strings to Nucleotide Sequences — decode_haplotype_strings
text: character haplotype string dosage one SNP block: "0" = homozygous REF  -> REF nucleotide (e.g. ) "1" = heterozygous    -> IUPAC ambiguity code (e.g. R /G) "2" = homozygous ALT  -> ALT nucleotide (e.g. G) "." = missing         -> N result data frame one row per unique haplotype allele per block, showing nucleotide sequence, frequency, REF/ALT SNP position. interpretable representation haplotype allele actually encodes biologically.

title: Decode Haplotype Strings to Nucleotide Sequences — decode_haplotype_strings
code: data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, package = "LDxBlocks") haps <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, min_snps = 3) decoded <- decode_haplotype_strings(haps, ldx_snp_info) head(decoded[, c("block_id","hap_rank","dosage_string",                   "nucleotide_sequence","frequency")]) #>             block_id hap_rank             dosage_string #> 1 block_1_1000_25027        1 1011121012111011210020110 #> 2 block_1_1000_25027        2 0111021121110121201121101 #> 3 block_1_1000_25027        3 0010010011000010101111000 #> 4 block_1_1000_25027        4 2022222002222002220020220 #> 5 block_1_1000_25027        5 1122122111221112211121211 #> 6 block_1_1000_25027        6 0000020022000020200020000 #>         nucleotide_sequence frequency #> 1 SCWRRTYAKTSSWARYCMGACCWRG      0.22 #> 2 GSWRGTYMTWSSTRAYCAKWCSWAR      0.14 #> 3 GCWAGYTAKWGCTARCYAKWSSTAG      0.12 #> 4 CCAGATCAGTCGAAGTCCGACCAGG      0.12 #> 5 SSAGRTCMKWCGWRRTCMKWCSARR      0.11 #> 6 GCTAGTTATTGCTAACCAGACCTAG      0.10

what: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
title: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
text: Maps significant GWAS markers onto LD blocks define QTL   regions. Blocks significant markers multiple traits flagged   pleiotropic. Implements approach Tong et al. (2024).

title: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
code: define_qtl_regions(   gwas_results,   blocks,   snp_info,   p_threshold = 5e-08,   trait_col = "trait",   min_snps = 3L,   ld_decay = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
text: gwas_results Data frame columns SNP (Marker), CHR, POS. Optional columns: P (p-value), BETA (additive effect estimate), trait. blocks LD block data frame run_Big_LD_all_chr. snp_info Full SNP metadata data frame. p_threshold Significance threshold. Default 5e-8. NULL uses markers regardless p-value. trait_col Trait column name gwas_results. Default "trait". min_snps Minimum SNPs per block. Default 3L. ld_decay Optional LDxBlocks_decay object compute_ld_decay, data frame columns CHR decay_dist_bp. supplied, candidate gene windows extended chromosome-specific decay distance sides lead SNP position, adding columns candidate_region_start, candidate_region_end, candidate_region_size_kb output. Default NULL. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
text: Data frame one row per block containing significant markers:   block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp,   n_snps_block, n_sig_markers, lead_snp,   lead_p, lead_beta (BETA supplied), sig_snps,   sig_betas (BETA supplied), traits, n_traits,   pleiotropic.

title: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
text: multiple SNPs within block GWAS-significant, marginal BETA values correlated (due LD) summed directly. LDxBlocks returns three complementary columns BETA present gwas_results: lead_beta BETA lead (lowest-p) SNP. simplest     proxy block effect – assumes lead SNP fully tags     causal variant. Underestimates block effect multiple     independent signals exist. sig_snps Semicolon-separated IDs significant SNPs     block. Pass conditional/joint analysis tool (e.g.     COJO, SuSiE, finemap) obtain independent within-block effects. sig_betas Semicolon-separated marginal BETA values     significant SNPs (order sig_snps). absolute     values upper bound true block effect     include LD-induced inflation; use lead_beta joint analysis     calibrated estimates. biologically correct block-level effect, fit haplotype model: build_haplotype_feature_matrix() produces 0/1/2 dosage columns whose regression coefficients (alpha_h) true per-allele effects.

title: Map GWAS Hits to LD Blocks (Post-GWAS QTL Region Definition) — define_qtl_regions
text: Tong J, Tarekegn ZT, Jambuthenne D, Alahmad S, Periyannan S, Hickey L, Dinglasan E, Hayes B (2024). Stacking beneficial haplotypes Vavilov wheat collection accelerate breeding multiple disease resistance. Theoretical Applied Genetics 137:274. doi:10.1007/s00122-024-04784-w Yang J et al. (2012). Conditional joint multiple-SNP analysis GWAS summary statistics identifies additional variants influencing complex traits. Nature Genetics 44(4):369-375. doi:10.1038/ng.2213

what: Estimate Diplotype Effects and Dominance Deviations Per LD Block — estimate_diplotype_effects
title: Estimate Diplotype Effects and Dominance Deviations Per LD Block — estimate_diplotype_effects
text: Fits per-block diplotype model decompose phenotypic variation additive dominance components LD block, correcting population structure kinship via haplotype GRM. function provides direct evidence non-additive gene action: dominance, overdominance, heterosis. Method: LD block, unique diplotypes (canonical haplotype-pair strings inferred infer_block_haplotypes, e.g. "010/110") treated levels fixed factor. mixed model haplotype GRM kinship random effect fitted via rrBLUP::mixed.solve() obtain de-regressed phenotype residuals. Diplotype class means computed residuals. pair common alleles (, B), classical quantitative genetics parameters derived three diplotype class means: Additive effect: \(= (\bar{y}_{BB} - \bar{y}_{AA}) / 2\) Dominance deviation: \(d = \bar{y}_{AB} -     (\bar{y}_{AA} + \bar{y}_{BB}) / 2\) Dominance ratio: \(d/\) - key quantity interpreting     gene action (see d_over_a column description).

title: Estimate Diplotype Effects and Dominance Deviations Per LD Block — estimate_diplotype_effects
code: estimate_diplotype_effects(   haplotypes,   blues,   blocks,   min_freq = 0.05,   min_n_diplotype = 3L,   id_col = "id",   blue_col = "blue",   blue_cols = NULL,   sig_threshold = 0.05,   sig_metric = c("p_omnibus_adj", "p_omnibus_fdr", "p_omnibus_simplem",     "p_omnibus_simplem_sidak"),   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   meff_max_cols = 1000L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Estimate Diplotype Effects and Dominance Deviations Per LD Block — estimate_diplotype_effects
text: haplotypes Named list produced extract_haplotypes. Phase-ambiguous diplotypes (unphased heterozygotes) excluded dominance analysis included omnibus F-test diplotype string unambiguous. accurate dominance decomposition, phased input (read_phased_vcf, phase_with_beagle, via phase_with_beagle) strongly recommended. blues Pre-adjusted phenotype means. Accepts four formats test_block_haplotypes: named numeric vector, single-trait data frame, multi-trait data frame, named list. formats require individual IDs matching names haplotype strings. blocks LD block table run_Big_LD_all_chr. Used supply block coordinate metadata (CHR, start_bp, end_bp) output tables. min_freq Numeric (0, 1). Minimum allele frequency. Alleles threshold full panel excluded dominance decomposition (dominance_table) diplotypes still contribute diplotype_means omnibus_tests sufficient individuals carry . Default 0.05. min_n_diplotype Integer. Minimum number individuals must carry given diplotype class included diplotype_means dominance decomposition. Diplotype classes fewer individuals silently excluded. omnibus F-test uses retained classes. Default 3L. Increasing 5-10 improves mean estimate stability small panels; decreasing 3 recommended. id_col Character. Name individual-ID column blues data frame. Default "id". blue_col Character. Name phenotype column single-trait data frames. Default "blue". blue_cols Character vector. Phenotype column names multi-trait data frames. Default NULL. sig_threshold Numeric (0, 1]. Significance cutoff applied p-value chosen sig_metric. Default 0.05. sig_metric Character. p-value drives significant flag $omnibus_tests. One : "p_omnibus_adj" (default) - plain Bonferroni:     \(\min(p \times n_{blocks},\,1)\), computed per trait.     Retained backward compatibility. "p_omnibus_fdr" - Benjamini-Hochberg FDR per trait. "p_omnibus_simplem" - simpleM Bonferroni-style:     \(\min(p \times M_{\mathrm{eff}},\,1)\),     \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\) estimated eigenspectrum     block-summary PC1 matrix. "p_omnibus_simplem_sidak" - simpleM Sidak-style:     \(1-(1-p)^{M_{\mathrm{eff}}}\). Recommended: consistent     test_block_haplotypes. four p-value columns always present $omnibus_tests regardless choice. meff_percent_cut Numeric (0, 1). Proportion variance explained retained PCs simpleM eigendecomposition used estimate \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\) sig_metric = "p_omnibus_simplem" "p_omnibus_simplem_sidak". Default 0.995 (99.5%), following original simpleM recommendation (Gao et al. 2008). meff_max_cols Integer. Maximum columns per eigendecomposition chunk estimating \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\). Default 1000L. verbose Logical. TRUE (default) prints progress per trait.

title: Estimate Diplotype Effects and Dominance Deviations Per LD Block — estimate_diplotype_effects
text: named list class c("LDxBlocks_diplotype", "list")   three elements: diplotype_means Data frame. One row per diplotype class per     LD block per trait, diplotype classes >= min_n_diplotype     individuals. Contains 9 columns: block_id (character) - Block identifier. CHR (character) - Chromosome. start_bp, end_bp (integer) - Block coordinates. trait (character) - Trait name. diplotype (character) - Canonical diplotype string: two         haplotype allele strings sorted alphabetically joined         "/", e.g. "010/110". Homozygotes identical         halves: "010/010". format ensures "010/110"         "110/010" always represented identically. n_class (integer) - Number individuals carrying         specific diplotype combination. n_total (integer) - Total individuals non-missing         data block (sum n_class across diplotype classes). mean_blue (numeric) - Mean de-regressed phenotype value         diplotype class (residual scale GRM correction). se_mean (numeric) - Standard error mean         (sd / sqrt(n_class)). dominance_table Data frame. One row per ordered allele pair     (, B) per block per trait, pairs three diplotype classes     (AA, AB, BB) >= min_n_diplotype individuals.     Contains 14 columns: block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp,         trait - . allele_A, allele_B (character) - two alleles         compared (alphabetically ordered: comes B). mean_AA, mean_AB, mean_BB (numeric) -         Diplotype class means de-regressed phenotype scale. (numeric) - Additive effect:         \((\bar{y}_{BB} - \bar{y}_{AA}) / 2\). Positive means allele B         increases trait value relative . d (numeric) - Dominance deviation:         \(\bar{y}_{AB} - (\bar{y}_{AA} + \bar{y}_{BB}) / 2\).         Positive: heterozygote advantage (overdominance \(|d| > ||\)).         Negative: heterozygote disadvantage (underdominance). d_over_a (numeric NA) - Dominance ratio         \(d/\). Interpretation: 0 = purely additive gene action;         \(\pm 0.5\) = partial dominance; \(\pm 1\) = complete dominance         (heterozygote equal one homozygote); \(|d/| > 1\) = overdominance         (heterozygote exceeds homozygotes - evidence heterosis         locus). NA \(|| < 10^{-10}\) (additive         variation homozygotes). overdominance (logical) - TRUE         \(|d/| > 1\) d_over_a NA. omnibus_tests Data frame. One row per LD block per trait,     blocks least min_n_diplotype individuals carry     least 2 distinct diplotype classes. Contains 9 columns: block_id, trait - . n_diplotypes (integer) - Number diplotype classes         >= min_n_diplotype individuals, used factor levels. F_stat (numeric) - F-statistic one-way ANOVA         (diplotype factor) de-regressed phenotype residuals. df1 (integer) - Numerator df = n_diplotypes - 1. df2 (integer) - Denominator df = n_obs - n_diplotypes. p_omnibus (numeric, (0,1]) - Raw p-value. p_omnibus_adj (numeric, (0,1]) - Bonferroni-adjusted         across tested blocks per trait. significant (logical) - TRUE         p_omnibus_adj < 0.05. Sorted ascending p_omnibus.

title: Estimate Diplotype Effects and Dominance Deviations Per LD Block — estimate_diplotype_effects
code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, ldx_blues, package = "LDxBlocks") haps <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, min_snps = 5) dip_res <- estimate_diplotype_effects(   haplotypes = haps,   blues      = setNames(ldx_blues$YLD, ldx_blues$id),   blocks     = ldx_blocks,   verbose    = FALSE ) # Overdominant blocks dip_res$dominance_table[dip_res$dominance_table$overdominance, ] #>                block_id CHR start_bp end_bp trait                  allele_A #> 1 block_1_155368_179371   1   155368 179371 trait 0100010000000000001000100 #> 2 block_2_161515_180473   2   161515 180473 trait      00100000100000000000 #>                    allele_B   mean_AA   mean_AB   mean_BB         a         d #> 1 0111011011101110001000101 -0.438808  0.135218 -0.125740  0.156534  0.417491 #> 2      00100011100000110110  0.695056 -0.011832  0.214904 -0.240076 -0.466812 #>   d_over_a overdominance #> 1   2.6671          TRUE #> 2   1.9444          TRUE # Significant diplotype effects dip_res$omnibus_tests[dip_res$omnibus_tests$significant, ] #>  [1] block_id                trait                   n_diplotypes            #>  [4] F_stat                  df1                     df2                     #>  [7] p_omnibus               p_omnibus_adj           p_omnibus_fdr           #> [10] p_omnibus_simplem       p_omnibus_simplem_sidak significant             #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) # }

what: Export Candidate Gene Regions to BED, CSV, or biomaRt Format — export_candidate_regions

title: Export Candidate Gene Regions to BED, CSV, or biomaRt Format — export_candidate_regions
text: qtl_regions Data frame define_qtl_regions. format Character. Output format: "bed", "csv", "biomart". Default "bed". out_file Character path. NULL (default), returns object invisibly without writing disk. chr_prefix Character. Prefix add chromosome names BED output (e.g. "chr" UCSC, "" Ensembl). Default "". use_lead_snp Logical. TRUE ld_decay supplied define_qtl_regions, use LD-extended candidate_region_start/candidate_region_end columns instead block boundaries. Default TRUE. padding_bp Integer. Additional bp add side region (applied LD extension ). Default 0L.

title: Export Candidate Gene Regions to BED, CSV, or biomaRt Format — export_candidate_regions

"biomart" Named list elements chromosome_name,     start, end suitable     biomaRt::getBM(filters = c("chromosome_name","start","end"), ...).

title: Export Candidate Gene Regions to BED, CSV, or biomaRt Format — export_candidate_regions
code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, ldx_gwas, package = "LDxBlocks") qtl <- define_qtl_regions(ldx_gwas, ldx_blocks, ldx_snp_info,                            p_threshold = NULL) # BED file bed <- export_candidate_regions(qtl, format = "bed", chr_prefix = "chr") head(bed) #>   chrom  start    end                  name score strand #> 1  chr1    999  25027    block_1_1000_25027     3      . #> 2  chr1  81063  99022   block_1_81064_99022     3      . #> 3  chr1 155367 179371 block_1_155368_179371     3      . #> 4  chr2    999  30023    block_2_1000_30023     4      . #> 5  chr2  86235 105290  block_2_86236_105290     3      . #> 6  chr3    999  19068    block_3_1000_19068     4      . # biomaRt-ready list bm  <- export_candidate_regions(qtl, format = "biomart") # biomaRt::getBM(attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id","external_gene_name"), #                filters = c("chromosome_name","start","end"), #                values  = bm, mart = my_mart) # }

what: Fine-Map Epistatic SNP Pairs Within a Single Block — fine_map_epistasis_block
title: Fine-Map Epistatic SNP Pairs Within a Single Block — fine_map_epistasis_block
text: Exhaustively adaptively identifies specific SNP pairs within single LD block drive block-level epistatic signal. Three methods available: "pairwise" Exhaustive C(p,2) scan. Tests pairs using     model scan_block_epistasis. Recommended     blocks p <= 200 SNPs. "lasso" Fits LASSO model main effects     pairwise interaction terms using glmnet. Identifies     interaction terms non-zero coefficients cross-validated     lambda. Recommended blocks p > 200 SNPs. "auto" Dispatches "pairwise" p <= 200,     "lasso" otherwise.

title: Fine-Map Epistatic SNP Pairs Within a Single Block — fine_map_epistasis_block
code: fine_map_epistasis_block(   block_id,   geno_matrix,   snp_info,   blocks,   y_resid,   method = c("auto", "pairwise", "lasso"),   min_freq = 0.05,   sig_threshold = 0.05,   sig_metric = c("p_simplem_sidak", "p_simplem", "p_bonf", "p_fdr"),   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   lasso_nfolds = 5L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fine-Map Epistatic SNP Pairs Within a Single Block — fine_map_epistasis_block
text: block_id Character. block fine-map. geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs) LDxBlocks_backend. snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS. blocks LD block table. y_resid Named numeric vector GRM-corrected REML residuals (test_block_haplotypes null model). Names must match individual IDs. method Character. One "pairwise", "lasso", "auto". Default "auto". min_freq Numeric. Minimum MAF. Default 0.05. sig_threshold Numeric. Significance threshold. Default 0.05. lasso_nfolds Integer. CV folds glmnet lambda selection. Default 5L. verbose Logical. Default TRUE.

title: Fine-Map Epistatic SNP Pairs Within a Single Block — fine_map_epistasis_block
text: data frame sorted p_wald (pairwise) |coefficient|   (lasso). Columns: SNP_i, SNP_j, POS_i,   POS_j, dist_bp, aa_effect lasso_coef,   SE, t_stat, p_wald, p_bonf,   significant (pairwise) selected (lasso).

what: Harmonize Haplotype Allele Labels Across Panels or Analysis Runs — harmonize_haplotypes
title: Harmonize Haplotype Allele Labels Across Panels or Analysis Runs — harmonize_haplotypes
text: Ensures haplotype allele labels biologically comparable across different datasets, analysis runs, training/validation splits. Without harmonization, allele string "010110" one panel guaranteed correspond biological haplotype another panel block boundaries, SNP ordering, allele encoding differ runs. function anchors allele identity reference dictionary built training/reference panel. New (target) haplotypes matched dictionary: Exact match: allele string exists verbatim     reference dictionary -> labelled reference allele label. Nearest-Hamming match: exact match -> labelled     similar reference allele (minimum Hamming distance).     minimum Hamming distance exceeds max_hamming, allele     labelled "<novel>". Novel: distance > max_hamming -> "<novel>".

title: Harmonize Haplotype Allele Labels Across Panels or Analysis Runs — harmonize_haplotypes
code: harmonize_haplotypes(   haplotypes_target,   haplotypes_ref,   min_freq_ref = 0.02,   max_hamming = NULL,   missing_string = "." )

title: Harmonize Haplotype Allele Labels Across Panels or Analysis Runs — harmonize_haplotypes
text: haplotypes_target Named list extract_haplotypes (panel harmonize - validation set, new environment, etc.). haplotypes_ref Named list extract_haplotypes (reference panel - training set, base population, etc.). Must cover blocks haplotypes_target (extra blocks either panel silently skipped). min_freq_ref Numeric. alleles frequency reference panel form dictionary. Default 0.02. max_hamming Integer. Maximum Hamming distance nearest-neighbour match; alleles beyond distance labelled "<novel>". Default NULL (limit - always assigns nearest reference allele). missing_string Character. Missing data placeholder. Default ".".

title: Harmonize Haplotype Allele Labels Across Panels or Analysis Runs — harmonize_haplotypes
text: Named list structure haplotypes_target,   allele strings replaced reference-anchored equivalents.   block_info attribute haplotypes_target preserved.   harmonization_report attribute attached: data frame   one row per block reporting n_exact, n_nearest,   n_novel, mean_hamming_dist matched alleles.

title: Harmonize Haplotype Allele Labels Across Panels or Analysis Runs — harmonize_haplotypes
code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, package = "LDxBlocks") # Split into training (70 pct) and validation (30 pct) n    <- nrow(ldx_geno) idx  <- sample(n) ref_geno  <- ldx_geno[idx[1:round(n*0.7)], ] tgt_geno  <- ldx_geno[idx[(round(n*0.7)+1):n], ] haps_ref  <- extract_haplotypes(ref_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) haps_tgt  <- extract_haplotypes(tgt_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) haps_harm <- harmonize_haplotypes(haps_tgt, haps_ref) attr(haps_harm, "harmonization_report") #>                                    block_id n_exact n_nearest n_novel #> block_1_1000_25027       block_1_1000_25027      24        12       0 #> block_1_81064_99022     block_1_81064_99022      32         4       0 #> block_1_155368_179371 block_1_155368_179371      31         5       0 #> block_2_1000_30023       block_2_1000_30023      28         8       0 #> block_2_86236_105290   block_2_86236_105290      31         5       0 #> block_2_161515_180473 block_2_161515_180473      33         3       0 #> block_3_1000_19068       block_3_1000_19068      30         6       0 #> block_3_74532_93854     block_3_74532_93854      33         3       0 #> block_3_149647_168376 block_3_149647_168376      31         5       0 #>                       mean_hamming_dist #> block_1_1000_25027             4.000000 #> block_1_81064_99022            1.000000 #> block_1_155368_179371          1.400000 #> block_2_1000_30023             4.250000 #> block_2_86236_105290           1.000000 #> block_2_161515_180473          1.000000 #> block_3_1000_19068             1.166667 #> block_3_74532_93854            1.000000 #> block_3_149647_168376          1.000000 # }

what: Infer Structured Block-Level Diplotypes Per Individual — infer_block_haplotypes
title: Infer Structured Block-Level Diplotypes Per Individual — infer_block_haplotypes
text: Converts raw haplotype strings produced extract_haplotypes structured per-individual, per-block diplotype table explicit hap1 / hap2 gamete alleles, diplotype code, heterozygosity flag, phase-ambiguity flag. input phased list (read_phased_vcf pre-phased VCF via read_phased_vcf), hap1 hap2 true gametic strings phase_ambiguous always FALSE. input unphased (diploid allele strings), heterozygous individuals two possible phase assignments. function flags phase_ambiguous = TRUE; hap1 / hap2 set NA individuals unless resolve_unphased = TRUE, case -frequent gametic split consistent observed allele frequencies imputed (maximum-parsimony heuristic - statistically rigorous, use phase_with_beagle rigorous phasing).

title: Infer Structured Block-Level Diplotypes Per Individual — infer_block_haplotypes
code: infer_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes,   resolve_unphased = FALSE,   missing_string = "." )

title: Infer Structured Block-Level Diplotypes Per Individual — infer_block_haplotypes
text: haplotypes Named list extract_haplotypes. produced phased list input, strings contain "|" separators (e.g. "011|100"). unphased, strings plain diploid allele codes (e.g. "012201"). resolve_unphased Logical. unphased heterozygous genotypes, impute parsimonious hap1/hap2 split using population allele frequencies (parsimony heuristic). Default FALSE (leave NA). missing_string Character. Missing data placeholder. Default ".".

title: Infer Structured Block-Level Diplotypes Per Individual — infer_block_haplotypes
text: Data frame one row per individual x block combination: block_id Block identifier. CHR, start_bp, end_bp Block coordinates. id Individual ID. hap1 Gamete 1 allele string (NA unresolvable). hap2 Gamete 2 allele string (NA unresolvable). diplotype Canonical diplotype code: two gamete alleles     sorted alphabetically joined "/", e.g.     "010/110". heterozygous Logical; TRUE individual     biologically heterozygous. phased input: hap1 != hap2.     unphased input: dosage position equals 1, regardless     whether phase resolved. phase_ambiguous Logical; TRUE unphased     heterozygous individuals resolve_unphased = FALSE.     individuals heterozygous = TRUE     hap1 = hap2 = NA. missing Logical; TRUE allele string     contains missing placeholder.

title: Infer Structured Block-Level Diplotypes Per Individual — infer_block_haplotypes
code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, package = "LDxBlocks") haps <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) dip  <- infer_block_haplotypes(haps) # Proportion of heterozygous diplotypes per block tapply(dip$heterozygous, dip$block_id, mean, na.rm = TRUE) #>    block_1_1000_25027 block_1_155368_179371   block_1_81064_99022  #>             0.7500000             0.7833333             0.6916667  #>    block_2_1000_30023 block_2_161515_180473  block_2_86236_105290  #>             0.7750000             0.7916667             0.7166667  #>    block_3_1000_19068 block_3_149647_168376   block_3_74532_93854  #>             0.7583333             0.8000000             0.7000000  # }

text: qtl_regions Data frame define_qtl_regions. pred_result List run_haplotype_prediction. diversity Data frame compute_haplotype_diversity. Optional. supplied, adds sweep_flag output. He_threshold Minimum expected heterozygosity flag block sufficiently diverse haplotype stacking. Default 0.3.

title: Integrate GWAS QTL Regions with Haplotype Prediction Results — integrate_gwas_haplotypes
text: Data frame one row per block appears least one   input sources, columns:   block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp,   n_snps, has_gwas_hit (logical),   lead_snp, lead_p, lead_beta,   n_sig_markers, is_important (logical, scaled var >= 0.9),   var_scaled, , sweep_flag,   is_diverse (logical, >= He_threshold),   priority_score (0-3),   recommendation (character label).   Sorted priority_score descending, var_scaled   descending.

title: Integrate GWAS QTL Regions with Haplotype Prediction Results — integrate_gwas_haplotypes
text: Three complementary evidence layers combined per block: Biological (GWAS) block contain genome-wide significant     marker? Sourced define_qtl_regions(). Statistical (variance) block explain substantial variance     trait? Blocks scaled Var(local GEBV) >= 0.9     flagged important run_haplotype_prediction(). Diversity () block enough haplotype diversity     stack favourable alleles? Sourced     compute_haplotype_diversity(). output priority_score sum binary flags layer (0-3). Blocks scoring 3 supported three lines evidence strongest candidates haplotype stacking. Blocks scoring 2 worth investigating. Blocks scoring 1 0 require caution.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # 1. GWAS integration gwas   <- read.csv("gwas_results.csv")   # SNP, CHR, POS, P, BETA qtl    <- define_qtl_regions(gwas, blocks, snp_info, p_threshold = 5e-8)  # 2. Haplotype prediction blues  <- read.csv("blues.csv") pred   <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks,                                     blues    = blues,                                     id_col   = "id",                                     blue_col = "YLD")  # 3. Haplotype diversity haps   <- extract_haplotypes(geno, snp_info, blocks, min_snps = 3) div    <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)  # 4. Integrate all three priority <- integrate_gwas_haplotypes(qtl, pred, diversity = div)  # Top priority blocks for haplotype stacking priority[priority$priority_score == 3, ] } # }

what: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
title: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
text: Fast, scalable linkage disequilibrium (LD) block detection genome-wide SNP data using C++/Armadillo computational core two LD metrics: standard r^2 kinship-adjusted rV^2. Includes haplotype reconstruction, diversity metrics, prediction feature matrices, multi-format genotype /O.

title: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
text: r^2 (method = "r2") Standard squared Pearson correlation.     kinship matrix required. C++ kernel via RcppArmadillo + OpenMP.     Suitable unstructured populations large datasets (10 M+ markers). rV^2 (method = "rV2") Kinship-whitened squared correlation     (Mangin et al. 2012, Heredity 108:285-291). Corrects LD inflation relatedness. livestock,     inbred lines, family-based cohorts < 200 k markers per chromosome.

title: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
text: Read genotype data: read_geno – auto-detects CSV,     HapMap, VCF, GDS, BED, plain matrix. WGS panels peak RAM     concern, use read_geno_bigmemory build     memory-mapped store (requires bigmemory). Detect LD blocks chromosome-wise: run_Big_LD_all_chr. Optionally auto-tune parameters: tune_LD_params. Reconstruct haplotypes: extract_haplotypes. Decode haplotype strings nucleotides:     decode_haplotype_strings. Compute diversity metrics: compute_haplotype_diversity. Build prediction feature matrix:     build_haplotype_feature_matrix. Compute LD decay chromosome-specific decay distances:     compute_ld_decay. Provides critical r\(^2\)     threshold (parametric: 95th percentile unlinked-marker r\(^2\))     per-chromosome decay distances candidate gene windows. Map GWAS hits QTL regions (LD-aware windows     ld_decay supplied): define_qtl_regions. Genomic prediction (Tong et al. 2024/2025):     run_haplotype_prediction – full pipeline BLUEs     block importance; step--step via     compute_haplotype_grm,     backsolve_snp_effects,     compute_local_gebv,     rank_haplotype_blocks. Haplotype association testing simpleM correction     (Gao et al. 2008, 2010, 2011): test_block_haplotypes.     sig_metric selects among p_wald, p_fdr,     p_simplem (Bonferroni-style), p_simplem_sidak     (Sidak-style, recommended). four p-value columns always present.     meff_scope controls Meff estimation scope. Cross-population GWAS validation - two functions depending     association analysis performed: compare_block_effects - results         test_block_haplotypes. IVW meta-analysis, Cochran Q,         -squared, direction agreement per block. compare_gwas_effects - results external         GWAS tools (GAPIT, TASSEL, FarmCPU, PLINK, etc.). Accepts raw GWAS         data frames define_qtl_regions output. Derives SE         z-score absent. functions support block_match = "position" match         LD blocks genomic interval overlap (Intersection--Union) rather         block_id string equality. handles common case         block boundaries differ populations due different         ancestral LD structures. overlap_min (default 0.50) sets         minimum IoU valid positional match. match_type output         column reports "exact", "position",         "pop1_only" every block. Write outputs: write_haplotype_numeric,     write_haplotype_character,     write_haplotype_diversity. run everything : run_ldx_pipeline.

title: LDxBlocks: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection and Haplotype Analysis — LDxBlocks-package
text: ldx_geno 120 x 230 genotype matrix, 3 chromosomes,     9 simulated LD blocks. ldx_snp_info SNP metadata (SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT). ldx_blocks Reference block table ldx_geno. ldx_gwas 20 toy GWAS markers tuning demos. ldx_blues Pre-adjusted BLUEs (id, YLD, RES)     run_haplotype_prediction demos. Available .rda     dataset inst/extdata/example_blues.csv.

SNP ID block start. end.rsID Character. SNP ID block end. start.bp Integer. Base-pair position block start. end.bp Integer. Base-pair position block end. CHR Character. Chromosome label. length_bp Integer. end.bp - start.bp + 1. Physical     span block base pairs. n_snps Integer.
