title: Example LD Block Table — ldx_blocks
code: data(ldx_blocks) ldx_blocks #>   start end start.rsID end.rsID start.bp end.bp CHR length_bp n_snps #> 1     1  25     rs1001   rs1025     1000  25027   1     24028     25 #> 2    31  50     rs1031   rs1050    81064  99022   1     17959     20 #> 3    56  80     rs1056   rs1080   155368 179371   1     24004     25 #> 4    81 110     rs2001   rs2030     1000  30023   2     29024     30 #> 5   116 135     rs2036   rs2055    86236 105290   2     19055     20 #> 6   141 160     rs2061   rs2080   161515 180473   2     18959     20 #> 7   161 180     rs3001   rs3020     1000  19068   3     18069     20 #> 8   186 205     rs3026   rs3045    74532  93854   3     19323     20 #> 9   211 230     rs3051   rs3070   149647 168376   3     18730     20 summarise_blocks(ldx_blocks) #>      CHR n_blocks min_bp median_bp  mean_bp max_bp total_bp_covered #> 1      1        3  17959     24004 21997.00  24028            65991 #> 2      2        3  18959     19055 22346.00  29024            67038 #> 3      3        3  18069     18730 18707.33  19323            56122 #> 4 GENOME        9  17959     19055 21016.78  29024           189151  # \donttest{ # Compare haplotype allele frequencies between two sample groups data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info) haps <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) ids  <- rownames(ldx_geno) cmp  <- compare_haplotype_populations(   haplotypes  = haps,   group1      = ids[1:60],   group2      = ids[61:120],   group1_name = "cycle1",   group2_name = "cycle2" ) head(cmp[, c("block_id","n1","n2","FST","divergent")]) #>                block_id n1 n2    FST divergent #> 1    block_1_1000_25027 60 60 0.0096     FALSE #> 2   block_1_81064_99022 60 60 0.0068     FALSE #> 3 block_1_155368_179371 60 60 0.0072     FALSE #> 4    block_2_1000_30023 60 60 0.0046     FALSE #> 5  block_2_86236_105290 60 60 0.0034     FALSE #> 6 block_2_161515_180473 60 60 0.0099     FALSE  # Cross-population haplotype effect concordance. # test_block_haplotypes() produces LDxBlocks_haplotype_assoc objects; # compare_block_effects() compares their allele effects block by block. data(ldx_blues) blues_vec <- setNames(ldx_blues$YLD, ldx_blues$id) # Simulate two populations by splitting the 120 individuals set.seed(2L) idx1 <- sample(ids, 70L);  idx2 <- setdiff(ids, idx1) haps1 <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno[idx1, ], ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) haps2 <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno[idx2, ], ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) assoc1 <- test_block_haplotypes(haps1, blues = blues_vec[idx1],                                  blocks = ldx_blocks, n_pcs = 0L,                                  verbose = FALSE) assoc2 <- test_block_haplotypes(haps2, blues = blues_vec[idx2],                                  blocks = ldx_blocks, n_pcs = 0L,                                  verbose = FALSE) # block_match = "position" handles different block boundaries; # use "id" (default) when both populations share the same block table. conc <- compare_block_effects(   assoc1, assoc2,   pop1_name   = "Pop1",   pop2_name   = "Pop2",   blocks_pop1 = ldx_blocks,   blocks_pop2 = ldx_blocks,   block_match = "id",   verbose     = FALSE ) conc$concordance[, c("block_id","n_shared_alleles",                      "direction_agreement","meta_p","replicated")] #>                block_id n_shared_alleles direction_agreement    meta_p #> 1    block_1_1000_25027                7              0.4286 0.7964556 #> 2   block_1_81064_99022                5              0.4000 0.7000451 #> 3 block_1_155368_179371                7              0.4286 0.8408251 #> 4    block_2_1000_30023                7              0.5714 0.6899425 #> 5  block_2_86236_105290                7              0.4286 0.7257983 #> 6 block_2_161515_180473                6              0.6667 0.7767358 #> 7    block_3_1000_19068                7              0.8571 0.5867187 #> 8   block_3_74532_93854                6              0.5000 0.8366144 #> 9 block_3_149647_168376                8              0.5000 0.9410410 #>   replicated #> 1      FALSE #> 2      FALSE #> 3      FALSE #> 4      FALSE #> 5      FALSE #> 6      FALSE #> 7       TRUE #> 8      FALSE #> 9      FALSE # }

code: blues_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_blues.csv",                           package = "LDxBlocks") blues <- read.csv(blues_file) head(blues) #>       id     YLD     RES #> 1 ind001 -0.5175  0.6771 #> 2 ind002  0.7635  1.3764 #> 3 ind003 -1.3093 -0.9946 #> 4 ind004 -1.1162 -1.4089 #> 5 ind005  1.1343 -0.5120 #> 6 ind006  0.9307 -0.4573  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single-trait prediction (YLD) data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) res <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = ldx_geno,   snp_info    = ldx_snp_info,   blocks      = ldx_blocks,   blues       = blues,   id_col      = "id",   blue_col    = "YLD" ) res$block_importance[res$block_importance$important, ]  # Multi-trait prediction (YLD + RES simultaneously) res_mt <- run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix     = ldx_geno,   snp_info        = ldx_snp_info,   blocks          = ldx_blocks,   blues           = blues,   id_col          = "id",   blue_cols       = c("YLD", "RES"),   importance_rule = "any" ) res_mt$solver_used   # 'sommer' or 'rrBLUP' res_mt$block_importance[res_mt$block_importance$important_any,   c("block_id", "var_scaled_YLD", "var_scaled_RES", "n_traits_important")] } # }  # \donttest{ # Cross-validate genomic prediction accuracy data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) blues_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_blues.csv",                           package = "LDxBlocks") blues <- read.csv(blues_file) cv <- cv_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix = ldx_geno,   snp_info    = ldx_snp_info,   blocks      = ldx_blocks,   blues       = blues,   k           = 3L,   id_col      = "id",   blue_col    = "YLD",   verbose     = FALSE ) cv$pa_mean #>   trait         PA     RMSE     PA_sd    RMSE_sd #> 1   YLD 0.08409993 1.073279 0.1879066 0.03967989 # }

title: Per-Environment BLUEs for Stability Analysis Examples — ldx_blues_list
code: data(ldx_blues_list) names(ldx_blues_list)          # "env1" "env2" #> [1] "env1" "env2" length(ldx_blues_list$env1)    # 120 #> [1] 120 head(ldx_blues_list$env1) #>  ind001  ind002  ind003  ind004  ind005  ind006  #> -0.3923  0.6664 -1.4078 -1.4531  1.3324  1.1296   # Mean BLUE difference between environments (expected ~0.4) mean(ldx_blues_list$env2 - ldx_blues_list$env1) #> [1] 0.3960925  # \donttest{ # Finlay-Wilkinson stability analysis across two environments data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) stab <- run_haplotype_stability(   geno_matrix = ldx_geno,   snp_info    = ldx_snp_info,   blocks      = ldx_blocks,   blues_list  = ldx_blues_list,   verbose     = FALSE ) # Stable blocks (regression slope b not significantly different from 1) head(stab[stab$stable, c("block_id","b","b_se","r2_fw")]) #> [1] block_id b        b_se     r2_fw    #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names) # }

title: Example Genotype Matrix — ldx_geno
text: numeric matrix 120 rows (individuals, named ind001   ind120) 230 columns (SNPs). SNP identifiers follow   pattern rs{CHR}{count}: chromosome 1 uses rs1001–rs1080,   chromosome 2 uses rs2001–rs2080, chromosome 3 uses   rs3001–rs3070. Values additive dosages   {0, 1, 2} missing data.

title: Example Genotype Matrix — ldx_geno
text: Simulated data-raw/generate_example_data.R   using founder-haplotype model (K = 4 founders per block).   Seed: set.seed(20250407).

title: Example Genotype Matrix — ldx_geno
text: 230 SNPs divided across three chromosomes (80, 80, 70 SNPs respectively). chromosome contains three distinct LD blocks separated short stretches (5 SNPs) low-LD singletons 50 kb inter-block gap: Chromosome 1 Three blocks 25, 20, 25 SNPs. Chromosome 2 Three blocks 30, 20, 20 SNPs. Chromosome 3 Three blocks 20, 20, 20 SNPs. Within block, SNPs generated using founder-haplotype model: K = 4 binary founder haplotypes drawn per block, individual assigned diploid combination two founders (flip rate 1%, MAF enforced >= 10% per SNP). guarantees K(K+1)/2 = 10 distinct diplotype classes per block (~12 individuals per class), ensuring sufficient diversity estimate_diplotype_effects. -block SNPs simulated independently binomial distributions randomised allele frequencies produce near-zero LD.

title: Example Genotype Matrix — ldx_geno
code: data(ldx_geno) dim(ldx_geno)           # 120 230 #> [1] 120 230 range(ldx_geno)         # 0 2 #> [1] 0 2  # Compute r^2 for the first 30 SNPs r2 <- compute_r2(ldx_geno[, 1:30]) image(r2, main = "r^2 - first 30 SNPs (chr1 block 1)")   # \donttest{ # Sliding-window diversity scan (independent of LD blocks) data(ldx_snp_info) scan <- scan_diversity_windows(   geno_matrix  = ldx_geno,   snp_info     = ldx_snp_info,   window_bp    = 50000L,   step_bp      = 25000L,   min_snps_win = 3L ) head(scan[, c("CHR","win_mid","n_snps","He","freq_dominant")]) #>   CHR win_mid n_snps     He freq_dominant #> 1   1   25999     25 0.9164        0.1833 #> 2   1   75999     25 0.9976        0.0333 #> 3   1  100999     24 0.9966        0.0333 #> 4   1  150999     26 0.9990        0.0167 #> 5   1  175999     29 0.9966        0.0250 #> 6   1  200999      4 0.8859        0.1917 # }

title: Example GWAS Marker Table — ldx_gwas
text: Twenty toy GWAS markers drawn simulated LD blocks use tune_LD_params, define_qtl_regions, compare_gwas_effects. 20 markers fall within LD blocks, nine blocks represented: chromosome 3 contributes markers first block; blocks 2 3 chromosome 3 GWAS markers. correctly parameterised run tune_LD_params return zero unassigned markers. dataset includes effect sizes (BETA, SE) can serve minimal external GWAS result compare_gwas_effects demonstrations without requiring full association analysis.

title: Example GWAS Marker Table — ldx_gwas
code: ldx_gwas

title: Example GWAS Marker Table — ldx_gwas
text: data.frame 20 rows 7 columns: Marker Character. SNP identifier, matching entries     ldx_snp_info$SNP. CHR Character. Chromosome label. POS Integer. Base-pair position. BETA Numeric. Simulated effect size. Derived     z-score P 5% random noise. Positive first     10 markers, negative last 10, providing testable directional     signal compare_gwas_effects demonstrations. SE Numeric. Standard error BETA. Derived     |BETA|/|z| inflated 10%, consistent standard     mixed-model GWAS output. P Numeric. Toy p-value. Eight markers P < 1e-6     (clearly significant), seven P (1e-5, 1e-3) (suggestive),     five P (1e-3, 0.05) (sub-threshold). trait Character. Toy trait label ("TraitA"     "TraitB"), assigned randomly 60/40 probability. Used     demonstrate multi-trait pleiotropic block detection     define_qtl_regions. single trait analysed,     column simply ignored trait_col argument omitted.

title: Example GWAS Marker Table — ldx_gwas
text: Simulated data-raw/generate_example_data.R.   Seed: set.seed(42).

title: Example GWAS Marker Table — ldx_gwas
code: data(ldx_gwas) head(ldx_gwas) #>   Marker CHR   POS   BETA     SE            P  trait #> 1 rs1001   1  1000 0.2510 0.0560 8.151737e-07 TraitA #> 2 rs1005   1  5188 0.2572 0.0558 4.023280e-07 TraitA #> 3 rs1017   1 16833 0.2425 0.0539 7.524202e-07 TraitA #> 4 rs1034   1 83635 0.2584 0.0572 6.638623e-07 TraitB #> 5 rs1040   1 89846 0.2560 0.0569 7.299685e-07 TraitA #> 6 rs1048   1 97212 0.2426 0.0524 3.493586e-07 TraitA # All markers should fall within ldx_blocks: data(ldx_blocks) data(ldx_snp_info) all(is.element(ldx_gwas$Marker, ldx_snp_info$SNP)) #> [1] TRUE  # \donttest{ # Map markers to LD blocks and identify lead SNPs per block qtl <- define_qtl_regions(   gwas_results = ldx_gwas,   blocks       = ldx_blocks,   snp_info     = ldx_snp_info,   p_threshold  = 1e-5,   verbose      = FALSE ) qtl[, c("block_id", "CHR", "lead_snp", "lead_p", "lead_beta", "n_sig_markers")] #>                block_id CHR lead_snp       lead_p lead_beta n_sig_markers #> 1    block_1_1000_25027   1   rs1005 4.023280e-07    0.2572             3 #> 2   block_1_81064_99022   1   rs1048 3.493586e-07    0.2426             3 #> 3 block_1_155368_179371   1   rs1070 3.653110e-09    0.3262             2  # Cross-population effect concordance from external GWAS results. # Here ldx_gwas is used for both populations; in practice these # would be independent GWAS runs on separate cohorts. set.seed(1L) gwas_pop2        <- ldx_gwas gwas_pop2$BETA   <- ldx_gwas$BETA * 0.8 + rnorm(20, 0, 0.02) gwas_pop2$SE     <- ldx_gwas$SE   * 0.9 + runif(20, 0, 0.005) gwas_pop2$P      <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(gwas_pop2$BETA / gwas_pop2$SE))  # Path 1: pre-mapped (recommended for auditability) qtl1 <- define_qtl_regions(ldx_gwas,  ldx_blocks, ldx_snp_info,                            p_threshold = NULL, verbose = FALSE) qtl2 <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_pop2, ldx_blocks, ldx_snp_info,                            p_threshold = NULL, verbose = FALSE) conc <- compare_gwas_effects(   qtl_pop1    = qtl1,   qtl_pop2    = qtl2,   blocks_pop1 = ldx_blocks,   blocks_pop2 = ldx_blocks,   pop1_name   = "DiscoveryPop",   pop2_name   = "ReplicationPop",   verbose     = FALSE ) conc$concordance[, c("block_id","direction_agreement","meta_p","replicated")] #>                block_id direction_agreement       meta_p replicated #> 1    block_1_1000_25027                   1 1.855521e-10       TRUE #> 2   block_1_81064_99022                   1 1.687084e-11       TRUE #> 3 block_1_155368_179371                   1 3.037882e-14       TRUE #> 4    block_2_1000_30023                   1 1.409021e-07       TRUE #> 5  block_2_86236_105290                   1 3.304721e-07       TRUE #> 6    block_3_1000_19068                   1 7.263598e-03       TRUE  # Path 2: raw GWAS + blocks (calls define_qtl_regions internally) conc2 <- compare_gwas_effects(   gwas_pop1     = ldx_gwas,   gwas_pop2     = gwas_pop2,   blocks_pop1   = ldx_blocks,   blocks_pop2   = ldx_blocks,   snp_info_pop1 = ldx_snp_info,   p_threshold   = NULL,   verbose       = FALSE ) print(conc2) #> LDxBlocks Cross-Population Effect Concordance #>   Populations: pop1 vs pop2 #>   Traits:       trait  #>   Blocks compared:           6  #>   With enough shared alleles: 6  #>   Directionally concordant:   6  #>   Replicated (dir + Q_p>0.05): 6  #>   Boundary warnings:          0 (overlap ratio < 0.8 ) #>   Shared allele comparisons:  6  # }

title: Statistical Phasing via Beagle 5.x — phase_with_beagle
text: Wrapper around Beagle 5.x statistical haplotype phasing. Calls java -jar beagle.jar writes phased VCF.gz disk. recommended phasing method LDxBlocks: Beagle performs chromosome-level statistical phasing using population-LD across markers simultaneously, producing true inferred haplotypes suitable block-level frequency estimation diversity analysis.

title: Statistical Phasing via Beagle 5.x — phase_with_beagle
code: phase_with_beagle(   input_vcf,   out_prefix,   beagle_jar = NULL,   java_path = "java",   java_mem_gb = NULL,   nthreads = 1L,   ref_panel = NULL,   map_file = NULL,   chrom = NULL,   seed = NULL,   burnin = NULL,   iterations = NULL,   window = NULL,   overlap = NULL,   beagle_args = "",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Statistical Phasing via Beagle 5.x — phase_with_beagle
text: input_vcf Path input VCF VCF.gz (unphased). out_prefix Output path prefix. Beagle appends .vcf.gz. beagle_jar Path beagle.jar. Default: searched dirname(out_prefix), standard locations. java_path Java executable. Default "java". java_mem_gb Java heap size GB (e.g. 8 sets -Xmx8g). Default NULL (uses JVM default). Increase large VCFs. nthreads Beagle threads. Default 1L. ref_panel Optional path phased reference VCF. Default NULL. map_file Optional genetic map file accurate phasing structured populations. Default NULL. chrom Optional chromosome string passed Beagle (e.g. "chr1" "1"). Default NULL (chromosomes). seed Integer random seed reproducibility. Default NULL. burnin Beagle burn-iterations. Default NULL (Beagle default). iterations Beagle phasing iterations. Default NULL (Beagle default). window Beagle window size. Default NULL (Beagle default). overlap Beagle window overlap. Default NULL (Beagle default). beagle_args Additional Beagle arguments string, space-separated. Default "". verbose Logical. Default TRUE.

title: Statistical Phasing via Beagle 5.x — phase_with_beagle
text: Invisibly returns path phased VCF.gz.   Beagle stdout stderr written out_prefix.log.

title: Statistical Phasing via Beagle 5.x — phase_with_beagle
text: Sys.("beagle.jar") typically fails find .jar   files PATH. Supply beagle_jar explicitly place   beagle.jar next out_prefix.   Download: https://faculty.washington.edu/browning/beagle/beagle.html

title: Statistical Phasing via Beagle 5.x — phase_with_beagle
text: Browning et al. (2018) J Hum Genet 103:338-348.

what: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
text: Assembles inputs required fit GBLUP model external software (rrBLUP, sommer, ASReml-R, BGLR) subsequently run Tong et al. (2025) haplotype stacking pipeline. Returns VanRaden GRM computed haplotype feature matrix, aligned user-supplied phenotype table.

title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
code: prepare_gblup_inputs(   hap_matrix,   pheno_df,   id_col = "id",   trait_col = NULL,   bend = TRUE )

title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
text: hap_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x haplotype alleles) build_haplotype_feature_matrix. pheno_df Data frame phenotypes. Must contain: ID column (set via id_col, default "id").     Values must match rownames(hap_matrix) exactly     (case-sensitive, leading/trailing spaces). One numeric trait columns (referenced via     trait_col). Column names arbitrary. NA values trait columns allowed. Minimal single-trait format: id_col Name individual ID column pheno_df. Default "id". trait_col Name trait column extract numeric vector. Default NULL – y_vec returned, aligned data frame. bend Logical. Add 0.001 diagonal G positive-definiteness. Default TRUE (recommended mixed model solvers).

title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
text: named list: G VanRaden GRM (n x n), aligned individuals present     hap_matrix pheno_df. pheno_df Phenotype data frame subset reordered match     rows G. y_vec Named numeric vector requested trait (    trait_col supplied). NA values preserved     user can decide handle (e.g. set NA     prediction-individuals training/validation split). n_train Number individuals non-missing trait values. n_predict Number individuals missing trait values     (prediction candidates).

title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
text: LDxBlocks handles genotype processing block detection. Phenotype handling GBLUP fitting intentionally left dedicated R packages phenotype data requires preprocessing (multi-environment adjustment, outlier removal, covariate inclusion) dataset-specific. handoff : LDxBlocks (function): produce aligned G matrix     phenotype vector. External GBLUP (rrBLUP / sommer / ASReml-R / BGLR):     fit model, obtain GEBV. LDxBlocks (backsolve_snp_effects +     compute_local_gebv): derive block-level haplotype effects     GEBV.

what: Example GBLUP calls after this function
title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
code: # rrBLUP library(rrBLUP) fit  <- kin.blup(data = inp$pheno_df, geno = "id",                  pheno = "trait", K = inp$G) gebv <- fit$g  # sommer library(sommer) fit  <- sommer::mmes(trait ~ 1, random = ~vsm(ism(id), Gu = inp$G),              data = inp$pheno_df) gebv <- fit$U$`u:id`$trait  # BGLR library(BGLR) fit  <- BGLR(y = inp$y_vec, ETA = list(list(K = inp$G, model = "RKHS"))) gebv <- fit$yHat

title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
text: Tong J et al. (2025). Haplotype stacking improve stability stripe rust resistance wheat. Theoretical Applied Genetics 138:267. doi:10.1007/s00122-025-05045-0 VanRaden PM (2008). Efficient methods compute genomic predictions. Journal Dairy Science 91(11):4414-4423. doi:10.3168/jds.2007-0980

title: Prepare Genomic Prediction Inputs for External GBLUP Software — prepare_gblup_inputs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # After building haplotype feature matrix: feat  <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps, top_n = 5)$matrix pheno <- read.csv("phenotypes.csv")   # columns: id, YLD, PHT, ...  inp <- prepare_gblup_inputs(feat, pheno, id_col = "id",                              trait_col = "YLD")  # Fit GBLUP with rrBLUP library(rrBLUP) fit  <- kin.blup(data = inp$pheno_df, geno = "id",                  pheno = "YLD", K = inp$G) gebv <- fit$g  # Then derive block-level haplotype effects snp_fx <- backsolve_snp_effects(geno_matrix, gebv, G = inp$G) loc    <- compute_local_gebv(geno_matrix, snp_info, blocks, snp_fx) } # }

what: Prepare Genotype Matrix for LD Computation — prepare_geno
title: Prepare Genotype Matrix for LD Computation — prepare_geno
text: Central preparation function called inside Big_LD() run_Big_LD_all_chr. Depending method: "r2" Mean-centres SNP column. kinship needed.     Returns centred matrix directly. Fast - O(np). "rV2" Computes VanRaden GRM via AGHmatrix, tunes     via ASRgenomics, inverts via Cholesky/eigen, left-multiplies     centred genotype matrix. Returns whitened matrix     whitening factor use appendSGTs.

title: Rank Haplotype Blocks by Evidence Strength — rank_haplotype_blocks
text: diversity Data frame compute_haplotype_diversity. Required three use cases. qtl_regions Optional. Data frame define_qtl_regions. supplied, blocks binary-flagged containing GWAS hit . p-value used ranking. Default NULL. pred_result Optional. List run_haplotype_prediction. supplied, blocks ranked Var(local GEBV). Default NULL. He_threshold Minimum consider block diverse enough haplotype stacking. Default 0.3. top_n_blocks Return top n blocks. Default NULL (return ).

title: Rank Haplotype Blocks by Evidence Strength — rank_haplotype_blocks

title: Rank Haplotype Blocks by Evidence Strength — rank_haplotype_blocks
text: Genotype (GWAS, phenotype): blocks ranked     haplotype diversity (, effective number alleles). Blocks     high diversity potential haplotype stacking. Genotype + GWAS (phenotype): blocks binary-flagged     whether contain GWAS-significant marker. Within     GWAS-hit group within non-hit group, blocks     ordered . p-value used ranking – marker either     crosses significance threshold . Genotype + phenotype (? GWAS): blocks ranked scaled     Var(local GEBV) run_haplotype_prediction.     GWAS results also available, binary GWAS flag added     secondary layer via integrate_gwas_haplotypes.

title: Rank Haplotype Blocks by Evidence Strength — rank_haplotype_blocks
text: min_freq hard population-level pre-filter identical purpose MAF filtering single SNPs: haplotype alleles observed frequency threshold effects reliably estimated regardless trait association, dropped dosage matrix built. rank_haplotype_blocks operates entirely downstream filter – ranks blocks already passed min_freq, individual alleles. block survives long least one alleles passes min_freq.

text: Difabachew YF et al. (2023). Genomic prediction haplotype blocks wheat. Frontiers Plant Science 14:1168547. doi:10.3389/fpls.2023.1168547 Weber SE, Frisch M, Snowdon RJ, Voss-Fels KP (2023). title: Rank Haplotype Blocks by Evidence Strength — rank_haplotype_blocks
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ haps <- extract_haplotypes(geno, snp_info, blocks) div  <- compute_haplotype_diversity(haps)  # Use case 1: genotype only -- rank by diversity ranked <- rank_haplotype_blocks(div)  # Use case 2: genotype + GWAS -- binary flag, then diversity within groups qtl    <- define_qtl_regions(gwas_df, blocks, snp_info) ranked <- rank_haplotype_blocks(div, qtl_regions = qtl)  # Use case 3: genotype + phenotype -- rank by Var(local GEBV) pred   <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, snp_info, blocks, blues = blues) ranked <- rank_haplotype_blocks(div, qtl_regions = qtl, pred_result = pred)  # Top 20 blocks ranked <- rank_haplotype_blocks(div, pred_result = pred, top_n_blocks = 20) } # }

what: Extract a Genotype Slice from an LDxBlocks Backend — read_chunk
title: Extract a Genotype Slice from an LDxBlocks Backend — read_chunk
text: Returns n_samples x length(col_idx) numeric matrix dosage values (0/1/2/NA) selected SNP columns. Works identically supported backend types.

title: Extract a Genotype Slice from an LDxBlocks Backend — read_chunk
code: read_chunk(backend, col_idx)

title: Extract a Genotype Slice from an LDxBlocks Backend — read_chunk
text: backend object class "LDxBlocks_backend" returned read_geno. col_idx Integer vector column indices (1-based SNP positions).

title: Extract a Genotype Slice from an LDxBlocks Backend — read_chunk

what: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
text: Reads genotype data disk (accepts -memory matrix) returns unified backend object LDxBlocks functions can consume. format auto-detected file extension unless overridden format.

title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
code: read_geno(   path,   format = NULL,   snp_info = NULL,   sample_ids = NULL,   sep = ",",   na_strings = c("NA", "N", "NN", "./.", ".", ""),   gds_cache = NULL,   clean_malformed = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
text: path Character. Path genotype file, R numeric matrix format = "matrix". format Character NULL. One "numeric", "hapmap", "vcf", "gds", "bed", "matrix". NULL (default) auto-detects extension. snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS (optionally REF, ALT). Required format = "matrix". sample_ids Character vector. Override sample IDs extracted file. Length must equal number samples. sep Character. Field separator "numeric" format. na_strings Character vector. Strings treated NA. Default c("NA", "N", "NN", "./.", ".", ""). gds_cache Character NULL. Path GDS cache file written format = "vcf" SNPRelate available. NULL (default), GDS file placed next VCF .gds extension. Set FALSE disable auto-conversion read VCF fully memory instead. cache file already exists reused without re-converting (fast subsequent calls). Ignored non-VCF formats. Default ",". clean_malformed Logical. TRUE, input file stream-cleaned reading: line whose delimiter-separated column count match header silently removed. adds one extra streaming pass file required files produced variant callers (e.g. NGSEP) embed extra characters FORMAT fields. Applies numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF formats. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages.

title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
text: object class "LDxBlocks_backend" elements:   type, n_samples, n_snps, sample_ids,   snp_info (data.frame SNP/CHR/POS/REF/ALT). Use   read_chunk extract genotype slices   close_backend release file handles.

title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
text: "numeric" CSV TXT, one row per SNP. Required columns:     SNP, CHR, POS, REF, ALT. Remaining     columns sample dosages {0, 1, 2, NA}. Extension: .csv,     .txt. "hapmap" Standard 11-column HapMap header followed sample     columns two-character nucleotide calls (AA, ,     NN missing). Extension: .hmp.txt. "vcf" VCF v4.2. phased (0|1) unphased     (0/1) GT fields accepted. Multi-allelic sites use first ALT.     Missing (./.) becomes NA. Extension: .vcf,     .vcf.gz. "gds" SNPRelate GDS file. Requires     BiocManager::install("SNPRelate"). Extension: .gds. "bed" PLINK binary BED. Companion .bim     .fam files must exist path stem. Extension:     .bed. "matrix" -memory R numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs,     0/1/2). Must supply snp_info separately.

title: Read Genotype Data into an LDxBlocks Backend — read_geno
code: # \donttest{ # In-memory matrix (existing workflow, unchanged) G   <- matrix(sample(0:2, 100*500, replace=TRUE), 100, 500) rownames(G) <- paste0("ind", 1:100) colnames(G) <- paste0("rs",  1:500) info <- data.frame(SNP=colnames(G), CHR=rep(1:5, each=100),                    POS=rep(seq(1e4,5e6,length.out=100),5)) be <- read_geno(G, format="matrix", snp_info=info) be$n_snps   # 500 #> [1] 500 mat <- read_chunk(be, 1:20) dim(mat)    # 100 x 20 #> [1] 100  20 close_backend(be) # }

what: Open a bigmemory-backed Genotype Store — read_geno_bigmemory
title: Open a bigmemory-backed Genotype Store — read_geno_bigmemory
text: Creates big.matrix disk supported genotype source wraps standard LDxBlocks_backend interface.  Subsequent calls read_chunk() retrieve columns via memory-mapped /O – OS pages requested bytes demand, peak RAM proportional columns accessed, full matrix. useful : filtered genotype matrix (individuals x SNPs) large     hold RAM (> 4-8 GB typical server configurations). pipeline needs restarted previous run –     .bin .desc files persist disk can reused     without re-loading source VCF/GDS. Multiple R sessions future workers need simultaneous read access     matrix (bigmemory's file-backed store safe concurrent     reads).

title: Open a bigmemory-backed Genotype Store — read_geno_bigmemory
code: read_geno_bigmemory(   source,   snp_info = NULL,   backingfile = tempfile("ldxbm_"),   backingpath = tempdir(),   type = "char",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Open a bigmemory-backed Genotype Store — read_geno_bigmemory
text: source Either LDxBlocks_backend object (format), plain R matrix, path previously saved .desc file (reattach without reloading). snp_info Data frame SNP, CHR, POS. Required source plain matrix .desc file (bigmemory store metadata). Optional ignored source file path LDxBlocks_backend – SNP info obtained automatically sources. backingfile Character. Stem .bin .desc files. Default: tempfile. Supply persistent path reuse across sessions. backingpath Character. Directory backing files. Default: tempdir(). type Storage type: "char" (1 byte per cell, values 0-2 fit, saves 8x vs double), "short" (2 bytes), "double". Default "char". verbose Logical. Default TRUE.

title: Open a bigmemory-backed Genotype Store — read_geno_bigmemory
text: LDxBlocks_backend object type = "bigmemory".   Use read_chunk(, col_idx) close_backend() normal.

title: Open a bigmemory-backed Genotype Store — read_geno_bigmemory
text: big.matrix stores matrix raw binary file (.bin) companion descriptor (.desc).  OS memory-maps file: read_chunk(, col_idx) calls bigmemory::.matrix(bm[, col_idx]) triggers page faults load requested column pages. equivalent GDS streaming model works input format avoids repeated snpgdsGetGeno() calls.

title: Open a bigmemory-backed Genotype Store — read_geno_bigmemory
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Convert a GDS backend to a persistent bigmemory store be_gds <- read_geno("mydata.gds") be_bm  <- read_geno_bigmemory(be_gds,                               backingfile = "mydata_bm",                               backingpath = "/data/ldxblocks") close_backend(be_gds)  # All subsequent runs reattach without reloading be_bm2 <- read_geno_bigmemory("/data/ldxblocks/mydata_bm.desc") blocks  <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be_bm2, CLQcut = 0.70) close_backend(be_bm2)  # From a plain matrix be_bm <- read_geno_bigmemory(ldx_geno, snp_info = ldx_snp_info) } # }

title: Read Pre-Phased VCF — read_phased_vcf

title: Read Pre-Phased VCF — read_phased_vcf
text: List: hap1, hap2 (SNPs x individuals, 0/1), dosage (0/1/2), snp_info, sample_ids.

what: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection by Chromosome — run_Big_LD_all_chr
title: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection by Chromosome — run_Big_LD_all_chr
text: Applies Big_LD() chromosome chromosome, collects results returns single tidy data frame annotated chromosome block length. recommended entry point genome-wide analyses.

title: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection by Chromosome — run_Big_LD_all_chr
code: run_Big_LD_all_chr(   geno_matrix,   snp_info = NULL,   CLQcut = 0.5,   method = c("r2", "rV2"),   n_threads = 1L,   clstgap = 40000,   leng = 200,   subSegmSize = 1500,   MAFcut = 0.05,   appendrare = FALSE,   singleton_as_block = FALSE,   checkLargest = FALSE,   CLQmode = "Density",   kin_method = "chol",   split = FALSE,   digits = -1L,   seed = NULL,   min_snps_chr = 10L,   chr = NULL,   clean_malformed = FALSE,   max_bp_distance = 0L,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection by Chromosome — run_Big_LD_all_chr
text: geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs; values 0/1/2), spanning chromosomes. snp_info Data frame columns CHR, SNP, POS. Column order flexible; columns matched name. CLQcut, clstgap, leng, subSegmSize, MAFcut, appendrare, checkLargest, CLQmode, kin_method, split, digits, seed, verbose Forwarded Big_LD(). See function's documentation details. method Character. LD metric: "r2" (default, standard squared Pearson correlation) "rV2" (kinship-adjusted). See Big_LD() details. n_threads Integer. Number OpenMP threads C++ LD kernel. Default 1L. Increase multi-core systems. singleton_as_block Logical. TRUE, SNPs pass MAF filtering assigned clique returned single-SNP blocks (start == end, length_bp == 1). Default FALSE. See Big_LD() details. min_snps_chr Integer. Chromosomes fewer SNPs MAF filtering skipped. Default 10L. Increase skip small scaffolds whole-genome datasets. chr Character vector NULL. supplied, named chromosomes processed. Labels must match values snp_info$CHR normalisation (chr prefix). E.g. chr = c("1","3","5") chr = "X". Default NULL processes chromosomes. clean_malformed Logical. TRUE, stream-clean input file reading removing lines whose column count match header. relevant geno_matrix file path wrapped backend. Default FALSE. max_bp_distance Integer. Maximum base-pair distance SNP pair r\(^2\) computed. Pairs beyond distance set zero adjacency matrix (assumed negligible LD). 0L (default) disables computes pairs (original behaviour). Recommended value WGS panels: 500000L (500 kb). effect CLQmode "Louvain" "Leiden" window spans less max_bp_distance. Requires sorted SNP positions within sub-segment (guaranteed run_Big_LD_all_chr).

title: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection by Chromosome — run_Big_LD_all_chr
text: data.frame columns:   start, end, start.rsID, end.rsID,   start.bp, end.bp, CHR, length_bp.   Rows sorted CHR start.bp.

title: Genome-Wide LD Block Detection by Chromosome — run_Big_LD_all_chr
code: # \donttest{ # Use the package example data -- 120 individuals, 230 SNPs, 3 chromosomes, # 9 simulated LD blocks (3 per chromosome). data(ldx_geno,     package = "LDxBlocks") data(ldx_snp_info, package = "LDxBlocks") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info,   method = "r2", CLQcut = 0.55, leng = 15L,   subSegmSize = 100L, verbose = FALSE ) head(blocks) #>   start end start.rsID end.rsID start.bp end.bp n_snps CHR length_bp #> 1     1  25     rs1001   rs1025     1000  25027     25   1     24028 #> 2    31  50     rs1031   rs1050    81064  99022     20   1     17959 #> 3    56  80     rs1056   rs1080   155368 179371     25   1     24004 #> 4     1  30     rs2001   rs2030     1000  30023     30   2     29024 #> 5    36  55     rs2036   rs2055    86236 105290     20   2     19055 #> 6    61  80     rs2061   rs2080   161515 180473     20   2     18959 summarise_blocks(blocks) #>      CHR n_blocks min_bp median_bp  mean_bp max_bp total_bp_covered #> 1      1        3  17959     24004 21997.00  24028            65991 #> 2      2        3  18959     19055 22346.00  29024            67038 #> 3      3        3  18069     18356 18385.00  18730            55155 #> 4 GENOME        9  17959     18959 20909.33  29024           188184 # }

what: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: Runs complete Tong et al. (2025) haplotype stacking pipeline using pre-adjusted phenotype values (BLUEs, BLUPs, adjusted entry means). Accepts either single trait multiple traits simultaneously. single trait supplied, GBLUP fitted via rrBLUP::kin.blup() block importance ranked Var(local GEBV) trait. multiple traits supplied, single trait-agnostic GRM computed shared across traits. rrBLUP::kin.blup() fitted per trait using shared GRM. Block importance summarised across traits per-trait columns plus cross-trait aggregates.

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
code: run_haplotype_prediction(   geno_matrix,   snp_info,   blocks,   blues,   id_col = "id",   blue_col = "blue",   blue_cols = NULL,   importance_rule = c("any", "all", "mean"),   top_n = NULL,   min_freq = 0.01,   min_snps = 3L,   bend = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs), values 0/1/2/NA. Row names must genotype IDs. snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS. blocks Block table run_Big_LD_all_chr. blues Pre-adjusted phenotype values. See section accepted formats. id_col Name ID column blues data frame. Default "id". blue_col Name BLUE column single-trait data frames. Default "blue". blue_cols Character vector trait column names multi-trait data frames. Default NULL – numeric non-ID columns used. importance_rule set combined important flag multi-trait results: "" (default): TRUE important >= 1 trait. "": TRUE important traits. "mean": TRUE var_scaled_mean >= 0.9. Ignored single-trait runs (single-trait important always scaled variance >= 0.9). top_n Integer NULL. Max haplotype alleles per block. Default NULL. min_freq Minimum haplotype allele frequency. Default 0.01. min_snps Minimum SNPs per block. Default 3L. bend Logical. Add ridge GRM diagonal. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default TRUE.

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: Named list. single-trait runs, contains: blocks, diversity, hap_matrix,     haplotypes, G Core pipeline outputs. n_blocks, n_hap_columns Summary counts. n_traits Integer: 1 single-trait. traits Character vector trait names. solver_used Character: always "rrBLUP". gebv Named numeric vector GEBV. snp_effects Per-SNP additive effects. local_gebv Matrix (individuals x blocks). block_importance Data frame block_id,     coordinates, var_local_gebv, var_scaled,     important. n_train, n_predict Training/prediction counts. multi-trait runs, structure gebv, snp_effects, local_gebv named lists (one per trait), block_importance contains additional per-trait cross-trait columns described Block importance section. block_importance_list added named list single-trait block importance data frames.

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: Accepts three formats: named numeric vector – single trait, names genotype     IDs: c(G001 = 4.2, G002 = 3.8). id_col/blue_col     ignored. data frame one trait column – single trait,     id_col blue_col specify columns. data frame multiple trait columns – multi-trait,     id_col names ID column, blue_cols names trait     columns (NULL use numeric non-ID columns). named list named numeric vectors – multi-trait     potentially different individuals per trait:     list(YLD = c(G001=4.2, ...), DIS = c(G001=0.3, ...)).

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: traits fitted using rrBLUP::kin.blup() per-trait loop sharing GRM. produces numerically identical results sommer::mmes() single-trait models (verified empirically 4 decimal places), avoiding multi-trait cbind() formula fails sommer <= 4.4.5 (Armadillo fixed-size matrix error C++ coefficient matrix construction). traits use GRM, cross-trait block importance values directly comparable. solver_used always "rrBLUP".

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: Var(local GEBV) computed per block backsolving per-SNP effects GEBV. Blocks scaled [0,1]; scaled variance >= 0.9 flagged important. follows Tong et al. (2024).

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: Per-trait columns var_scaled_<trait> important_<trait> added every trait. Cross-trait aggregates: var_scaled_mean Mean scaled variance across traits.     Primary ranking criterion – blocks consistently important across traits     robust stacking candidates. n_traits_important Count traits block     flagged important. important_any TRUE important least one trait. important_all TRUE important traits. important Controlled importance_rule.

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
text: Tong J et al. (2025). Haplotype stacking improve stability stripe rust resistance wheat. Theoretical Applied Genetics 138:267. doi:10.1007/s00122-025-05045-0 Tong J et al. (2024). Stacking beneficial haplotypes Vavilov wheat collection. Theoretical Applied Genetics 137:274. doi:10.1007/s00122-024-04784-w Endelman JB (2011). Ridge regression kernels genomic selection R package rrBLUP. Plant Genome 4:250-255. doi:10.3835/plantgenome2011.08.0024 Covarrubias-Pazaran G (2016). Genome-assisted prediction quantitative traits using R package sommer. PLOS ONE 11:e0156744. doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0156744

title: Haplotype Prediction and Block Importance from Pre-Adjusted Phenotypes — run_haplotype_prediction
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(LDxBlocks) be     <- read_geno("mydata.vcf.gz") blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(be, CLQcut = 0.70) geno   <- read_chunk(be, seq_len(be$n_snps)) rownames(geno) <- be$sample_ids colnames(geno) <- be$snp_info$SNP  # -- Single trait: named vector --------------------------------------------- blues_vec <- c(G001 = 4.21, G002 = 3.87, G003 = 5.14) res <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, be$snp_info, blocks, blues = blues_vec) res$block_importance[res$block_importance$important, ] sort(res$gebv, decreasing = TRUE)  # -- Single trait: data frame ----------------------------------------------- blues_df <- read.csv("blues.csv")   # columns: Genotype, YLD_BLUE res <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, be$snp_info, blocks,                                  blues    = blues_df,                                  id_col   = "Genotype",                                  blue_col = "YLD_BLUE")  # -- Multi-trait: data frame ------------------------------------------------ blues_mt <- read.csv("blues_mt.csv")  # columns: id, YLD, DIS, PHT res_mt <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, be$snp_info, blocks,                                     blues           = blues_mt,                                     id_col          = "id",                                     blue_cols       = c("YLD","DIS","PHT"),                                     importance_rule = "any") res_mt$n_traits        # 3 res_mt$solver_used     # "rrBLUP" res_mt$block_importance[   res_mt$block_importance$important_any,   c("block_id","var_scaled_YLD","var_scaled_DIS","var_scaled_mean",     "n_traits_important")]  # -- Multi-trait: named list (different individuals per trait) -------------- res_mt2 <- run_haplotype_prediction(geno, be$snp_info, blocks,   blues = list(     YLD = c(G001 = 4.2, G002 = 3.8),     DIS = c(G001 = 0.3, G003 = 0.7)   )) } # }

what: Finlay-Wilkinson Stability Analysis of Haplotype Effects Across Environments — run_haplotype_stability
title: Finlay-Wilkinson Stability Analysis of Haplotype Effects Across Environments — run_haplotype_stability
text: Estimates stability haplotype block's effect across multiple environments using Finlay-Wilkinson (1963) regression. block, per-environment GEBV contribution regressed environment mean (environmental index). regression slope b_i = 1 indicates average stability; b_i > 1 = -average response (exploits good environments); b_i < 1 = -average response (robust across environments).

title: Finlay-Wilkinson Stability Analysis of Haplotype Effects Across Environments — run_haplotype_stability
code: run_haplotype_stability(   geno_matrix,   snp_info,   blocks,   blues_list,   top_n = NULL,   min_freq = 0.05,   min_snps = 3L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Finlay-Wilkinson Stability Analysis of Haplotype Effects Across Environments — run_haplotype_stability
text: geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs). snp_info Data frame SNP, CHR, POS. blocks LD block table run_Big_LD_all_chr. blues_list Named list named numeric vectors, one per environment: list(env1 = c(id1=val,...), env2 = ...). top_n Integer NULL. Max haplotype alleles per block. Default NULL. min_freq Numeric. Minimum haplotype allele frequency. Default 0.05. min_snps Integer. Minimum SNPs per block. Default 3L. verbose Logical. Default TRUE.

title: Finlay-Wilkinson Stability Analysis of Haplotype Effects Across Environments — run_haplotype_stability
text: Data frame one row per block, sorted CHR   start_bp. block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp Block coordinates. b Finlay-Wilkinson slope. b=1: average stability;       b>1: exploits good environments; b<1: robust across environments. b_se Standard error b. r2_fw R-squared FW regression. s2d Deviation mean square (non-linear instability). stable Logical; TRUE b significantly       different 1 alpha=0.05.

title: Finlay-Wilkinson Stability Analysis of Haplotype Effects Across Environments — run_haplotype_stability
text: Finlay KW, Wilkinson GN (1963). analysis adaptation plant-breeding programme. Australian Journal Agricultural Research 14(6):742-754.

title: Finlay-Wilkinson Stability Analysis of Haplotype Effects Across Environments — run_haplotype_stability
code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, ldx_blues, package = "LDxBlocks") # Simulate two environments by splitting the BLUEs b1 <- setNames(ldx_blues$YLD + rnorm(nrow(ldx_blues), 0, 0.1),                ldx_blues$id) b2 <- setNames(ldx_blues$YLD + rnorm(nrow(ldx_blues), 0.5, 0.15),                ldx_blues$id) stab <- run_haplotype_stability(   geno_matrix = ldx_geno,   snp_info    = ldx_snp_info,   blocks      = ldx_blocks,   blues_list  = list(env1 = b1, env2 = b2),   verbose     = FALSE ) head(stab[order(stab$b), ]) #>                block_id CHR start.bp end.bp       b b_se r2_fw s2d p_b1 stable #> 7    block_3_1000_19068   3     1000  19068 -5.3651  NaN     1  NA  NaN  FALSE #> 6  block_2_86236_105290   2    86236 105290 -3.7255  NaN     1  NA  NaN  FALSE #> 9   block_3_74532_93854   3    74532  93854 -2.9515  NaN     1  NA  NaN  FALSE #> 2 block_1_155368_179371   1   155368 179371 -0.1938  NaN     1  NA  NaN  FALSE #> 5 block_2_161515_180473   2   161515 180473  1.6044  NaN     1  NA  NaN  FALSE #> 8 block_3_149647_168376   3   149647 168376  1.6153  NaN     1  NA  NaN  FALSE # }

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: Single-call wrapper: one genotype file , complete haplotype dataset . Handles format detection, MAF filtering, call-rate filtering, imputation, LD block detection, optional Beagle phasing, haplotype extraction, diversity analysis, feature matrix construction, output writing.

code: run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source,   out_dir = ".",   out_blocks,   out_diversity,   out_hap_matrix,   hap_format = c("numeric", "character"),   phase = FALSE,   beagle_jar = NULL,   beagle_threads = 1L,   java_path = "java",   beagle_java_mem_gb = NULL,   beagle_args = "",   beagle_ref_panel = NULL,   beagle_map_file = NULL,   beagle_chrom = NULL,   beagle_seed = NULL,   beagle_burnin = NULL,   beagle_iterations = NULL,   beagle_window = NULL,   beagle_overlap = NULL,   maf_cut = 0.05,   impute = c("mean_rounded", "mode", "none"),   min_callrate = 0,   CLQcut = 0.5,   method = c("r2", "rV2"),   kin_method = "chol",   CLQmode = c("Density", "Maximal", "Louvain", "Leiden"),   leng = 200L,   subSegmSize = 1500L,   clstgap = 40000L,   split = FALSE,   appendrare = FALSE,   singleton_as_block = FALSE,   checkLargest = FALSE,   digits = -1L,   n_threads = 1L,   min_snps_chr = 10L,   max_bp_distance = 0L,   min_snps_block = 3L,   top_n = NULL,   min_freq = 0.01,   scale_hap_matrix = FALSE,   chr = NULL,   clean_malformed = FALSE,   use_bigmemory = FALSE,   bigmemory_path = tempdir(),   bigmemory_type = "char",   verbose = TRUE )

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: geno_source File path LDxBlocks_backend. Supported formats: VCF (.vcf/.vcf.gz), HapMap (.hmp.txt), CSV, GDS, PLINK BED. phase = TRUE requires VCF/VCF.gz. out_dir Output directory. Default ".". phase = TRUE, beagle.jar expected phased VCF written . out_blocks Path LD block table CSV. out_diversity Path haplotype diversity table CSV. out_hap_matrix Path haplotype genotype matrix file. hap_format "numeric" (default) "character". phase TRUE, phase Beagle. Default FALSE. beagle_jar Path beagle.jar. Default: file.path(out_dir, "beagle.jar"). beagle_threads Beagle threads. Default 1L. java_path Java executable. Default "java". beagle_java_mem_gb JVM heap GB (-Xmx). Default NULL. beagle_args Extra Beagle argument string. Default "". beagle_ref_panel Phased reference VCF path. Default NULL. beagle_map_file Genetic map file path. Default NULL. beagle_chrom Restrict Beagle one chromosome. Default NULL (inherits chr set). beagle_seed Integer seed reproducibility. Default NULL. beagle_burnin Burn-iterations. Default NULL. beagle_iterations Phasing iterations. Default NULL. beagle_window Window size. Default NULL. beagle_overlap Window overlap. Default NULL. maf_cut Minimum MAF. Default 0.05. impute "mean_rounded" (default), "mode", "none". min_callrate Minimum per-SNP call rate. Default 0.0. CLQcut r\(^2\) threshold CLQD. Default 0.5. method "r2" (default) "rV2". kin_method "chol" (default) "eigen". CLQmode "Density" (default), "Maximal", "Louvain", "Leiden". leng Boundary scan half-window (SNPs). Default 200L. subSegmSize Max SNPs per sub-segment. Default 1500L. clstgap Max bp gap within clique. Default 40000L. split Split cliques largest gap. Default FALSE. appendrare Append rare SNPs nearest block. Default FALSE. singleton_as_block Return singletons blocks. Default FALSE. checkLargest Dense-core pre-pass. Default FALSE. digits Round r\(^2\) (-1L = ). Default -1L. n_threads OpenMP threads. Default 1L. min_snps_chr Skip chromosomes SNP count. Default 10L. max_bp_distance Max bp r\(^2\) (0L = ). Default 0L. min_snps_block Min SNPs per haplotype block. Default 3L. top_n Max alleles per block (NULL = min_freq). Default NULL. min_freq Min haplotype allele frequency. Default 0.01. scale_hap_matrix Scale haplotype matrix columns. Default FALSE. chr Chromosomes process (NULL = ). Default NULL. clean_malformed Remove malformed VCF lines. Default FALSE. use_bigmemory File-backed bigmemory store. Default FALSE. bigmemory_path Directory backing files. Default tempdir(). bigmemory_type "char" (default), "short", "double". verbose Print progress. Default TRUE.

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: phase = FALSE (default) Dosage-pattern haplotypes extracted directly imputed matrix.     external tools required. Fast. Suitable genomic prediction.     block entry multi-SNP dosage string - true gametic     haplotype. Frequencies individual-level pattern proportions. phase = TRUE Beagle 5.x called original input VCF LD block detection,     producing true statistically-inferred gametic haplotypes using     population-LD across markers. Haplotype strings become     "g1|g2". Frequencies computed \(2N\) gamete     observations. Recommended diversity analysis biologically     interpretable results. Requirements: geno_source must VCF/VCF.gz.     Place beagle.jar out_dir supply via     beagle_jar. Download:     https://faculty.washington.edu/browning/beagle/beagle.html

what: Why Beagle and why a cleaned VCF
title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
text: Beagle 5.x performs chromosome-level statistical phasing using population LD across markers simultaneously, producing true inferred gametic haplotypes. supported phasing method LDxBlocks. phase = TRUE, pipeline pass geno_source directly Beagle. Instead first writes cleaned VCF (<geno_source_stem>_cleaned.vcf.gz out_dir) containing exactly SNPs survived MAF filtering, call-rate filtering, chromosome subsetting, malformed-record removal. guarantees phased VCF's SNP set identical marker set used LD block detection, .read_and_cache_phased_vcf() can align phased gametes blocks without SNP-count mismatches.

title: End-to-End Haplotype Block Pipeline — run_ldx_pipeline
code: # \donttest{ geno_file <- system.file("extdata", "example_genotypes_numeric.csv",                           package = "LDxBlocks") res <- run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source = geno_file, out_dir = tempdir(),   out_blocks = tempfile(fileext=".csv"),   out_diversity = tempfile(fileext=".csv"),   out_hap_matrix = tempfile(fileext=".csv"),   phase = FALSE, maf_cut = 0.05, CLQcut = 0.5,   leng = 10L, subSegmSize = 80L, verbose = FALSE ) #> [LDxBlocks] Hap QC: n_snps range [19, 30] | blocks=90 | NA_matrix=0 #> [LDxBlocks] Pipeline QC: all checks passed. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # With Beagle phasing (place beagle.jar in out_dir first): res2 <- run_ldx_pipeline(   geno_source = "data.vcf.gz", out_dir = "results/",   out_blocks = "results/blocks.csv",   out_diversity = "results/diversity.csv",   out_hap_matrix = "results/hap_matrix.csv",   phase = TRUE, beagle_threads = 4L,   beagle_java_mem_gb = 8, beagle_seed = 42L ) } # }# }

what: Between-Block Haplotype Allele Epistasis Scan — scan_block_by_block_epistasis
title: Between-Block Haplotype Allele Epistasis Scan — scan_block_by_block_epistasis
text: Tests interactions haplotype allele dosage columns different LD blocks. significant allele (test_block_haplotypes), tests interaction every haplotype allele blocks: $$y = \mu + \alpha_i x_i + \alpha_j x_j + \gamma_{ij}(x_i x_j) + \varepsilon$$ Restricting significant alleles x blocks gives O(n_sig x n_total_alleles) tests – tractable WGS scale. panel: ~25 significant alleles x 17,943 alleles = ~450,000 tests. trans-haplotype epistasis scan: tests whether effect resistance haplotype one block depends haplotype background another block – form genetic background dependence single-SNP even single-block analyses detect.

what: Within-Block Pairwise SNP Epistasis Scan — scan_block_epistasis
title: Within-Block Pairwise SNP Epistasis Scan — scan_block_epistasis
text: Tests pairwise SNP x SNP interactions within haplotype blocks identified significant test_block_haplotypes. significant block, C(p, 2) SNP pairs tested interaction using model: $$y = \mu + a_i x_i + a_j x_j + aa_{ij}(x_i x_j) + \varepsilon$$ GRM-corrected REML residuals (null model test_block_haplotypes), ensuring tests population-structure-corrected. Restricting significant blocks avoids genome-wide explosion pairwise tests: 15 significant blocks ~200 SNPs , total number tests ~300,000, compared ~4.4 billion unrestricted genome-wide scan.

title: Within-Block Pairwise SNP Epistasis Scan — scan_block_epistasis
code: scan_block_epistasis(   assoc,   geno_matrix,   snp_info,   blocks,   blues,   haplotypes,   trait = NULL,   sig_blocks = NULL,   min_freq = 0.05,   max_snps_per_block = 300L,   sig_threshold = 0.05,   sig_metric = c("p_simplem_sidak", "p_simplem", "p_bonf", "p_fdr"),   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   id_col = "id",   blue_col = "blue",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Within-Block Pairwise SNP Epistasis Scan — scan_block_epistasis
text: assoc Output test_block_haplotypes. Used identify significant blocks obtain pre-computed GRM residuals. geno_matrix Numeric matrix (individuals x SNPs) LDxBlocks_backend. imputed, MAF-filtered genotype matrix Job 1 (res$geno_matrix). snp_info Data frame columns SNP, CHR, POS. blocks LD block table run_Big_LD_all_chr. blues Pre-adjusted phenotype means (format test_block_haplotypes). Named numeric vector named list. haplotypes Named list extract_haplotypes. Used re-build GRM REML residual computation. trait Character. trait use residual computation blues named list. Default NULL uses first trait. sig_blocks Character vector. Block IDs scan. NULL (default) uses blocks significant_omnibus = TRUE assoc$block_tests. min_freq Numeric. Minimum MAF SNPs within block. Default 0.05. max_snps_per_block Integer. Maximum SNPs per block switching random subsampling pairs. Default 300L (C(300,2)=44,850 pairs per block). Set NULL always use SNPs. sig_threshold Numeric. Significance threshold. Default 0.05. sig_metric Character. correction drives primary significant flag. One : "p_simplem_sidak" (default, recommended) – simpleM Sidak. "p_simplem" – simpleM Bonferroni. "p_bonf" – plain Bonferroni (p x n_pairs). three p-value columns always present regardless choice. meff_percent_cut Numeric. Variance cutoff simpleM Meff. Default 0.995. id_col Character. ID column blues data frame. blue_col Character. Phenotype column blues data frame. verbose Logical. Default TRUE.

Always present columns: block_id, CHR,     start_bp, end_bp, trait, SNP_i,     SNP_j, POS_i, POS_j, dist_bp,     aa_effect, SE, t_stat, p_wald,     Meff (simpleM effective test count interaction eigenspectrum),     p_bonf (Bonferroni: p x n_pairs),     p_simplem (simpleM Bonferroni: p x Meff),     p_simplem_sidak (simpleM Sidak: 1-(1-p)^Meff),     significant (driven sig_metric),     significant_bonf, significant_simplem,     significant_simplem_sidak. scan_summary Data frame. One row per block: number pairs     tested, number significant, minimum p-value. n_blocks_scanned Integer. n_pairs_total Integer. Total pairwise tests performed.

title: Sliding-Window Genome-Wide Diversity Scan — scan_diversity_windows
text: Computes haplotype diversity metrics (, Shannon entropy, n_eff_alleles, dominant frequency) sliding windows across genome, independently LD block boundaries. Useful identifying diversity valleys (bottlenecks, selective sweeps) comparing wild/elite panels without needing pre-defined blocks.

what: Score Individual Haplotype Portfolios Against Known Allele Effects — score_favorable_haplotypes
title: Score Individual Haplotype Portfolios Against Known Allele Effects — score_favorable_haplotypes
text: Scores individual's genome-wide haplotype composition table per-allele effects (genomic prediction association analysis), producing per-block score genome-wide stacking index. primary tool translating haplotype genomic prediction results actionable breeding rankings. Scoring rule: individual \(\) LD block \(b\), block score : $$s_{ib} = \sum_{j} \hat{\alpha}_j \cdot d_{ij}$$ \(\hat{\alpha}_j\) known effect allele \(j\) \(d_{ij}\) dosage (copies) allele \(j\) carried individual \(\) (0/1 unphased data; 0/1/2 phased data). genome-wide stacking index sum block scores across scored blocks, optionally normalised [0, 1].

title: Score Individual Haplotype Portfolios Against Known Allele Effects — score_favorable_haplotypes
code: score_favorable_haplotypes(   haplotypes,   allele_effects,   min_freq = 0.02,   missing_string = ".",   normalize = TRUE )

title: Score Individual Haplotype Portfolios Against Known Allele Effects — score_favorable_haplotypes
text: haplotypes Named list produced extract_haplotypes. Must carry block_info attribute. Individual IDs taken names(haplotypes[[1]]) (names first block's haplotype vector). individuals present haplotypes scored, including allele_effects (receive score 0 missing blocks). allele_effects Data frame specifying known per-allele additive effects. Required columns: block_id (character) - Block identifier matching     names(haplotypes). allele (character) - Haplotype allele string matching     strings haplotypes[[block_id]]. allele_effect (numeric) - Effect size. Positive = allele     increases trait value; negative = decreases . Units     phenotype scale used estimate effects. Accepts output decompose_block_effects directly. effects test_block_haplotypes used, filter one trait first (allele_effects[allele_effects$trait == "YLD", ]) rename effect allele_effect. Blocks alleles haplotypes matching entry allele_effects contribute score 0 individuals. min_freq Numeric (0, 1). Minimum allele frequency full panel. Alleles population frequency threshold excluded scoring even appear allele_effects. Default 0.02. prevents rare private alleles dominating scores based unreliable frequency estimates. missing_string Character. string used haplotype vectors denote missing genotype data (e.g. individuals insufficient marker coverage block). Individuals string block receive NA block's score (excluded mean still contribute n_blocks_scored = 0 block). Default ".". normalize Logical. TRUE (default), genome-wide stacking index linearly scaled [0, 1] across individuals: $$\text{index}_i = (S_i - S_{\min}) / (S_{\max} - S_{\min})$$ \(S_i\) raw sum block scores. makes index interpretable percentile rank within panel comparable across panels different numbers scored blocks. Set FALSE return raw summed effects (phenotype units), preferable comparing candidate sets across different evaluation sets.

title: Score Individual Haplotype Portfolios Against Known Allele Effects — score_favorable_haplotypes
text: Data frame one row per individual, sorted ascending   rank (best candidates first). Contains following columns: id Character. Individual identifier, taken     names(haplotypes[[1]]). stacking_index Numeric. Genome-wide sum per-block     haplotype scores. normalize = TRUE, scaled [0, 1]     1.0 = individual favourable genome-wide haplotype     combination panel 0.0 = least favourable.     normalize = FALSE, units phenotype units x allele dosage. n_blocks_scored Integer. Number LD blocks     least one allele effect available allele_effects     least one allele passed min_freq. Maximum possible value     equals number unique block_id values     allele_effects overlap names(haplotypes). mean_block_score Numeric. Raw (unnormalised) mean per-block     score: stacking_index_raw / n_blocks_scored. Useful comparing     candidates across panels different numbers scored blocks, since     independent n_blocks_scored. rank Integer. Rank based stacking_index, 1     indicating individual highest (favourable) index.     Ties broken "min" (tied individuals share lower rank     number). score_<block_id> Numeric. One column per scored LD block,     named score_ followed block identifier (e.g.     score_block_1_1000_103000). Contains raw per-block score     individual (effect x dosage sum across alleles). Zero     individual carries alleles known effects block. Used     identify genomic regions drive individual's stacking index.

title: Score Individual Haplotype Portfolios Against Known Allele Effects — score_favorable_haplotypes
code: # \donttest{ data(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks, ldx_blues, package = "LDxBlocks") haps    <- extract_haplotypes(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks) hap_mat <- build_haplotype_feature_matrix(haps)$matrix G       <- compute_haplotype_grm(hap_mat) pred    <- run_haplotype_prediction(ldx_geno, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks,                                      blues    = setNames(ldx_blues$YLD,                                                          ldx_blues$id),                                      verbose  = FALSE) ae <- decompose_block_effects(haps, ldx_snp_info, ldx_blocks,                                snp_effects = pred$snp_effects) scores <- score_favorable_haplotypes(haps, allele_effects = ae) head(scores[order(scores$rank), c("id","stacking_index","rank")]) #>            id stacking_index rank #> ind083 ind083       1.000000    1 #> ind025 ind025       0.944428    2 #> ind095 ind095       0.892078    3 #> ind108 ind108       0.862557    4 #> ind049 ind049       0.856506    5 #> ind068 ind068       0.847426    6 # }

what: Block-Level Haplotype Association Testing (Q+K Mixed Linear Model with simpleM Multiple-Testing Correction) — test_block_haplotypes
title: Block-Level Haplotype Association Testing (Q+K Mixed Linear Model with simpleM Multiple-Testing Correction) — test_block_haplotypes
text: Performs genome-wide haplotype block association tests one quantitative traits. LD block tested unit: per-allele Wald tests identify specific haplotype alleles drive association, omnibus F-test evaluates block whole. Population structure kinship corrected jointly unified mixed linear model. addition raw FDR-adjusted p-values, function applies simpleM-style multiple-testing correction account correlation among tested haplotype alleles. simpleM estimates effective number independent tests (\(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\)) eigenvalues haplotype allele correlation matrix derives either Bonferroni-style Sidak-style adjusted threshold. Statistical model (Q+K / EMMAX formulation): $$y = \mu + \alpha \cdot x_{\mathrm{hap}} +        \sum_{k=1}^{K} \beta_k \, PC_k + g + \varepsilon$$ \(x_{\mathrm{hap}}\) Haplotype allele dosage (0, 1, 2 copies     phased data; 0 1 unphased). \(PC_k\) k-th eigenvector haplotype GRM,     fixed-effect covariate population structure. \(g \sim MVN(0,\,\sigma_g^2 G)\) Polygenic background     random effect. \(\varepsilon \sim MVN(0,\,\sigma_e^2 )\) Residual error. simpleM extension (Gao et al. 2008, 2010, 2011): \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\) estimated eigenspectrum haplotype allele dosage correlation matrix - number principal components needed explain meff_percent_cut variance (default 99.5%). Two simpleM adjusted p-value paths always computed: p_simplem - Bonferroni-style:     \(\min(p \times M_{\mathrm{eff}},\,1)\). p_simplem_sidak - Sidak-style:     \(1 - (1 - p)^{M_{\mathrm{eff}}}\). haplotype alleles correlated within across nearby blocks, simpleM less conservative raw Bonferroni still providing family-wise error control.

title: Block-Level Haplotype Association Testing (Q+K Mixed Linear Model with simpleM Multiple-Testing Correction) — test_block_haplotypes
code: test_block_haplotypes(   haplotypes,   blues,   blocks,   n_pcs = 0L,   top_n = NULL,   min_freq = 0.05,   id_col = "id",   blue_col = "blue",   blue_cols = NULL,   sig_threshold = 0.05,   sig_metric = c("p_wald", "p_fdr", "p_simplem", "p_simplem_sidak"),   meff_scope = c("chromosome", "global", "block"),   meff_percent_cut = 0.995,   meff_max_cols = 1000L,   optimize_pcs = FALSE,   optimize_pcs_max = 10L,   optimize_method = c("bic_lambda", "bic", "lambda"),   plot = FALSE,   out_dir = ".",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Block-Level Haplotype Association Testing (Q+K Mixed Linear Model with simpleM Multiple-Testing Correction) — test_block_haplotypes
text: haplotypes Named list produced extract_haplotypes. element named character vector (one haplotype dosage string per individual), list must carry block_info attribute. blues Pre-adjusted phenotype means. Accepted four formats: named numeric vector, single-trait data frame, multi-trait data frame, named list named numeric vectors. least 10 common individuals required per trait. blocks LD block table run_Big_LD_all_chr. Used block metadata annotation output . n_pcs Integer (>= 0) NULL. Number haplotype-GRM eigenvectors include fixed-effect population structure covariates. 0L (default): pure GRM / EMMAX. 1-10: Q+K model. NULL: auto-selected GRM scree-plot elbow, capped 10. Ignored optimize_pcs = TRUE. top_n Integer NULL. Maximum haplotype alleles per block feature matrix. NULL retains alleles min_freq. min_freq Numeric (0, 1). Minimum haplotype allele frequency. Default 0.05. id_col Character. Individual-ID column name blues data frame. Default "id". blue_col Character. Phenotype column name single-trait data frames. Default "blue". blue_cols Character vector. Phenotype column names multi-trait data frames. Default NULL. sig_threshold Numeric (0, 1]. Significance cutoff applied p-value chosen sig_metric. Default 0.05. Common choices: 0.05 - recommended sig_metric = "p_fdr",     "p_simplem", "p_simplem_sidak". 0.05 / n_tests - raw Bonferroni     sig_metric = "p_wald". sig_metric Character. p-value use significant significant_omnibus flags. One : "p_wald" - raw Wald p-value. Use pre-corrected     sig_threshold (e.g. 0.05 / n_tests). "p_fdr" - Benjamini-Hochberg FDR. Recommended     discovery-oriented analyses. "p_simplem" - simpleM Bonferroni-style adjusted p-value. "p_simplem_sidak" - simpleM Sidak-style adjusted p-value.     Recommended default family-wise error control desired     correlated haplotype predictors. four p-value columns always present output regardless choice. meff_scope Character. Scope \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\) estimated. One : "chromosome" (recommended) - separate \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\)     per chromosome; best balance scalability LD awareness. "global" - one genome-wide \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\); suitable     moderate-sized scans. "block" - one \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\) per LD block     allele level; block omnibus tests \(M_{\mathrm{eff}} = 1\). meff_percent_cut Numeric (0, 1). Proportion variance explained retained PCs simpleM eigendecomposition. Default 0.995 (99.5%), following original simpleM recommendation. meff_max_cols Integer. Maximum columns per eigendecomposition block computing \(M_{\mathrm{eff}}\). Larger groups chunked summed. Default 1000L. optimize_pcs Logical. TRUE, automatically selects n_pcs fitting REML null models k = 0, 1, ..., optimize_pcs_max choosing value minimises criterion set optimize_method. Selection uses first trait (haplotype GRM shared across traits). Default FALSE: n_pcs used directly. optimize_pcs_max Integer. Upper bound number PCs evaluated optimize_pcs = TRUE. Default 10L. optimize_method Character. Criterion PC model selection optimize_pcs = TRUE. One : "bic_lambda" (default, recommended) - hybrid score:     \(|\lambda_{GC} - 1| + 0.01 \times \tilde{BIC}\),     \(\tilde{BIC}\) BIC scaled \([0,1]\) across     k values. Minimises genomic inflation BIC breaks ties toward     fewer PCs. "bic" - minimise REML BIC null model:     \(-2 \cdot \log L + k_{params} \cdot \log n\). "lambda" - minimise \(|\lambda_{GC} - 1|\) alone,     \(\lambda_{GC}\) estimated fast 500-allele scan     GRM-corrected residuals. Ignored optimize_pcs = FALSE. plot Logical. TRUE, save Manhattan Q-Q plots. Default FALSE. Significance markers reflect sig_metric. out_dir Character. Directory optional plots. Default ".". verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.
