title: LDxBlocks 0.3.2.9000 (development)
text: Three new exported functions implement within-block -block epistasis detection. operate GRM-corrected REML residuals null model test_block_haplotypes(), ensuring population-structure-corrected tests throughout. scan_block_epistasis() — Within-block pairwise SNP epistasis scan. Tests C(p, 2) SNP pairs within significant blocks interaction term aa_ij y = mu + ai*xi + aj*xj + aa_ij*(xi*xj) + e. Restricted significant blocks (controlled sig_blocks) avoid genome-wide combinatorial explosion. Multiple-testing correction: Bonferroni simpleM Sidak within block; Meff estimated eigenspectrum pairwise interaction column matrix. sig_metric parameter (default "p_simplem_sidak") controls correction drives significant flag. Output columns: p_wald, p_bonf, p_simplem, p_simplem_sidak, Meff, significant, significant_bonf, significant_simplem, significant_simplem_sidak. Returns LDxBlocks_epistasis. scan_block_by_block_epistasis() — Trans-haplotype -block epistasis scan. Tests significant haplotype alleles every allele blocks: O(n_sig × n_total_alleles) tests, Bonferroni corrected. Identifies genetic background dependence resistance haplotype one locus functions presence specific background another locus — form epistasis single-block single-SNP analyses detect. 25 significant alleles × 17,943 total alleles scan involves ~450,000 tests. Returns LDxBlocks_block_epistasis. fine_map_epistasis_block() — Single-block epistasis fine-mapping. Identifies specific interacting SNP pairs within one block. Dispatches exhaustive pairwise scan blocks p ≤ 200 SNPs (method = "pairwise"), LASSO pairwise interaction terms via glmnet::cv.glmnet() lambda.1se larger blocks (method = "lasso"). method = "auto" (default) selects automatically. Requires pre-computed REML residuals (y_resid). Shared internal helpers added: .fit_null_reml(), .pairwise_interaction_scan(). glmnet added Suggests (required fine_map_epistasis_block(method = "lasso")). 43 new tests tests/testthat/test-epistasis.R.

what: Enhancement: PC model selection and expanded plot outputs in test_block_haplotypes()
title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development)
text: test_block_haplotypes() now supports automatic selection number population structure covariates (n_pcs) via new BIC/lambda/hybrid optimisation framework, produces three new diagnostic plots PDF format. New parameters: optimize_pcs = FALSE — TRUE, fits REML null models n_pcs = 0..optimize_pcs_max selects value minimising score criterion chosen optimize_method. Uses first trait (GRM shared across traits). FALSE (default), n_pcs used directly. optimize_pcs_max = 10L — upper bound number PCs evaluated optimisation. "bic" — minimise BIC null REML model: −2·logLik + k·log(n), k = intercept + n_pcs + σ²_g + σ²_e. "lambda" — minimise |λ_GC − 1|, λ estimated fast 500-allele scan GRM-corrected residuals. directly targets genomic control calibration. "bic_lambda" (default, recommended GWAS) — hybrid score: |λ − 1| + 0.01 × scaled_BIC. Minimises inflation/deflation BIC breaks ties toward simpler (fewer PCs) model. New return element: $pc_model_selection — data.frame one row per k tested: n_pcs, BIC, lambda_gc, score, selected. Printed print() visible run log. NULL optimize_pcs = FALSE. Plot changes (breaking): plots now saved PDF instead PNG. Three plots produced per run: manhattan_<trait>.pdf — unchanged content, format changed PDF. qq_<trait>.pdf — unchanged content, format changed PDF. pca_grm.pdf — new: individuals PC1 × PC2 space (GRM eigenvectors), coloured first trait’s phenotype using blue-white-red diverging palette. Subtitle reports number PCs used model. grm_scree.pdf — new: scree plot GRM eigenvalues (PC30), selected PC cutoff highlighted red cumulative variance curve overlaid dashed line. optimize_pcs = TRUE, subtitle reports selection criterion, selected k, lambda_GC. Breaking change note: downstream code expected manhattan_*.png qq_*.png file names must updated use .pdf extension.

what: Enhancement: full multiple-testing correction set in estimate_diplotype_effects()
title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development)
text: estimate_diplotype_effects() now computes four correction columns test_block_haplotypes(), making two modules symmetric. significant flag now driven user-selected criterion rather always using plain Bonferroni. New parameters: sig_threshold = 0.05 — significance cutoff applied p-value selected sig_metric. "p_omnibus_adj" — plain Bonferroni × n_blocks_per_trait (old default, retained backward compatibility). "p_omnibus_fdr" — Benjamini-Hochberg FDR. "p_omnibus_simplem" — simpleM Bonferroni-style: min(p × Meff, 1), Meff estimated block-summary PC1 eigenspectrum. "p_omnibus_simplem_sidak" (recommended) — simpleM Šidák-style: 1 − (1 − p)^Meff. Consistent test_block_haplotypes(). meff_percent_cut = 0.995 — variance threshold simpleM Meff estimation. meff_max_cols = 1000L — chunk size large eigendecompositions. New output columns $omnibus_tests (always present):

what: New feature: block_match = “position” in compare_block_effects() and compare_gwas_effects()
title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development)
text: cross-population comparison functions now support matching LD blocks populations genomic interval overlap rather block_id string equality. matters. LD block boundaries population-specific: causal QTL region may carved 100 kb block Population 130 kb block Population B, producing different block_id strings ("block_1_10000_85000" vs "block_1_10000_91000"). default block_match = "id", compared — block appears Pop1-replication signal lost. New parameters: block_match = c("id", "position") — "id" (default) preserves backward compatibility; "position" matches Intersection--Union (IoU) base pairs. overlap_min = 0.50 — minimum IoU two blocks considered region. Blocks threshold labelled "pop1_only". New output column match_type $concordance: "exact" — block_id string (boundaries identical) "position" — matched genomic overlap (boundaries differ IoU ≥ overlap_min) "pop1_only" — Pop2 block overlaps Pop1 block threshold NA — block tables supplied New internal function .match_blocks_by_position() performs interval join using CHR-filtered IoU search. Pop1 block, finds best-matching Pop2 block IoU records match type. Accessible via LDxBlocks:::.match_blocks_by_position(). 8 new tests test-association.R (122 → 130 total).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development)
text: Cross-population effect concordance external GWAS results. Complements compare_block_effects() users ran association analysis outside LDxBlocks (GAPIT, TASSEL, FarmCPU, PLINK, tool). Two input paths: Pre-mapped (recommended): supply define_qtl_regions() output population. Block assignment explicit auditable. Raw GWAS + blocks (convenience): supply raw GWAS data frames block tables; define_qtl_regions() called internally. SE derivation. SE column absent (common GAPIT/FarmCPU output), SE derived z-score: SE = |BETA| / |Φ⁻¹(P/2)|. se_derived_pop1 / se_derived_pop2 output columns flag population required step. Key differences compare_block_effects(): External GWAS produces one lead SNP per block rather multiple haplotype allele effects. Consequently: - effect_correlation always NA (needs ≥ 3 alleles). - direction_agreement 0 1 . - Q_stat, Q_p, I2 always NA (Cochran Q undefined df = 0). - replicated uses meta_p ≤ 0.05 instead Q_p > 0.05. GWAS-specific output columns added $concordance: lead_snp_pop1, lead_snp_pop2, lead_p_pop1, lead_p_pop2, se_derived_pop1, se_derived_pop2, both_pleiotropic. Flexible column naming. beta_col, se_col, p_col arguments accept column names (e.g. "effect", "std_err", "pvalue"). Marker column accepted alias SNP. Output class. Returns LDxBlocks_effect_concordance — class compare_block_effects(). existing print() method works immediately. 18 new tests test-association.R (104 → 122 total).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development)
text: Cross-population haplotype effect concordance. Takes two test_block_haplotypes() result objects returns per-block statistics systematic GWAS replication: IVW meta-analysis: inverse-variance weighted combined effect SE per block, identical framework two-sample Mendelian randomisation. Cochran Q heterogeneity: tests whether effect sizes differ significantly populations. Significant Q (low Q_p) flags G×E interaction population-specific LD structure differences. ² inconsistency: 0–100% measure -population heterogeneity. Values > 50% indicate two populations telling different biological story block. Direction agreement: fraction shared alleles effect sign. Controlled direction_threshold (default 0.75). replicated flag: composite criterion — enough_shared   directionally_concordant Q_p > 0.05. Block boundary diagnostics: blocks_pop1 blocks_pop2 supplied, boundary_overlap_ratio automatically computed (user-set) bp(intersection) / bp(union) every block. quantifies similarly two populations carved region LD blocks. boundary_overlap_warn (input parameter, default 0.80) threshold boundary_warning = TRUE set output. $shared_alleles: per-allele detail table ivw_effect, ivw_SE, direction_agree, raw effects/SEs populations. 15 new tests test-association.R (89 → 104 total).

what: Enhancement: simpleM multiple-testing correction in test_block_haplotypes()
title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development)
text: test_block_haplotypes() now implements simpleM procedure (Gao et al. 2008, 2010, 2011) LD-aware multiple-testing correction correlated haplotype allele tests. simpleM estimates effective number independent tests (Meff) eigenspectrum haplotype allele dosage correlation matrix, replacing raw test counting LD-aware effective count less conservative Bonferroni providing family-wise error control. New parameters: sig_metric: p-value drives significant / significant_omnibus. One "p_wald", "p_fdr", "p_simplem" (Bonferroni-style), "p_simplem_sidak" (Šidák-style, recommended). Default "p_wald". meff_scope: scope Meff estimation — "chromosome" (recommended), "global", "block". Default "chromosome". meff_percent_cut: variance threshold simpleM eigendecomposition. Default 0.995 (99.5%), following original simpleM recommendation. meff_max_cols: chunk size large eigendecompositions. Default 1000L. New output columns — always present regardless sig_metric: allele_tests: Meff, alpha_simplem, alpha_simplem_sidak, p_simplem, p_simplem_sidak, p_fdr block_tests: Meff, alpha_simplem, alpha_simplem_sidak, p_omnibus_fdr, p_omnibus_simplem, p_omnibus_simplem_sidak (plus p_omnibus_adj retained backward compatibility) New return list elements: meff_scope, meff_percent_cut, meff (nested list Meff summaries per trait: $allele$global, $allele$chromosome, $allele$block, $block$global, $block$chromosome). 13 new tests test-association.R (76 → 89).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development)
text: phased VCF . empty string ID column (column 3), read_phased_vcf() now synthesises CHR_POS identifiers rows, matching behaviour read_geno(). Without fix, -dot VCFs produced duplicate rownames(hap1) caused extract_haplotypes() fail slicing SNP name. Handles NA, ".", "" (empty string) row--row. Real rsIDs mixed VCFs preserved; missing IDs replaced. Verbose message reports count synthesised IDs. 3 new regression tests test-phasing.R (30 → 33). Note: gap affects direct calls read_phased_vcf() externally phased VCFs dot IDs. run_ldx_pipeline() path already safe read_geno() synthesises CHR_POS IDs writing cleaned VCF Beagle.

what: Breaking change: SNP ID separator changed from : to _
title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development patch)
text: VCF GDS file rsID (.e. ID field . empty), read_geno() previously generated fallback SNP identifiers CHR:POS format (e.g. "1:4106"). now generated CHR_POS format (e.g. "1_4106"). Reason: colon : reserved character many genomic file formats (BED, VCF INFO field, R data frames used rownames) causes silent parsing errors downstream. underscore _ safe contexts. Impact: cached bigmemory backing files (ldxblocks_bm*.rds, ldxblocks_bm*.bin, ldxblocks_bm*.desc) built old format must deleted rerunning. pipeline rebuild automatically. Files delete rerunning:
code: results_dir <- "/your/results/dir" for (f in list.files(results_dir, pattern = "^ldxblocks_bm", full.names = TRUE))   file.remove(f)

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development patch)
text: extract_chr_haplotypes_cpp() — retained_idx field added (critical) C++ extractor now returns retained_idx integer vector (1-based) giving row index input block_sb/block_eb arrays retained block. Previously compact hap_strings n_snps arrays (indexed retained blocks ) paired R chr_blk_srt[b, ] (indexed blocks), causing systematic mismatch: singleton coordinates paired multi-SNP haplotype strings inflated n_snps values. backend streaming path matrix path extract_haplotypes() now use ret_idx <- cpp_res$retained_idx fetch block metadata, eliminating mismatch entirely. Sanity checks stop immediately informative message retained_idx missing malformed (indicating old compiled binary still use). extraction paths also pre-filter chr_blk_srt findInterval calling C++, providing first-pass guard independent compilation. Pipeline QC singleton check — run_ldx_pipeline() now asserts block_info row start_bp == end_bp n_snps > 1 immediately extract_haplotypes(), stopping clear message stale binary still loaded. .validate_hap_output() added — called end pipeline; reports NA count feature matrix, duplicated hap_id values, n_snps range, hap_id / column alignment.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1.9000 (development patch)
text: alleles column writer — write_haplotype_numeric() now decodes nucleotide sequences directly hap_info$hap_string + snp_info per row, without calling decode_haplotype_strings(). Single source truth: dosage string stored hap_info always one used build matrix column. Backend extraction list accumulation — backend streaming path extract_haplotypes() now accumulates block_info rows pre-allocated list (bi_rows) binds end data.table::rbindlist(), matching matrix path eliminating O(n²) rbind growth. .prefilter_blocks_by_span() helper — shared findInterval-based pre-filter extracted single internal function used extraction paths; eliminates duplicated logic future drift risk. extract_chr_haplotypes_phased_cpp() — new C++ phased extractor (Item 5) dedicated phased haplotype extractor now compiled ld_core.cpp. accepts hap1_chr hap2_chr (0/1 gamete matrices) builds "gamete1|gamete2" strings one OpenMP pass, counting gamete frequencies correctly (individual contributes two observations). Returns contract extract_chr_haplotypes_cpp(): retained_idx, retained_start_bp, retained_end_bp, hap_freq, freq_dominant, etc. matrix path extract_haplotypes() now dispatches function isp = TRUE, eliminating previous R loop called build_hap_strings_cpp() twice per block concatenated results paste0(). R (b) loop now identical phased unphased: read cpp_res$hap_strings[[b]] respective C++ extractor. Imputed backend parameter fingerprinting (Item 6) — .make_imputed_backend() now accepts maf_cut, min_callrate, impute_method parameters saves ldxblocks_bm_imputed_params.rds alongside four backing files. reattach saved fingerprint compared current parameters; differ (fingerprint file absent, indicating old-format cache), five files cleaned backend rebuilt. Without , run maf_cut = 0.10 silently reuse imputed backend built maf_cut = 0.05. run_ldx_pipeline() passes maf_cut, min_callrate, impute argument .make_imputed_backend(). C++ bp coordinate truth — retained_start_bp retained_end_bp (Item 8) extract_chr_haplotypes_cpp() extract_chr_haplotypes_phased_cpp() now return retained_start_bp retained_end_bp integer vectors: retained block, values direct copies block_sb[b] block_eb[b] C++ arrays — R-side index lookup chr_blk_srt. R extraction loops check !.null(cpp_res$retained_start_bp) read coordinates directly C++ available. R-side fallback (chr_blk_srt[ret_idx[b], ]) remains binary-compatibility old compiled objects. removes last avenue coordinate mismatch C++ compact array index R full-block-table row index.

what: Performance: C++ overlap resolution and WGS stall fixes
title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: Big_LD() post-segment pipeline now routes C++ overlap resolution, eliminating several O(n²)–O(n·p) bottlenecks caused chromosome processing stall segment detection completed. resolve_overlap_cpp() — new exported C++ function Replaces two sequential R .resolve_overlap() calls single C++ pass implementing identical cumulative-score split rule. Four improvements R version: BLAS DGEMM scoring — overlapping block pair, disputed SNP scores computed rowMeans(C_L²) − rowMeans(C_R²) C_L = (Z_overlap.t() × Z_left_reps) / (n−1) via Armadillo matrix multiply. replaces vapply() loop calling col_r2_cpp() per SNP p columns (314k chr1). Cost per disputed SNP drops O(n × p) O(n × 2k_rep) — 15,700× reduction chr1. Lazy column cache — column adj_mat standardised . Columns never accessed representatives overlap SNPs never touched. ~1,000 overlapping pairs using 20 reps , fewer 1% chr1’s 314k columns ever standardised. Single pass — one call replaces two. second R call needed first pass create new adjacent overlaps; C++ loop handles naturally within pass. OpenMP pairs — overlapping pairs collected first O(n_blocks) scan, resolved #pragma omp parallel (scores computed parallel; boundaries applied serially reverse preserve index stability). R-level running counters Four additional O(n) scan operations replaced O(1) running counters: sum(!.na(LDblocks[,1L])) called 419 times per chromosome → ld_count max((!.na(newLDblocks[,1L]))) re-merge loop → remerge_count min((.na(newLDblocks[,1L]))) re-merge loop → remerge_count done <- LDblocks[!.na(LDblocks[,1L]),] → LDblocks[seq_len(ld_count),] Re-merge skip modeNum=2 optional re-merge loop across forced cut-points now skipped cut-points forced (length(atfcut) >= length(cutpoints) - 2), occurs cutsequence.modi switches modeNum=2. mode every cut forced definition, re-running CLQD across boundaries produces benefit. 419 forced segments chr1 eliminated potentially hundreds CLQD calls serial R loop. intersect() → findInterval() re-merge check per-pair forced-cut check length(intersect(eb[2L]:nb[1L], atfcut)) > 0L — O(n_blocks × n_atfcut) total — replaced findInterval(eb[2L], atfcut) binary search: O(log n_atfcut) per call. score_overlap_cpp() resolve_seam_cpp() (internal static helpers) Two additional C++ helpers src/ld_core.cpp implement BLAS scoring kernel seam-local resolver future use truly massive scale (pangenome panels global O(n_blocks) scan becomes bottleneck). static functions exported R.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: test-resolve-overlap.R (307 lines, new file): parallel property tests .resolve_overlap() (R reference) resolve_overlap_cpp() (C++), plus cross-validation tests verifying identical boundaries six biological cases: non-overlapping passthrough, union merge (B inside ), -left path, -right path, tie-breaking (zero-variance overlap), mixed cumulative-score split. Output invariant tests: new blocks created, output non-overlapping, global span preserved. test-cpp.R: five additional tests resolve_overlap_cpp() covering dimensionality, non-overlapping passthrough, remaining overlaps, start ≤ end invariant, R vs C++ agreement random overlapping input.

what: Haplotype association testing and breeding decision functions
title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: Four new exported functions complete statistical inference breeding decision layer. Haplotype association testing test_block_haplotypes(haplotypes, blues, blocks, n_pcs, top_n, min_freq,   id_col, blue_col, blue_cols, alpha, verbose) — Block-level haplotype association tests via unified Q+K mixed linear model (EMMAX/GAPIT3 formulation): y = mu + alpha·x_hap + sum(beta_k·PC_k) + g + e, PC_k GRM-derived eigenvectors (fixed effects population structure) g ~ MVN(0, sigma_g^2 G) polygenic kinship random effect. GRM inverted per trait via rrBLUP::mixed.solve() (O(n^3)); per-allele scan across blocks fully vectorised single crossprod() call (O(n*p), BLAS trick marginal SNP screening). Returns LDxBlocks_haplotype_assoc object $allele_tests (per-allele Wald tests: effect, SE, t, p_wald, p_wald_adj, significant) $block_tests (omnibus F-test per block: F_stat, p_omnibus, p_omnibus_adj, var_explained, significant_omnibus). n_pcs = 0L (default): EMMAX pure GRM; n_pcs > 0: Q+K model; n_pcs = NULL: auto-select via scree elbow. estimate_diplotype_effects(haplotypes, blues, blocks, min_freq,   min_n_diplotype, id_col, blue_col, blue_cols, verbose) — Estimates additive dominance effects diplotype class means LD block, GRM kinship correction. allele pair (, B): additive effect = (mean_BB - mean_AA) / 2; dominance deviation d = mean_AB - midpoint; dominance ratio d/(0 = additive, +/-1 = complete dominance, |d/| > 1 = overdominance). Returns LDxBlocks_diplotype object $diplotype_means, $dominance_table (, d, d_over_a, overdominance per allele pair per block per trait), $omnibus_tests (F-test per block). Breeding decision tools score_favorable_haplotypes(haplotypes, allele_effects, min_freq,   missing_string, normalize) — Scores individual’s genome-wide haplotype portfolio table known per-allele effects. Block score = sum(allele_effect × dosage) per block. Genome-wide stacking index = sum across scored blocks, normalised [0,1] normalize = TRUE. Returns ranked data frame (one row per individual) stacking_index, n_blocks_scored, mean_block_score, rank, one score_<block_id> column per scored block detailed inspection. summarize_parent_haplotypes(haplotypes, candidate_ids, allele_effects,   blocks, min_freq, missing_string) — Produces tidy long-format allele inventory (one row per individual × block × allele) candidate parents. Reports allele dosage (0/1 unphased; 0/1/2 phased), population allele frequency, optional allele effect, is_rare flag (freq < 0.10). Includes rows dosage = 0 candidates can compared rows. Primary tool identifying complementary rare alleles across candidates designing haplotype stacking crosses.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: ldx_blues_list — named list two environments (env1, env2) named numeric BLUEs (120 individuals ), generated polygenic architecture ldx_blues environment-specific offsets. Used examples run_haplotype_stability() cv_haplotype_prediction(). Flat-file copy inst/extdata/example_blues_env.csv.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: Ten new exported functions extend pipeline without modifying existing function. two new files: R/analysis_extensions.R R/haplotype_inference.R. Cross-validation model evaluation cv_haplotype_prediction() — k-fold cross-validation haplotype GBLUP model. Masks phenotypes fold--fold, predicts via rrBLUP::kin.blup() using shared haplotype GRM, returns predictive ability (Pearson r) RMSE per trait per fold. Supports multiple replications multiple traits. Returns LDxBlocks_cv object pa_summary, pa_mean, k, n_rep. Population comparison compare_haplotype_populations() — computes Weir-Cockerham (1984) FST allele frequency differences per block two named sample groups. Returns FST, max_freq_diff, dominant allele per group, chi-squared p-value (Monte Carlo, B=2000), divergent flag (FST > 0.1 p < 0.05). Suitable breeding cycle monitoring wild/elite panel comparisons. Visualisation plot_haplotype_network() — draws minimum-spanning network haplotype alleles within one LD block using igraph::mst() Hamming-distance edge weights. Node size proportional frequency. Optional group colouring via groups named vector. Returns igraph MST object invisibly. Multi-environment stability run_haplotype_stability() — Finlay-Wilkinson (1963) regression per-block local GEBV contributions environmental index across environments. Returns slope b (stability coefficient), SE, R², deviation mean square s²d, stable flag (H0: b=1 rejected alpha=0.05). Requires least 2 environments. Annotation export export_candidate_regions() — converts define_qtl_regions() output BED (0-based, UCSC/BEDtools-compatible), CSV, named list ready biomaRt::getBM(). Supports chr_prefix, LD-extended windows via use_lead_snp, padding_bp. Effect decomposition decompose_block_effects() — aggregates per-SNP additive effects (backsolve_snp_effects()) per-haplotype-allele effect table. Effect = sum(SNP_effect × allele_dosage) per allele position. Returns allele_effect, effect_rank, frequency per allele per block. Directly links prediction model output selection index construction. Genome-wide diversity scanning scan_diversity_windows() — sliding-window / Shannon / n_eff_alleles scan across genome independent LD block boundaries. Window size step controlled window_bp step_bp. Returns data frame one row per window including sweep_flag (freq_dominant >= 0.90). True haplotype inference harmonisation infer_block_haplotypes() — converts raw haplotype strings structured per-individual, per-block diplotype table explicit hap1, hap2, diplotype (canonical sorted string), heterozygous, phase_ambiguous, missing columns. Handles phased input (read_phased_vcf(), phase_ambiguous always FALSE) unphased input (heterozygous genotypes set phase_ambiguous = TRUE unless resolve_unphased = TRUE triggers maximum-parsimony heuristic). collapse_haplotypes() — merges rare haplotype alleles (min_freq) biologically meaningful groups rather dropping . Three strategies: "rare_to_other" (pool <>), "nearest" (merge similar common allele Hamming distance), "tree_based" (UPGMA dendrogram; merges rare alleles coarsest cut avoids merging common alleles ). Preserves label_map attribute recording every original→collapsed mapping use harmonize_haplotypes(). harmonize_haplotypes() — makes haplotype allele labels transferable across training/validation splits, populations, environments. Builds reference dictionary alleles min_freq_ref reference panel; matches target alleles exact string first, nearest Hamming neighbour (max_hamming), labels unmatched alleles "<novel>". Attaches harmonization_report attribute reporting n_exact, n_nearest, n_novel, mean_hamming_dist per block.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: compute_ld_decay() – LD decay analysis per chromosome. Estimates distance r² (rV²) drops critical threshold using memory-efficient position-first random sampling strategy: pair indices sampled SNP positions , read_chunk() loads unique SNP columns involved (~2% WGS chromosome 50k random pairs). compute_r2_sparse_cpp() (Armadillo BLAS + OpenMP) called per chromosome place R pair--pair loop. Two threshold approaches: fixed numeric (e.g. 0.1, standard GWAS practice) parametric (95th percentile r² unlinked markers different chromosomes, measuring background kinship-induced LD level). Optional LOESS Hill-Weir (1988) nonlinear decay model fitting. Chromosome-specific decay distances can passed directly define_qtl_regions(ld_decay = decay) define biologically justified candidate gene windows. Censored distances (threshold never crossed within max_dist) flagged censored = TRUE output emitted warnings. Requires change existing code. plot_ld_decay() – ggplot2 visualisation r² vs physical distance per chromosome, optional raw points, threshold line, per-chromosome decay distance markers, facet option.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: LD-informed overlap resolution replacing blind union merge Big_LD(). Overlapping blocks sub-segment seams now resolved cumulative-score boundary rule: disputed SNP, score = mean_r2(left core) -   mean_r2(right core). cumulative sum tracked across overlap zone split boundary placed last position cumulative score >= 0. Representatives selected boundary-adjacent SNPs core (nearest overlap zone, first-k). Three clean cases: -left (block keeps overlap, block B shrinks), -right (block shrinks, block B keeps overlap), mixed (split cumulative boundary). Falls back union merge one core empty (one block fully inside ).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: compute_r2_sparse_cpp() gains n_threads parameter (OpenMP). Thread-local vector accumulation prevents contention; results merged parallel loop. R wrapper RcppExports.R updated expose new argument default n_threads = 1L.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: compute_ld_decay() pair sampling – snp_info now sorted POS within chromosome pair index sampling. Previously, unsorted input produce incorrect pair distances silently. compute_ld_decay() column precomputation – prepared columns (mean-imputed, optionally whitened) now computed per chunk, per pair. Monomorphic columns (sd < 1e-6 preparation) skipped r² computation. compute_ld_decay() mode – sliding-window random pairs now kept separate: sliding window feeds decay curve, random pairs feed parametric threshold . Previously merged one pool, biasing curve shape estimate. compute_ld_decay() fitting failures – LOESS Hill-Weir nonlinear failures now emit warning() chromosome name error message instead silently swallowed tryCatch(..., error = function(e) NULL). compute_ld_decay() threshold crossing – linear interpolation last-first-bins sub-bin precision (: first-bin midpoint). Non-monotone curves fall back first-. r2_threshold validation – numeric thresholds outside [0,1], NA values, unrecognised strings now produce informative errors instead silent wrong results.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: extract_haplotypes() C++ optimisation — haplotype strings now built via build_hap_strings_cpp(), replacing R vapply() loop incurred one function call per individual per block. 3M-SNP panel 17,078 blocks 204 individuals: ~3.5 million R calls -> one C++ call per block. Expected speedup: 20-50x (3.5 hours -> ~5-10 minutes panel). read_chunk() bigmemory NA fix — char-type big.matrix stores NA -128; now correctly restored NA_integer_. Also removed redundant integer->double type conversion.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
text: Louvain/Leiden community detection (CLQmode = "Louvain" "Leiden"): Polynomial-time O(n log n) community detection replaces Bron-Kerbosch (igraph::max_cliques()) exponential worst-case complexity. original Big-LD run 3M-SNP WGS panel found 4.26 million maximal cliques single 1500-SNP window ran > 1 hour without completing. Louvain/Leiden finish window < 1 second. Block boundaries equivalent better Density mode WGS panels LD structure dense. Available CLQD(), Big_LD(), run_Big_LD_all_chr(), tune_LD_params(), run_ldx_pipeline(). Edge weights inversely proportional base-pair distance local LD structure respected. Sparse LD computation (max_bp_distance parameter): max_bp_distance > 0, SNP pairs within physical distance r² computed via compute_r2_sparse_cpp(). Pairs beyond threshold assumed negligible LD set zero adjacency matrix. reduces O(p²) LD computation near-O(p) WGS panels — 500 kb, approximately 70–90% pairs skipped. Available CLQD(), propagated callers. Default 0L (disabled) preserves original behaviour. Memory-mapped genotype store (read_geno_bigmemory()): Wraps genotype source bigmemory::big.matrix backed binary file disk. read_chunk() retrieves columns via OS page faults — requested bytes loaded RAM. Peak RAM proportional n_samples × subSegmSize × 8 bytes, full genome matrix. Backing files persist across R sessions: supply .desc path reattach without re-loading source data. Storage type "char" (1 byte per cell) saves 8× RAM vs double 0/1/2 dosage values. Requires bigmemory (added Suggests).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.1 (development)
code: blocks <- run_Big_LD_all_chr(   be,   CLQmode         = "Leiden",    # polynomial — guaranteed connected communities   CLQcut          = 0.70,        # sparser LD graph   max_bp_distance = 500000L,     # skip pairs > 500 kb   subSegmSize     = 500L,        # smaller windows for safety   leng            = 50L,         # narrow boundary scan for WGS density   checkLargest    = TRUE,        # belt-and-suspenders for Density mode   n_threads       = n_threads )

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: read_geno(): removed pheno_ids parameter. Sample subsetting caller’s responsibility – use $sample_ids match externally. Removed read_pheno() align_geno_pheno() entirely. Phenotype handling part LD block detection functions algorithmic role. run_Big_LD_all_chr(): parameter rV2method renamed kin_method. digits default changed 6 -1 (rounding) throughout. SeqArray backend replaced SNPRelate throughout. GDS files now created via SNPRelate::snpgdsVCF2GDS() read via SNPRelate::snpgdsGetGeno(). Existing .gds files created SeqArray compatible; delete re-convert read_geno(..., verbose = TRUE).

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: read_phased_vcf(): read pre-phased VCF 0|1 GT fields; returns hap1/hap2 gamete matrices plus combined dosage. phase_with_beagle(): call Beagle 5.x statistical phasing WGS data; returns path phased VCF.gz. unphase_to_dosage(): collapse phased gamete matrices back 0/1/2 (internal helper; exported). Auto-detects phased vs unphased input list structure. Unphased mode: diploid string "012201" per individual per block. Phased mode: two-gamete string "011|100" per individual per block. Blocks strictly per-chromosome; cross-chromosome blocks architecturally impossible. n_eff_alleles: effective number alleles = 1/Σpᵢ² (Hill 1973), ranging 1 (monomorphic) k (equal frequencies). sweep_flag: TRUE freq_dominant ≥ 0.90, flagging possible selective sweeps strong founder effects (Difabachew et al. 2023). now sample-size corrected following Nei (1973): = n/(n-1) × (1 − Σpᵢ²). Output now includes CHR, start_bp, end_bp, n_snps columns direct use genomic region analyses. Converts raw dosage strings (e.g. "02110") nucleotide sequences (e.g. "AGTTA") using REF/ALT snp_info. Returns data frame: block_id, CHR, start_bp, end_bp, hap_rank, hap_id, dosage_string, nucleotide_sequence, frequency, n_carriers, snp_positions, snp_alleles. New encoding = parameter: "additive_012" (default) "presence_01". Phased + "additive_012": true 0/1/2 allele counts per gamete (0=absent, 1=one gamete, 2=gametes). Unphased + "additive_012": 0/1/NA — 1=present, 0=absent. value 2 used homozygosity inferred unphased strings. "presence_01": 0/1 presence/absence kernel methods random forests (formerly "presence_02"; "presence_02" accepted backward-compat alias). New min_freq = parameter drop rare haplotype allele columns. top_n default changed 5L NULL (retain alleles min_freq). Maps significant GWAS markers onto LD blocks post-GWAS. Flags pleiotropic blocks (significant hits multiple traits). Implements haploblock-based QTL cataloguing approach Tong et al. (2024) Theoretical Applied Genetics 137:274. Now accepts optional BETA column gwas_results. supplied, output includes: lead_beta (effect lead SNP), sig_snps (significant SNP IDs, semicolon-separated), sig_betas (marginal effects). See ?define_qtl_regions block effect estimation approaches. write_haplotype_numeric(): tab-delimited dosage matrix (0/1/2/NA). Metadata column alleles shows nucleotide sequence allele. Compatible rrBLUP, BGLR, ASReml-R. write_haplotype_character(): tab-delimited nucleotide matrix. cell shows nucleotide sequence individual carries allele, "-" absent, "." missing. Heterozygous SNP positions encoded IUPAC ambiguity codes (R=/G, Y=C/T, S=G/C, W=/T, K=G/T, M=/C), keeping sequence length n_snps. Alleles column shows REF/ALT every block SNP (semicolon-separated). write_haplotype_diversity(): CSV per-block diversity metrics optional genome-wide mean summary row. write_haplotype_hapmap() removed. diploid AA//TT HapMap encoding meaningful multi-SNP haplotype alleles. Haplotype string decoder (decode_haplotype_strings()): exported function mapping dosage strings nucleotide sequences full SNP-level metadata. Extends run_haplotype_prediction() k traits simultaneously. One trait-agnostic GRM (computed ) shared across traits. GBLUP solver: attempts sommer::mmer() multi-trait model first; automatically falls back rrBLUP::kin.blup() per-trait loop sommer installed model fails converge. Block importance aggregated across traits: var_scaled_mean, n_traits_important, important_any, important_all per block. importance_rule argument controls combined flag: '' (≥ 1 trait), '' (traits), 'mean' (mean ≥ 0.9). blues accepts wide data frame (one column per trait) named list named numeric vectors (different individuals per trait). sommer added Suggests; rrBLUP required fallback. run_haplotype_prediction_mt() removed; functionality fully absorbed unified run_haplotype_prediction() API. Cites Covarrubias-Pazaran (2016) sommer Endelman (2011) rrBLUP. compute_haplotype_grm(): VanRaden (2008) GRM haplotype feature matrix. Mean-imputes NA, clamps frequencies, returns symmetric n×n matrix. backsolve_snp_effects(): derives per-SNP additive effects GEBV without refitting marker model: α̂ = M′G⁻¹ĝ / 2Σp(1−p) (Tong et al. 2025 Theor Appl Genet 138:267). compute_local_gebv(): local haplotype GEBV per block = sum SNP effects within block. Ranks blocks Var(local GEBV); important flag scaled variance ≥ 0.90 (Tong et al. 2024 Theor Appl Genet 137:274). prepare_gblup_inputs(): aligns haplotype feature matrix phenotype data frame; computes returns bended GRM ready rrBLUP, sommer, ASReml-R, BGLR. run_haplotype_prediction(): end--end Tong et al. (2024/2025) pipeline pre-adjusted phenotype values (BLUEs/BLUPs) block importance. Accepts blues named numeric vector data frame id_col blue_col arguments. Uses rrBLUP::kin.blup() REML-based GBLUP. Returns standard pipeline outputs plus GEBV, per-SNP effects, local haplotype GEBV matrix, block importance table. integrate_gwas_haplotypes(): combines GWAS evidence (has_gwas_hit), variance evidence (is_important, scaled Var(local GEBV) ≥ 0.9), diversity evidence (is_diverse, ≥ threshold) priority_score (0–3) plain-language recommendation per block. diversity-— rank ; (2) GWAS-— binary GWAS-hit flag tiebreaker, p-value used ranking; (3) phenotype — rank scaled Var(local GEBV). Returns standard pipeline outputs plus ranked_blocks data frame. hap_format = "hapmap" renamed hap_format = "character". Big_LD arguments now exposed: clstgap, split, appendrare, singleton_as_block, checkLargest, digits, kin_method, CLQmode. Previously 8 silently using Big_LD defaults. min_freq parameter exposed (previously hardcoded inside build_haplotype_feature_matrix). Return list now includes geno_matrix (individuals × SNPs, MAF-filtered), enabling direct use tune_LD_params() run_haplotype_prediction() without reloading genotype file. tune_LD_params() default grid now jointly optimises CLQcut (4 values) min_freq (2 values), giving 8 combinations. Weber et al. (2023) show hyperparameters requiring dataset-specific tuning.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: Multi-format /O: read_geno() supports numeric dosage CSV, HapMap, VCF / bgzipped VCF, SNPRelate GDS, PLINK BED/BIM/FAM, plain R matrices. SNPRelate GDS streaming backend: peak RAM proportional one window. Replaces previous SeqArray backend entirely. PLINK BED backend: BEDMatrix-backed memory-mapped access. read_chunk(), close_backend(): unified accessor interface. run_Big_LD_all_chr() accepts LDxBlocks_backend directly. min_snps_chr parameter: skip scaffolds fewer SNPs (default 10). clean_malformed parameter read_geno(), run_Big_LD_all_chr(), run_ldx_pipeline(): streams input files remove malformed lines GDS conversion (NGSEP GATK-origin VCFs). chr parameter run_Big_LD_all_chr() run_ldx_pipeline(): restrict processing specific chromosomes. ldx_gwas example dataset now includes trait column ("TraitA" / "TraitB") demonstrate multi-trait pleiotropic block detection via define_qtl_regions(trait_col = "trait").

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: boundary_scan_cpp(): C++ boundary scan replaces R inner loop (~20x faster). maf_filter_cpp(): combined MAF + monomorphic filter, single O(np) pass (~10x faster panels > 100 k SNPs). build_adj_matrix_cpp(): -place adjacency construction, eliminates intermediate allocation. compute_r2_sparse_cpp(): sparse r² within bp window, avoids O(p²) cost large sub-segments.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: Chunked pre-allocated numeric CSV reader: two-pass strategy – header scan Pass 1, fixed 50,000-row chunks fill single pre-allocated matrix Pass 2. Peak RAM = one chunk, 2x file. VCF HapMap mandatory SNPRelate GDS auto-conversion: transparent streaming GDS backend first call; cache reused subsequent calls. Explicit per-chromosome gc() run_Big_LD_all_chr() extract_haplotypes(): prevents heap fragmentation across 20-30 chromosome passes.

title: LDxBlocks 0.3.0
text: test-ld-decay.R: 33 tests compute_ld_decay() plot_ld_decay() covering: input validation, LDxBlocks_decay object structure, sampling modes, unsorted snp_info handling, threshold types (fixed, parametric, , NULL), fit_model options, decay distance correctness, censored flag, three backend types (matrix, LDxBlocks_backend, bigmemory), print plot methods, define_qtl_regions() integration ld_decay=. test-basic.R: smoke tests covering major functions via 230-SNP example dataset (3 chromosomes, 9 LD blocks). test-algorithm.R: property-based tests Big-LD segmentation algorithm: CLQD, Big_LD, run_Big_LD_all_chr, summarise_blocks, plot_ld_blocks. test-haplotypes.R: comprehensive tests haplotype extraction (phased unphased), diversity metrics (n_eff_alleles, sweep_flag), QTL region definition (lead_beta, sig_snps, sig_betas), feature matrix encoding (additive_012 / presence_01), output writers, rank_haplotype_blocks, integrate_gwas_haplotypes.
